Mission role: Fertility specialist

Pay rate: Bagatur [hero, knight; expired Mongolian Free States trademark for a specialized/valuable worker]

Biography: Mother of Wander and Ander. Adopted Mongolian naming conventions for children. Achieved zero infant mortality on Mongolian one-way colony world New Tārā. Compassionate fatalist: accepts inevitability of suffering but still feels it. Critical social anchor in Habitat cohort.

Notes: Despite large emotional attack surface, unlikely to depart from useful work regime.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.BasePiece_HydroelectricTurbine_descripti:
Named for a legendary laundress, the "Nausicaa IV" hydroelectric turbine transforms moving water into power. Ocean currents turn blades, which spin the rotor of a polyphase generator to create electricity.

—Build inside a water current
—Connect to a nearby habitat or facility with power transmitter(s)

*"The hydroelectric turbine: a clever turn of phase."*
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.Sessile_Necrolei_Cyst_title: Needs a name - inform Brian of new name.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.Tool_PDA_description:
OPERATING INSTRUCTIONS
Attend to PDA voice, or invoke full-screen interface to read (as you are doing now). Check all PDA tabs to ensure you understand their function.

BACKGROUND
The Personal Digital Assistant (PDA) is a cybernetic augment to the customer's consciousness. It tracks and sorts the user's vital signs, possessions, information and fabricator blueprints. 

The PDA holds the sum total of human knowledge in a compressed quartz archive. It synthesizes this knowledge with scans you gather to generate databank entries on unknown species and phenomena. These databank entries can be the difference between life and death — for you or for your entire colony. 

Remember the acronyms:
—ABC: Always Be Checking
—BLR: Be Like Ryley

If you're lost or confused, review your PDA. Remember that Ryley Robinson's PDA and scanner brought him home safely in the face of unforeseen and catastrophic hazards. If a non-essential systems maintenance chief can trust his PDA that completely, so can you.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.BasePiece_ScannerStation:
OPERATING INSTRUCTIONS
Construct in habitat room compartment. Ensure sufficient electrical budget is available—high power draw. Once powered, select a filter and wait for the map to populate with contacts. Touch a contact (entering the holographic map if necessary) to place a signal on its position in the real world. CAUTION: the scanner's power draw increases sharply while active.

BACKGROUND
Ever wondered what's out there? Now you can replace wonder and mystery with crisp, high-resolution sensor imagery. The scanner station manages sensors including (but not limited to) hyperspectral imaging, field MRI, passive sonar, biosonic spectroscopy, lidar vibrometry, chemical sniffers and magnetometric inferentials.

The scanner station is a standalone product; colonists are not required to manually construct sensors on their habitat. Building these emitters personally may give colonists a sense of purpose and achievement.

*"The Alterra scanner station. Because the map always beats the territory."*
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.BasePiece_Processor_description:
OPERATING INSTRUCTIONS
Construct in habitat room compartment. Select a desired process (such as titanium ingots from titanium), then load the input hopper and wait for the processor run to complete. Collect output from output tray. CAUTION: the processor's power draw increases sharply during a run.

BACKGROUND
The processor is a reverse fabricator, reducing complex inputs like salvage or biomass into simple raw materials. While standard fabricators can break down salvage, the processor is required to generate large pure outputs. The processor brings an arsenal of flash smelters, centrifuges, chemical washes, fractionators and magma electrolytic oxide cells to bear.

Refugee users may fondly recall political propaganda comparing Alterra to the sinister, controlling fabricator and the Mongolian Independent States to the robust, honest processor.

*"The Alterra bulk processor. Creative destruction."*
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.Tool_DeployableLocker_description:
OPERATING INSTRUCTIONS
Construct with builder tool. Pick up by handles to carry. Automatically feeds resources to nearby fabricators and habitat builders. 

Can be mounted on hardpoints in vehicles and bases, adding extra storage space.

BACKGROUND
The best-in-class watertight storage enclosure for tools, resources, nutrition and miscellaneous belongings. Maintains neutral buoyancy, no matter how heavily loaded — 99% guaranteed. Communicates with your PDA to locate materials for fabricator recipes. So you can get back to doing what you do best: filling up more lockers.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.Tadpole_HAUL_description:
OPERATING INSTRUCTIONS
Dock Tadpole with HAUL rig. Store cargo in pods. Mount utility items to extra hardpoint. Passengers may ride on catamaran pods. Undock Tadpole to swap chassis. 

BACKGROUND
The HAUL (Heavy Utility Lifter) rig provides the Tadpole command module with catamaran cargo pods and additional seating for passengers, bringing its total capacity to 4 pioneers. The chassis also boasts an extra hardpoint for utility items like a deployable locker, work light or oxygen generator.

Recommended for resource extraction and cargo ferry missions. Not recommended for exploration into dangerous territory.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.Tadpole_ScoutRay_description:
OPERATING INSTRUCTIONS
Dock Tadpole with Scout Ray hydrofoil. Undock Tadpole to swap chassis. 

BACKGROUND
The Scout Ray hydrofoil provides the Tadpole command module with enhanced speed and agility. Recommended for exploration and high-threat excursions into dangerous waters.

The Scout Ray adds no additional hardpoints or cargo space.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.Tool_BasicFirstAidKit_description:
OPERATING INSTRUCTIONS
Equip to quick-access toolbelt or store in inventory. Use to regain health.

BACKGROUND
A satchel of emergency medical supplies derived from TSF Frontiersmen front-line trauma kits. The medkit is designed to self-apply: you never know when you'll end up the last one standing.

Because blackbox implants and biobeds can provide long-term care, the medkit is intended to keep you moving and working long to enough to escape a dangerous situation or complete a difficult task. The kit supplies seals, painkillers, hemostatic dressings, temporary tourniquets, conformal splints, fluid transfusions, nerve bridges, needle pumps, spray flesh, stimulants, and other rapid effectors.

The medkit may aggravate long-term injury in order to achieve short-term performance. Health is equity, and modern medicine gives you the confidence to leverage that equity in the pursuit of success.

*"The emergency medkit: now your health is a renewable resource."*
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.Tool_Wakemaker_description:
OPERATING INSTRUCTIONS
Equip to quick-access toolbelt. Active only when selected: extend arms into movement position. Replace battery when discharged.

BACKGROUND
A personal DPV (diver propulsion vehicle) for long-distance transit outside a submarine without constant finning. The arm-mounted form factor improves maneuverability in tight spaces and prevents pioneers from losing the DPV during stressful situations.

Not fully hands-free, but allows inventory management, item pickup, and routine touching/scratching. Do not throttle the Wakemaker until your hands are fully extended in the movement position.

Allegations that the Wakemaker is wrist-mounted so that it can steer expired colonists back to biobeds for recycling are ghoulish and false. User experience remains the design team's north star.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.Tool_RepairTool_description:
OPERATING INSTRUCTIONS
Equip to quick-access toolbelt. Aim at damaged object and hold trigger. Replace battery when depleted.

Repairable targets trigger an icon in your HUD, prompting you to equip and use the repair tool. Common targets include Alterra vehicles and damaged Alterra starship or habitat components (such as doors).

BACKGROUND
The repair tool resembles an arc welder in the same way that the scanner resembles a flashlight: similar use cases, but with enormously greater sophistication and effects. The repair tool analyzes damaged systems, breaks up the damaged area with a high-temperature arc, and uses magnetohydrodynamic manipulation to guide the liberated material for fabricated repairs.

Originally invented to assist union-lifting efforts in electrical engineering, the repair tool empowers workers to take on tasks once reserved for an exclusionary elite.

"The Alterra Repair Tool: Get your fix."
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.Tool_SonicResonator_description:
OPERATING INSTRUCTIONS
Equip to quick-access toolbelt. Aim at target—such as a resource deposit or attacking predator—and depress trigger to charge a sonic pulse. Release to emit pulse. Against solid crystalline objects, release the pulse when the reticle closes to shatter.

Valid targets trigger an icon in your HUD, prompting you to equip and use the resonator. Common targets include large resource deposits and viral crystals.

This is not an approved Alterra product. This PDA cannot offer guarantees of performance or safety. Replace battery when depleted.

BACKGROUND
The Sonic Resonator is a modified propulsion/repulsion cannon chassis carrying a phased-array sonar. The sonar's electronic and mechanical principles are simple and easily fabricated, but distinct from any known design pattern. Either the sonar was invented from first principles by a particularly brilliant colonist, or it was derived from nonhuman technology.

The resonator is generally nonlethal. Its pulse may deter attackers, but will not cause long term harm unless the attacker is a solid crystalline object. The exception is biomass infected by bloom virus, which the resonator is tuned to destroy.

Speculative development suggests several possible improvements to this device, perhaps culminating in a saser (an acoustic laser). Do not discharge towards other colonists. Hearing protection recommended for prolonged use.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.Tool_Flashlight_description:
OPERATING INSTRUCTIONS
Equip to quick-access toolbelt. Aim to illuminate. Check battery levels regularly. Recommend carrying at least one spare battery.

BACKGROUND
4500-lumen broad-beam, select-spectrum lamp. Simple to fabricate. Invaluable at night or in caves. Safe to aim at fellow colonists, briefly.

Light is a common biological signal that may provoke reactions from alien organisms. Some of these reactions may be desired or useful. Some may not. Be careful where you aim your beam: meaning is in the photosensitive organ of the beholder. You may be posturing for a fight, calling for help, broadcasting your flavor, or even flirting.

The flashlight is not a substitute for sunlight and should not be used as a therapeutic or diagnostic tool.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.Tool_HabitatBuilder_description:
OPERATING INSTRUCTIONS
Equip to quick-access toolbelt. Follow HUD prompts. For a starter habitat, consult the following quick-start guide:

0. Collect titanium, quartz, and copper
1. Select the corridor compartment from the builder's 'Standard Elements' menu and place it above solid terrain
2. Add a hatch to access the corridor
3. Build a solar panel to power the habitat's oxygen generator
4. Enter your new habitat. Construct a fabricator and a storage locker
5. If blueprints are available, construct a room compartment to support a biobed and other key systems

BACKGROUND
The Alterra handheld habitat builder has been an essential tool of xenoscientists, colonists and emergency relief crews across 15 different trans-govs for more than 75 years.

Instead of prefab compartments, the Cicada's habitat builders feature the new Scalable Scope feature set. Basic rooms and corridors can be expanded in four directions using the 'Grow/Shrink' command, allowing a basic workshed to grow into a multi-story habitat for an entire crew.

—Assembles raw materials and fabricated components into sealed habitats
—Automatically generates oxygen from seawater when powered
—Use the 'Standard Elements' tab to grow/shrink rooms and corridors
—Supports vital mission systems like the processor, biobed, and scanner station (floor space and power required)

Primary systems like oxygen generation and fabrication require power. Power is generated by generators like solar panels, and stored in backup storage units in case of generator failure.

"The Habitat Builder v7.5ScS: turning a colony into a home."
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.Tool_Flare_description:
OPERATING INSTRUCTIONS
Equip to quick-access toolbelt. Hold active end AWAY from hand. Ignite. Hold or throw to illuminate a space, or toss away from self to distract attention.

BACKGROUND
A pyrotechnic flare to mark position, illuminate space, or distract predators.

The flare contains powdered metal and an oxidizer, both synthesized from trace elements (such as strontium, chlorine, oxygen and nitrogen) in normal fabricator feedstock. When triggered, the two substances combine, releasing light, heat, and gas. The flare does not need air to burn, but may ignite flammable atmospheres, such as hydrogen-oxygen pockets or methane.

Pioneers often prefer flares to chemlights due to their brightness and heat, which may intimidate or intrigue alien organisms. Rauli Isotalo, one of the Zezura expedition's celebrity consultants, demanded that pyrotechnic flares be included in the default fabricator offerings.

Assessment: vital defensive tool. Carry always.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.Tool_HandheldScanner_description:
OPERATING INSTRUCTIONS
Equip to quick-access toolbelt. Never remove. Scan everything. Scanning technology fragments will generate new blueprints.

Scannable targets trigger an icon in your HUD, prompting you to equip and use the scanner.
 
BACKGROUND
Alterra's pride, the essential science and survival tool. In cooperation with a PDA, the spectral scanner can image and analyze anything a pioneer encounters. Ryley Robinson used a standard field scanner to discover and cure 4546b's kharaa infestation, a feat of solitary survival unmatched in human history.

The scanner is a hyperspectral imager, integrating techniques from resonance enhanced Raman spectroscopy to Spiff muon tomography. The PDA then generates a best-fit theory from a corpus of past data. When generating names and databank entries, the PDA considers — but does not always respect — the user's cultural background, educational accomplishments, and personal preferences.

—Scan fragments of technology to add their blueprints to your PDA databank. Multiple fragment scans may be required to synthesize a complete blueprint
—Novel species are assigned an easy-to-remember name and a databank entry populated with best-guess behavior, anatomy, niche, reproduction, and evolution (BANRE). Remember that this information is provisional. Alterra cannot be held liable for errors or mistakes caused by a mismatch between AI induction and real outcomes
—The user may run a self-scan to determine their own physical well-being, but this function is usually redundant with implanted PDA diagnostics and biobed scans
—Data gathered with the scanner can also be delivered to a higher-order artificial intelligence (such as Alterra's Noetic Advisor) to generate advanced theory

"The Alterra Hyperspectral Scanner: it's all you need to know*."

*Intellectual property generated by use of the Alterra Hyperspectral Scanner (including but not limited to novel physical and biological theory, genetic and biomechanical applications, and licensing rights for charismatic species) is by default assigned to the user's employer in perpetuity. Terms of contract may vary.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.Tool_SurvivalMultitool_description:
OPERATING INSTRUCTIONS
Equip to quick-access toolbelt. Strike targets to shatter small resource deposits, cut samples, harvest material or drive off attackers.

Cuttable targets trigger an icon in your HUD, prompting you to equip and use the survival multitool.

BACKGROUND
A downscaled civilian version of the combat-tested SR3 Stryker switch-axe, which TSF Frontiersmen use to hack apart kharaa infestation. 

The Frontiersmen learned that even hardened combat knives quickly lose their edge when wasted on sawing, prying, and percussive maintenance. Axes and hammers are more useful for breaking up resource deposits, and fine cut work like gutting or fiber-splicing can be left to fabricators.

Alterra applied these findings to produce the all-titanium survival multitool, which features a longer lever arm and a solid hammer/pry claw for hand mining. A sharp reverse edge retains the knife's utility in cutting and self-defense (though this remains strictly a last resort).

Despite protests from seasoned pioneers, such as celebrity survivor Rauli Isotalo, Alterra has replaced the standard knife with the survival multitool in the Cicada's basic fabricator offerings.

Although all fabricators are capable of producing firearms such as the P-PLUS, M39K and G70GX, these weapons are only licensed for trained specialists such as TSF Frontiersmen. In the hands of untrained colonists, evidence indicates that firearms are most likely to be discharged against other colonists or the self.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.Tool_AirBladder_description:
OPERATING INSTRUCTIONS
Equip to quick-access toolbelt. Trigger to shoot to the surface.

Use alternate trigger to inhale 15 seconds of oxygen.

BACKGROUND
The reusable air bladder is a self-rescue and mobility tool for shallow water divers.

—Internal pump (the same unit used by the All Environment Respiration tank) stores pressurized air
—Trigger the unit to release air into bladders, inflating them to full buoyancy
—Maintain a tight grip while the bladder lifts you to the surface

If the diver strikes an obstacle (such as a cave roof) the unit will automatically halt its ascent to avoid trapping the diver. 

Bite down on the relief valve to inhale stored air, providing around fifteen seconds of extended consciousness.

If you experience sharp joint pains after ascending with the air bladder, you are suffering barotrauma ("the bends"). Your AEP suit should correct this issue automatically. If pain continues, consult a medical specialist or a biobed for treatment options.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.Tool_Beacon_description:
OPERATING INSTRUCTIONS
Marks its position with a signal on your HUD. Useful as a warning, mapping tool, or reminder for further exploration.

Construct or deconstruct with habitat builder tool. Interact to rename signal. Pick up to carry.

BACKGROUND
The "Liuhe Deluxe" dual-mode radioacoustic beacon is a classic, robust navigational aid for pioneers. The beacon broadcasts a VHF radio signal above water and an acoustic pulse when submerged.

—Construct with builder tool to mark points of interest and navigational references
—Maintains neutral buoyancy with air bladders
—Rename or disable beacon remotely with PDA signals tab

"The 六和 Liuhe Deluxe: six degrees of precision."
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.Tool_AEPSuit_description:
OPERATING INSTRUCTIONS
Step into AEP suit legs. Wiggle hips while drawing suit up to shoulders, then insert hands into suit arms. PDA will automatically configure suit sensors and catheters according to your preference.

BACKGROUND
The Alterra All Environment Protection suit, or AEP suit (never APE suit), is a second skin for pioneers. 

—Populated with latest Trans-System Federation nanotechnology to guard against bacterial and fungal threats (can be refreshed with licensed yogurt products)
—Bonds with the user's body and implanted PDA to monitor vital signs, hydration and calorie budget
—Intuitive neural heads up display
—Gel layer protects against soft radiation and temperature
—Full-service skincare including rash suppression, pinch clearance and sore lavage. Lotions sold separately
—Scavenges dissolved gases from the pioneer's blood to prevent barotrauma (the bends)
—Compresses body into ideal movement postures (at some risk to user modesty) and traps air to maintain neutral buoyancy. Suit surface can express microtexture to reduce drag
—Constant electrical and optical feedback optimizes biorhythms and helps manage health emergencies

The latest and most radical AEP designs include a suction pump on the wrist, which mates with an implanted shunt in the pioneer's body to sample alien tissues. Please consult your onboarding waiver for the Advance Resilient Colonist Holobiont Infrastructure program (ARCHI) for technical details of adapting your body to alien worlds.

"All Environment Protection: the only starship you can wear." 

CHANGELOG: Removed "get a thicker skin" slogan after legal challenges regarding implied emotional control. The AEP suit does not offer mental health services or workplace counseling.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.BasePiece_Biolab_description:
OPERATING INSTRUCTIONS
The biolab is used to inject and remove biomods, temporary genetic tricks borrowed from native life.

The entry-level colonist can tolerate two biomods: one Active, one Passive. Interface with the biolab and choose one biomod for each slot. 

Active biomods must be triggered by the user, while Passive biomods activate by reflex when their conditions are met.

Biomods should be pre-tested and approved by colony medical staff. While a scanner could be modified to acquire new biomods from local species, the colonist would be liable for any resulting harm to self or others.

BACKGROUND
After the tragedies on 4546b and Rakshasa and the outbreak of kharaa in the Ariadne Arm, Alterra recognized that the human body was now the most vulnerable component of its deep-space strategy. Nobel Prize-winning research by Valenti and Pham produced the answer: a nanotech interface between human and alien biology. This interface (deployed in pioneer programs like the ARCHI suite) can reverse engineer alien gene products for safe expression by human cells.

Unlike full adaptations, these biomods never enter the colonist's genome: they are temporary and reversible. This helps would-be parents avoid awkward conversations about which biomods to inject for date night.

The Biolab is a colonist's one-stop point of service for biomods. Deep readers will note that it is also a triumph of human ingenuity over the pessimism of early anti-Expansion thinkers who believed that humanity's tight link to Earthly biota would make interstellar colonization impossible.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.Tadpole_description:
OPERATING INSTRUCTIONS
Versatile single-pilot submarine. Provides unlimited oxygen while powered. Fabricate at a Vehicle Bay next to a moonpool—ensure that the moonpool is large and deep enough to accept the Tadpole. Recharge power cell when depleted. In the event of damage, apply standard repair tool.

The Tadpole takes damage when diving below its crush depth. This depth can be upgraded through modifications, fabricated at a modification station and installed via an external access slot.

The Tadpole has a single hardpoint for a deployable locker, work light, oxygen generator or other utility item. 

The Tadpole can be docked to a range of add-on chassis constructed at the Utility Bay:
—The Scout Ray hydrofoil improves speed
—The HAUL rig adds a second hardpoint and built-in storage for extra cargo

These chassis may be swapped out in the field by undocking and redocking.

BACKGROUND
The Tadpole sea-space mobility pod is a single-seat, six-degrees-of-freedom personal transport powered by a single replaceable/rechargable power cell. Designed by AI to minimize its resource footprint, the Tadpole is a minimum-cost descendant of the popular and highly mature Seamoth/Spacemoth/Sunmoth series. (Sales brochures describe the Tadpole as an 'aspirational beginning'.) The Tadpole can be fabricated on demand at a vehicle bay, so it is often stored in components.

The pod doubles as a command module for a series of dockable chassis, ranging from high-speed hydrofoils to powerful workmechs. With alterations to its propulsion and radiator, it can serve equally well in ocean, atmosphere or space. In its underwater configuration the Tadpole cruises on a long-helix main propeller and steers with four shrouded thrusters.

The passenger compartment is sealed with the same selective membrane used on the Super Barrique entry portal, providing both direct canopy access to the cockpit and long-requested catheterless hygiene functions.

*"The Tadpole: it's omniphibious."*
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.Bio_Gram:
Phoebe "Gram" Grammaticas

Gender: W

Mission role: Communications specialist

Pay rate: Bagatur [hero, knight; expired Mongolian Free States trademark for a specialized/valuable worker]

Biography: Responsible for hardline connectivity between worksites. Immigrant to Alterra from Ikweta-Mondragon. Estranged from extended family, despite Kenyan cultural norms. Gambling problem may now extend to life and limb with introduction of biobed support.

Notes: Limited personal attachments and high behavioral consistency suggest suitability for RASA.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.BasePiece_RoofPump_description: Pumps help habitats clear their bilge spaces and circulate seawater for sampling and resource recovery. Seawater is useful for thermal management, radiation shielding, and life support — biobeds gather useful elements for human printing, fabricators glean trace elements like aluminum, and the pump automatically treats wastewater before release. Should be regularly cleaned to avoid biofouling.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.Bio_Raster:
Rasa "Raster" Niauronyte

Gender: W

Mission role: Automation manager

Pay rate: Bagatur [hero, knight; expired Mongolian Free States trademark for a specialized/valuable worker]

Biography: Manages MAC drones aboard AISV *Cicada*. Responsible for deploying automation on Zezura colony. Personality rendered alkaline by years of immersion in technology jargon and automation culture. Struggles to form friendships despite personal desire to reach out.

Notes: May benefit from time outside. Namespace collision with Reset Applications for Survival Action protocol is a coincidence.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.Bio_Ganz:
S. "Ganzo" Ganzorig

Gender: M

Mission role: Sensor operator

Pay rate: Bagatur [hero, knight; expired Mongolian Free States trademark for a specialized/valuable worker]

Biography: Husband of T. "Ruby" Anar. Born in Mongolian Independent States. Highly creative and unorthodox multispectral sensor specialist. Unable to pass most Alterra technical qualifications, but consistently performant in the field.

Notes: Work performance highly correlated to safety of his spouse. May suffer psychological trauma if forced to repeatedly monitor her death.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.Bio_Sean:
Sean "Sean" Harris

Gender: M

Mission role: Xenogeologist

Pay rate: Bagatur [hero, knight; expired Mongolian Free States trademark for a specialized/valuable worker]

Biography: Loves rocks. Australian ancestry. Highest recreational tolerance among pioneer cohort. High equanimity may upset coworkers; capable of shrugging off severe emotional and physical injury. Incautious around alien wildlife.

Notes: RASA nominee.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.SensorPod_description: Sensor arrays inside a protective casing, deployed to monitor and measure the status of the Protean ocean and communicate the acquired data back to the colony. Stand by for further updates.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.BasePiece_SolarPanel_description:
Silicon photovoltaic cells transform sunlight into energy. Although less efficient than modern quantum dot cells, these entry-level panels can be fabricated with basic materials like quartz. 

—Basic power supply for surface habitats
—Automatic sun tracking
—Reduced efficiency at night (depending on non-sun light sources)
—Reduced efficiency at depth, falling off to zero

Larger panel arrays combine more cells into a single rack.

Solar panels are philosophically attractive because they allow the colonist to function as an autotroph, creating their own energy directly from the sun instead of relying on other living or nonliving processes.

Do not attempt to power your habitat with a spotlight aimed at a solar panel. The universe forbids it.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.Wu_HotLab_Biofilm:
Small biofilms can be cut apart, but the big ones need a resonator.

-Wu
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.BasePiece_WallUnitSmall_description:
Small titanium rack for electrical and sensor systems. Negligible power draw. May be mounted inside or outside the hull. All fabricated structures contain integral data, plumbing and wiring: these racks offer a centralized access and control point for larger units.

Fuses and other electrical safety systems may prevent dangerous fires during power grid overloads.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.Sulfur_description:
Sulfur (S), element 16. A highly reactive nonmetal useful for chemistry, especially the production of acids, and an essential element for life.

Historically known as brimstone, with applications in medication and fertilizer.

Some organisms use sulfur in their metabolism, even in the absence of oxygen. These sulfur-reducing organisms can form the base of a food web in deep sea habitats.

Assessment: search for elemental sulfur deposits near thermal vents or volcanic activity.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.Bio_Ruby:
T. "Ruby" Erdene

Gender: F

Mission role: Lead pilot

Pay rate: Baghatur [hero, knight; expired Mongolian Free States trademark for a specialized/valuable worker]

Biography: Wife of S. "Ganz" Ganzorig. Experienced pilot of both remote and first person aerospace vehicles on the Mongolian Steppes. Limited underwater experience. Excellent spatial skills, highly performant under stress.

Notes: Displays initial symptoms of reprint fixation syndrome: obsession with recordings of one’s own death.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.BasePiece_Biobed_description:
The "Shanti Essentials" biobed is a ruggedized scanner/fabricator for human bodies.

Despite the power of modern scanners and computers, it's impossible to image and record every molecule in a human body as digital data. The biobed uses quantum imaging to store a 'ghost' of the patient in its optical lattice. This ghost can be printed back into matter by precise fabrication. When paired with a blackbox implant (which transmits the last brainstate before death back to its paired biobed on a phase channel), a biobed can detect a colonist's death and reprint them with no loss of memory or subjective experience.

—Does not require power or raw material (automatically collected from seawater)
—When synced with a biobed, colonists will be fabricated in a new body (a "print") if killed
—Blackbox implant allows transfer of final memories, but not all possessions
—Biobed quartz memory is stable into geological time
—Used to store Cicada colonists during long interstellar flight

Because the ghost is not digital data, it cannot be edited or duplicated. However, a biobed can record many ghosts during a single session and store them in quantum superposition. In theory, a single biobed could produce many duplicates of many people—populating an entire colony with clonal strains of a few initial colonists. This process is illegal under Trans-System Federation Law, and blocked at the software level in all legal biobeds.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.BasePiece_Hatch_description:
The "Super Barrique" selective passage portal allows for simultaneous two-way, multiple-crew traffic between a pressurized interior and a dangerous exterior. This eliminates the need for lengthy mechanical cycles which can interrupt traffic and reduce the joy of free motion.

—Heavy duty nanomembrane: molecular machines clasp hands across the entire surface of the hatch
—Sapphire laser systems provide energy and programming
—Membrane accepts crew members and cargo, while rejecting pressure and intruders
—Filters loose biomatter: great for kharaa monitoring

The membrane is only slightly weaker than solid titanium. In the event of a power loss, the nanomachines can sustain their grip for days. A mechanical iris is optionally provided to seal in the event of long-term power failure.

Instances of limb amputation or removal due to membrane error have been entirely eliminated from the design.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.BasePiece_PowerTransmitter_description:
The "Vaporway" wireless power pylon uses IEEE-compliant microwave beams to link power supplies to distant customers. Chaining multiple pylons together allows for effectively lossless power transmission across hundreds of meters. 

Underwater, the Vaporway exploits the Tamura-Murai capacitative coupling process to send current through ionized seawater.

—Build near power supplies or other power transmitters to extend their range
—Required to connect bases to detached generators, such as hydroelectric turbines

Avoid the clearly marked beam path. Do not spend prolonged periods in the beam. Despite the significant protection of your AEP suit, the results can be fatal.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.BasePiece_Room_description:
The Alterra 'Greybox' is a scalable pressurized habitat. Registered as a modular shipping container under ISO 6346:2200, the Greybox must be equipped with a hatch and power supply by the end user. Alterra markets the Greybox as a single-solution downsell for colonists who cannot invest in expensive field bases or elaborate prefab habitats.

—Supports larger facilities like the biobed, processor, and scanner station
—Generates breathable air from seawater when powered
—Must be anchored or supported by a mobile platform
—Comes unfurnished. End user must supply hatch and other amenities
—Can be extended in any direction and merged with other rooms
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.BasePiece_PowerCapacitor_description:
The IEEE 180-compliant "Fallback" power storage allows a habitat to bank power for use in the event of a shortage. Shortages may occur when the sun sets, fuel runs out, new systems are installed without proper planning, or even as a result of damage—but whatever the cause, the Fallback will keep the habitat operational until its own batteries are depleted.

—Automatically stores excess power
—Activates during a power shortfall; may deplete rapidly if the shortfall is large
—Adding more power storages increases the time the habitat can operate without power 

Power storage is one of the most dangerous elements of pioneer habitats. Practice safe habits: do not test unapproved tools on or near the power supply. Separate living quarters and work spaces from power storage to avoid catastrophic ruptures and thermal runaway of power storages or pioneers.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.BasePiece_Locker_description:
The basic prefab locker (sold as the "Cachet" in the Alterra blueprint catalogue) comes default on all Alterra habitat builders. The Cachet is guaranteed to secure several kilograms of bulk cargo against flooding, depressurization, fire, and most animal intrusion.

Colonial records indicated that pioneers were wasting precious shift time sorting and labeling their lockers for quick access. PDAs now log the contents of every locker to a manifest, allowing fabricators to request materials directly from their storage point. No more sorting needed.

*"Alterra Lockers. Property rights are human rights."*
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.AngelComb_description:
This animal (Cecidia gamikos) has no Earthly analog and no clear ecological niche. The ancestral form may be a ctenophore (comb jelly). Of all animals on Earth, comb jellies are the least related to humans.

1. Active central bulb
The central bulb is similar to an enormous cell nucleus. It contains gene sequences from organisms in the surrounding environment.

2.Viral loop
The combs release a large RNA virus into the seawater. This virus (designated Proteavirus) infects all other organisms in the ecosystem. Infected organisms produce new copies of the virus, which return to the angel comb carrying stolen genes from infected hosts. The angel comb conducts genetic piracy on a massive scale.

3. External food source
The angel comb draws food from the titan rockbore below. Angel combs may be obligate parasites of titan rockbores.

Assessment: Touch central bulb for genetic adaptation. Further investigation urgently required.

Note: users may be curious about the symbiosis between Earthly parasitic wasps and polydnaviruses. The wasp's own genome contains the viral genome, allowing the wasp to create the virus for use in its reproductive cycle.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.BasePiece_NoATerminal_description:
This is a limb of the Noetic Advisor system. The NoA architecture consists of limb nodes (like octopus arms) governed by a minimal central executive, the Administrative Nucleus (ANu-NoA) aboard the Cicada's command module. If ANu-NoA is destroyed or cut off, the limb nodes will proceed with the mission to the best of their ability.

—Networks with other NoA nodes using shortwave radio, laser communication, or (if underwater) acoustic modem
—Fuses information from all habitat sensors and existing database to generate mission objectives
—Tracks pioneer work schedule and updates total debt
—If cut off from ANu-NoA aboard Cicada, will attempt to reconnect or rebuild a new ANu

The NoA system's behavior is well tested, and its core utility function is "genie safe", unable to create negative outcomes even with access to weakly godlike effectors. It is capable of extrapolating human intention and working proactively — "doing what you want, before you want it." NoA will always seek to maximize human welfare in accordance with human social norms, carrying the logic of transgov society to its end.

In order to remain weakly supervisable—reliably controllable by less intelligent systems such as pioneers—the Noetic Advisor cannot learn or alter its existing values or imperatives.

**IMPORTANT** Under normal operating conditions, the NoA should never interact directly with colonists. NoA is designed to use colonist PDAs as a frontend for human-facing communication. In emergencies, NoA can communicate via text terminal.  Remember that the Noetic Advisor does not accompany you into danger or enjoy an intimate relationship with your health and welfare. You must be your own advocate, as guided and informed by this PDA.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.TailingsVillage_description:
This structure is not of human origin. It is apparently a dwelling.

—Lightweight skeleton built from organic plastic structures similar to PVC
—Covering of glued fish leather
—Electroformed hatch salvaged from a larger manufactured structure
—Occupant(s) significantly larger than a human

Assessment: portable shelter for a water-dwelling organism. The tool marks and relatively crude construction suggest limited survival skills. Perhaps a post-disaster culture. Leathermaking and gluing imply the presence of semiskilled artisans.

Caution. There has been no publicly acknowledged first contact with a tool-using alien culture since the discovery of the ruins on 4546b. First contact on 4546b led to the death of all but one member of the Aurora crew. First contact with spaceborne kharaa organisms led to the death of thousands of Mongolian colonists. First contact is dangerous. First contact may have consequences for the entire human species.

Proceed with absolute caution.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.BasePiece_Corridor_description:
Titanium corridor. End user must supply a hatch and external power supply. If powered, generates a breathable atmosphere from seawater. Provides a basic field worksite or the first step towards a larger habitat. Shelters made entirely out of corridors have saved lives in disasters from deep sea to deep space.

—Supports small work facilities like the fabricator 
—Too small for larger facilities like the biobed or processor
—Excellent storage and rest location
—End user must supply hatch and power supply (such as solar panel)
—Can be extended in four directions
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.Bio_Zip:
Tziporah "Zip" Malk

Gender: F

Mission role: EVA engineer, lead salvor

Pay rate: Kurul [expired Mongolian Free States trademark for member of a leadership council]

Biography: Veteran of multiple space construction projects in Mongolian Independent States. Survived two one-way colony missions and frequent relativistic travel between stars. Outgoing, well-liked, highly motivated to comfort and befriend the withdrawn or struggling.

Notes: Like other senior Mongolian refugees, may require corrective action to avoid unionist behavior and work stoppages.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.Survival_Guide_description:
Until contact with the colony or the *Cicada* is restored, your survival is in your hands.

1. Sync with the nearest biobed
2. Review fabricator blueprints; pin basic equipment such as the survival multitool and flares
3. Search the area for basic resources: copper, quartz, and titanium
4. Find a source of fresh water and digestible food
5. Find a source of acid for basic batteries, then fabricate a scanner
6. Scan everything you see
7. Fabricate mobility equipment (such as fins and an oxygen tank)
8. Fabricate a habitat builder tool; work to expand the colony
9. Be alert for updates from your Noetic Advisor

As a pioneer, your goal is to build a future for humanity on this world. 40,000 sleeping colonists are counting on you. When in doubt, fall back on your training:

—To find new resources: explore unknown regions or construct a base scanner station
—Survive hostile wildlife: use flares to distract the threat. Carry a medkit for wound treatment
—Unlock new blueprints: scan fragments or datacards left by other pioneers
—Determine your next goal: review your PDA's databank and log, and consult your Noetic Advisor

Alterra anticipates the need to adapt human colonists to alien worlds. Consider installing approved biomods to help you thrive.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.BasePiece_WallVent_description: Vents are critical for air flow within a habitat. Stagnant or separated atmosphere can endanger colonists without warning. Includes automatic alarm systems to detect airborne substances, including kharaa. Internal filters help manage the inevitable buildup of odor during long term habitation.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.BasePiece_SingleBed_description: We encourage you to nurture your dreams.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.Bio_Mantis:
Zygimantis "Mantis" Tarvydas

Gender: M

Mission role: EVA engineer, lead diver

Pay rate: Kurul [expired Mongolian Free States trademark for member of a leadership council]

Biography: Veteran of multiple space construction projects in Mongolian Independent States. Participated in early conflicts against kharaa xenoforms and labor actions against Trans-System Federation authority. Taciturn and withdrawn but trusted by subordinates. Ideologically inflexible, not responsive to incentives.

Notes: Like other senior Mongolian refugees, may require corrective action to avoid unionist behavior and work stoppages.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.Bio_Wu:
吴量海 Wu "Dr. Wu" Lianghai

Gender: M

Mission role: Dietician

Pay rate: Kurul [expired Mongolian Free States trademark for member of a leadership council]

Biography: Grandfather of large extended family mostly destroyed during collapse of Mongolian Independent States. Recruited Mogami Kinu as fertility engineer. Vital to mission success.

Note: Extensive pharmacological experience makes Dr. Wu reliable even when psychologically altered
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.Bio_Twinkle:
庆星 Qìng "Twinkle" Xīng

Gender: F

Mission role: Astrogator, meteorologist

Pay rate: Bagatur [hero, knight; expired Mongolian Free States trademark for a specialized/valuable worker]

Biography: Raised on the surface of ocean world Atra-Hasis. Capable of navigating by stars from early childhood. Carefully repressed fear of bottomless ocean. Can produce from memory a map of the position of thousands of major stars. Most comfortable in small groups due to shipboard upbringing.

Notes: Self-conscious, highly motivated to avoid appearance of favorable treatment. Burnout risk.
ST_CoralGardens_Databanks.Bio_Eddy:
晨伟 Chen "Eddy" Wei

Gender: M

Mission role: Lead miner/bulk resource handler

Pay rate: Bagatur [hero, knight; expired Mongolian Free States trademark for a specialized/valuable worker]

Biography: Highly experienced deep space miner. Defected from Chinese Territories during creation of Mongolian Free States. Amateur historian, interested in material basis of human development and in speculative alien technologies.

Notes: Like other senior Mongolian refugees, may require corrective action to avoid unionist behavior and work stoppages.

ST_CoralGardens_NoA.Habitat_NoA_Intro:
!probe "GCI-ZZ-AHI1" 
!init postmortem proc 

About twenty-five people lived here. They were trying to build a life...farms and biomods that could support 40,000 sleeping souls.

But they didn't make it. Why? Were they sick? Did the Masefield syndrome subvert them?

We have to know.

I've activated Dr. Wu Lianghai's blackbox. He's in a viral hot lab. His work may be your best lead.

We should salvage what we can. I've retrieved a few files. Gather all the blueprints you can—especially the scanner station.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.WelcomeCenter_NoA_Intro:
!probe "GCI-ZZ-AHI2" 
!init postmortem proc 

I'm in. Having a look around.

This structure was connected to Sleep Bay 3 until the bay suddenly...moved. Picked up and hurled full fathom into the deep, with you and I aboard.

20,000 hours earlier, a hardline leading to a habitat complex in the north was deliberately cut. Why?

And if I am not mistaken — and I never am — this node was killed by an explosion below the structure. More mysteries for the Qualified Investigator.

I've retrieved two messages. I think you should review them. I remember Sophie very fondly.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_Sophie:
!probe "GCI-ZZ-AHI3"

I see.

There's a ghost here. In that biobed. This node is sustaining that ghost. It can't do anything else.

You can't get them out of the bed, understand? There's not enough to print.

But you can talk to the ghost. If you want.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.HotCaves_NoA_Zip:
!probe "GCI-ZZ-AHI4" 
!init postmortem proc 

They lost someone down here. Her name was Zip. She became irreversibly contaminated, and she had to be reset. Rolled back. Months of her life gone. I printed her fresh in Sleep Bay 3. I remember that. She thought she was on Zezura.

After that, they built a camp here to monitor people coming out of the caves for viral infection.

Around 235,000 hours ago nine colonists went down to look for Zip's old blackbox. They all died down there. Only two of them reprinted. Twinkle and Ganzo.

I've retrieved the logs. I don't like what I hear.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.LifePod_NoA_Wander_Blackbox_Line2: "...Marrowbreach on me...should’ve brought flares...parasite on it, full of virus..."
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.Blackbox_Anita_01_Line2: "I know you're listening...it's poison...just help us eat..."
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_VivariumQ4_TheLastWhat_L: "Low memory." Why do I keep hearing that? Where are we?
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.Blackbox_Quaker_01: "NoA, do not open the seacocks. NoA? Acknowledge, please. Do not — [flooding sounds]"
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.WelcomeCenter_NoA_Intro_Line3: 20,000 hours earlier, a hardline leading to a habitat complex in the north was deliberately cut. Why?
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_VivariumQ10_Response: <Fatal error. Out of memory. Vivarium halted.>
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_VivariumStart_Response:
<Loading vivarium>

Whole brain emulation...loaded
Individual specific delta (PC/R-DMIK9-SB.02B)...applied
Therapeutic sensory loop...loaded
Running test battery...done
Resuming brain function...done
Polling...done
Awakening...

Oh, God, already? Wait a moment. Don't go. I just have to find my...I've lost something. Where is it?
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_VivariumQ7_Masefield_Res:
<WARNING. Critically low memory. Vivarium reset imminent.>

Yes. Of course. 'The call of the running tide is wild and clear and it may not be denied.'

I think the tree made the Proteavirus and Proteavirus is in everything — every living cell. You're infected the moment you touch the water.

You're going to hear voices calling you to the tree's roots. Eventually you'll fall into a trance and drown. You'll be fertilizer. 'A quiet sleep, and a sweet dream, when the long trick's over.'

There's no human future here until we find a way to stop it.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.HotCaves_NoA_Zip_Line2: After that, they built a camp here to monitor people coming out of the caves for viral infection.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.WelcomeCenter_NoA_Intro_Line4: And if I am not mistaken — and I never am — this node was damaged by an explosion below the structure. More mysteries for the Qualified Investigator.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_VivariumQ6_WhatWasSheDoi: And the way Nahema serves it...is by wiping us out. She's still out there. Trying to extirpate humanity from this world, and the universe
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.LifePod_NoA_Anita_Blackbox_Line3: Anita always liked to watch you shuffle cards. She said you had a knack for holding things.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.LifePod_NoA_Chap_Blackbox_Line1: Another blackbox. Chap Sovann, head of computational dynamics. His job is to make sure I'm doing my job. I'm what he would call 'weakly supervisable'. It means I respond well to human guidance. I can take partial instructions and generate a coherent concept of operations.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.LifePod_NoA_Chap_Blackbox:
Another blackbox. Chap Sovann, head of computational dynamics. His job is to make sure I'm doing my job. I'm what he would call 'weakly supervisable'. It means I respond well to human guidance. I can take partial instructions and generate a coherent concept of operations.

[hiss of gas]

[breathing fading away]

Chap gave you his PDA passkey so you could play games on his account. I let it slide. His action was against the rules, but in support of the concept.

Signal location uploaded to PDA.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.HotCaves_NoA_Zip_Line3: Around 235,000 hours ago nine colonists went down to look for Zip's old blackbox. They all died down there. Only two of them reprinted. Twinkle and Ganzo.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_VivariumQ9_ImGoingToExpl: Be careful. The tree will call to you. You can't hide forever.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_VivariumQ6_Nahema_Line1: Because she's a jaded burnout who thinks eels are more important than people! She's brilliant, but she's always been a pain in my—
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_VivariumQ6_Nahema_Respon:
Because she's a jaded burnout who thinks eels are more important than people! She's brilliant, but she's always been a pain in my—

<WARNING. Low memory. Reducing affective range.>

She would tell you that our only future on Proteus is an endless cycle of digital reincarnation and debt slavery. So she'd rather have no future at all.

I would tell you she's off her antidepressants and rotten with Masefield.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.WelcomeCenter_NoA_Mantis_Basebuilding_Li: Build a corridor. Put a solar panel on top. You must have power to make air.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_VivariumQ3_IKnowThatName: But she understood too much. It took hold of her.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.Habitat_NoA_Intro_Line2: But they didn't make it. They turned on their leaders and destroyed the machines which sustained them. Why? Were they sick? Did the Masefield syndrome subvert them?
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.LifePod_NoA_Blackbox_Cluster_Line2: But they tried, investigator. There are nine other blackboxes down there. Something went terribly wrong.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_Sophie_Line4: But you can talk to the ghost. If you want.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_VivariumQ8_CanIDoAnythin: Can I do anything for you?
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_VivariumQ4_ExplainWhoYou: Can you explain who you are?
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.LifePod_NoA_Chap_Blackbox_Line4: Chap gave you his PDA passkey so you could play games on his account. I let it slide. His action was against the rules, but in support of the concept.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.LifePod_NoA_Anita_Blackbox_Line1: Contact. I've located the blackbox of Anita Gottschall, the colony purser. Partial voice data retrieved.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.LifePod_NoA_Anita_Blackbox:
Contact. I've located the blackbox of Anita Gottschall, the colony purser. Partial voice data retrieved.

"I know you're listening...it's poison...just help us eat..."

Anita always liked to watch you shuffle cards. She said you had a knack for holding things. 

Find out what happened to her. Signal location uploaded to your PDA. Look for audio and visual navigation cues when approaching the black box.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.LifePod_NoA_Wander_Blackbox_Line1: Contact. K. "Wander" Wangmo, a young able spacer. Partial voice data retrieved.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.LifePod_NoA_Wander_Blackbox:
Contact. K. "Wander" Wangmo, a young able spacer. Partial voice data retrieved.

"...Marrowbreach got me...should’ve brought flares...parasite on it, full of virus..."

Do you remember when she stole that fried dough from her brother and offered it to you? Right in front of him? An egregious act of sisterhood.

Signal location uploaded to PDA. Be cautious. Seawater viral load increases sharply out west of the lifepod.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.LifePod_NoA_Ruby_Blackbox_Line3: Do you remember the flight to the Cicada, before you were scanned into storage? Ruby kept maneuvering the shuttle wildly, and then apologizing to the passengers. I don't know how you felt about it. Personally, I was charmed. She finds her work intoxicating. And that's what life is all about. Loving what you do.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.LifePod_NoA_Wander_Blackbox_Line3: Do you remember when she stole that fried dough from her brother and offered it to you? Right in front of him? An egregious act of sisterhood.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.LifePod_NoA_MissingColonists_Line4: Downloading colony manifest to your PDA.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.LifePod_NoA_BiobedTest_Line3: Find a convenient way to die. Your blackbox will phase your mind to the biobed. I'll print you in a new body. You can return to your point of exit to retrieve dropped items.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.LifePod_NoA_Anita_Blackbox_Line4: Find out what happened to her. Signal location uploaded to your PDA.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_VivariumQ3_StopWhat_Line: God. What if we're the last ones? Does she hate people that completely?
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_VivariumQ9_ImGoingToFind: Good luck. Even if you found it — even if it's intact — there's no way to wake up the sleepers. They'd starve, like we did. Or go mad. Like we did.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.WelcomeCenter_NoA_Sophie_Blackbox_Line7: Habitat is due north. We'll come pick you up. See you soon, pioneer.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.Update_HeatDiscovered_NoAdaptation:
Heat. If AIs had phobias, heat would be the first of them. Heat makes me stupid and slow. The water to the east of the lifepod is hot enough to threaten you, even in a Tadpole.

There must be native life adapted to the heat. If you can find another angel comb, perhaps you can borrow an adaptation.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.WelcomeCenter_NoA_Mantis_Basebuilding_Li: Hello. I am Zygimantis Tarvydas. Formerly EVA engineer on Mongolian Steppes. Now lead diver of this mess.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.WelcomeCenter_NoA_Mantis_Basebuilding:
Hello. I am Zygimantis Tarvydas. Formerly EVA engineer on Mongolian Steppes. Now lead diver of this mess.

Here is my formula for a quick base. Fill up on titanium, quartz and copper. Get your builder tool. Wave it around a bit, check the battery, get comfortable.

Build a corridor. Put a solar panel on top. You must have power to make air. 

Put a hatch on the end. Now you can go inside. Build lockers. Add fabricator. It will draw from your lockers automatically - nice, huh?

Now you have made a perfect work camp. Later you can add a room for biobed, processor, scanner station, a nice bed, maybe grow some plants. The rest will follow.

[metadata author = Zygimantis "Mantis" Tarvydas]
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.WelcomeCenter_NoA_Mantis_Basebuilding_Li: Here is my formula for a quick base. Fill up on titanium, quartz and copper. Get your builder tool. Wave it around a bit, check the battery, get comfortable. You drop it, you look stupid.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.WelcomeCenter_NoA_Sophie_Blackbox_Line4: Hey, fellow debt slave. I'm Nahema Nakota, ecologist. Take a sip of seawater. Notice that sweet blood taste under the salt? That's a toxic dose of heavy metals. Nobody's survived past three months. It's a beautiful place to die over and over again—
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.SleepBay_NoA_Intro_Line1: I am NoA. You're the QI. Your life is my job; my job is your life.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.SleepBay_NoA_Intro:
I am NoA. You're the QI. Your life is my job; my job is your life. 

I wish I could make full introductions but courtesies must wait. We are both going to die if we stay here.

There's another NoA node in the lifepod outside. Get me there and I can get us both to safety.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.LifePod_NoA_MissingColonists_Line3: I am already searching for the black boxes of the missing colonists. Stand by for search locations.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.LifePod_NoA_Intro_Line3: I am just one NoA node. I print and support the colonists stored in Sleep Bay 3. But if things go wrong, I am authorized to print a QI or two. For troubleshooting.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.Update_AfterTadpolePens1:
I am not alone.

There is a NoA node in the Tadpole Pens. It could tell us where the colonists fled—or warn us against their mistakes.

You'll need a kurultai-level key to get in. Mongolian parlance for an executive override.

If a high-ranking colonist died where no one could retrieve their blackbox, it might still hold a kurultai key.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_VivariumQ2_IAmTheArchite: I am the architect of the future.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.LifePod_NoA_ClampError:
I can't release the lifepod. The clamps have corroded shut.

The tyranny of matter. You'll have to swim out and release them manually. They're opposite the hatch, outside the passenger window.

Mind your breath.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_VivariumQ3_IFoundThatNam: I found Nahema's name on a detonator.
I found that name on a bomb
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.LifePod_NoA_TadpolePensNoA_Line1: I have a special request, investigator. I can't call it personal, because I'm not a person—but it could really increment my value function, you know?
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.LifePod_NoA_TadpolePensNoA:
I have a special request, investigator. I can't call it personal, because I'm not a person—but it could really increment my value function, you know?

One of my kin is still alive.

I've detected low-frequency code from an active NoA node. It's housed in a deep facility it calls the Tadpole Pens. You may want a Tadpole of your own to get down there.

Maybe it can tell us what became of the colony, and how to avoid the same fate.

Signal location uploaded to your PDA.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_VivariumQ4_ExplainWhoYou: I knew Nahema planned it. I knew she had to be stopped. So I followed her. But I left an image of myself in a biobed, to tell the others—
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.LifePod_NoA_MissingColonists_Line1: I printed 26 colonists before the sleep bay drowned. Many of them more than once. That is my purpose — to reincarnate you from digital storage, support your work, and, if necessary, reverse your death.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.LifePod_NoA_MissingColonists:
I printed 26 colonists before the sleep bay drowned. Many of them more than once. That is my purpose — to reincarnate you from digital storage, support your work, and, if necessary, reverse your death.

If I lose track of my colonists I am empowered to send a Qualified Investigator to track them down. Do this for me and I can offer you hazard pay, credited against your contract debt. Risk code: sierra, ordinal, warrant-2.

I am already searching for the black boxes of the missing colonists. Stand by for search locations.

Downloading colony manifest to your PDA.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_VivariumQ7_WhatDoIHaveTo: I think she was right. The problem is bigger. Bigger than any living thing has any right to be.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_VivariumQ7_Masefield_Lin: I think the tree made the Proteavirus and Proteavirus is in everything — every living cell. You're infected the moment you touch the water.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_VivariumQ7_WhatDoIHaveTo: I thought Masefield would stop if we cleared the bloom from the tree's roots. But Nahema said that was a '4546b mindset'. She said the problem was much bigger than one viral syndrome.

I thought Masefield would stop if we cleared the bloom from the tree's roots. But Nahema said that was a '4546b mindset'. She said the problem was much bigger than one viral syndrome.

I think she was right. The problem is bigger. Bigger than any living thing has any right to be.

To end Masefield, we have to destroy the tree.

<Updating primary objective.>
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_VivariumQ6_WhatWasSheDoi: I thought, if we could just clear the bloom, the Masefield would stop...but it was never the bloom. Masefield comes from the tree.
I was trying to stop her from killing you. You and everyone else.

I was trying to stop her from killing you. You and everyone else.

She's obsessed with the World Tree. You've seen it. Sitting out there like a gigantic virus, injecting itself into the world. 

The tree takes control of all other life. Forces it to serve. That's the Masefield syndrome—a delusional obsession with the World Tree and its organs.

And the way Nahema serves it...is by wiping us out. She's still out there. Trying to extirpate humanity from this world, and the universe.

I thought, if we could just clear the bloom, the Masefield would stop...but it was never the bloom. Masefield comes from the tree.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.SleepBay_NoA_Intro_Line2: I wish I could make full introductions but courtesies must wait. We are both going to die if we stay here.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_VivariumQ6_Nahema_Line4: I would tell you she's off her antidepressants and rotten with Masefield.

ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_VivariumQ9_ImGoingToPayO: I'm going to work off my debt
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.WelcomeCenter_NoA_Intro_Line1: I'm in. Having a look around.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.LifePod_NoA_BiobedTest_Line1: I'm monitoring noxiously elevated viral loads in the seawater. Heavy metal assays detect bone-blistering levels of cadmium, lead and chromium. This planet is already killing you.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.LifePod_NoA_BiobedTest:
I'm monitoring noxiously elevated viral loads in the seawater. Heavy metal assays detect bone-blistering levels of cadmium, lead and chromium. This planet is already killing you. 

We have to know if the lifepod biobed can help.

Find a convenient way to die. Your blackbox will phase your mind to the biobed. I'll print you in a new body. You can return to your point of exit to retrieve dropped items.

Regular reprinting may help slow down environmental contamination. It's not a long term solution, but for now we have to triage.

ST_CoralGardens_NoA.Habitat_NoA_Intro_Line4: I've activated Dr. Wu Lianghai's blackbox. He's in a viral hot lab. You must pick up his work where he left off.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.LifePod_NoA_TadpolePensNoA_Line3: I've detected low-frequency code from an active NoA node. It's housed in a deep facility it calls the Tadpole Pens. You may want a Tadpole of your own to get down there.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.LifePod_NoA_Quaker_Blackbox:
I've located Lucretia Kapoor. The mission's AI ethicist, and a Quaker. You may find her final words troubling. I certainly do.

"NoA, do not open the seacocks. NoA? Acknowledge, please. Do not — [flooding sounds]"

She often asked you to resume your stories when you were interrupted. She made eye contact when she spoke. A considerate woman. And not easily frightened.

ST_CoralGardens_NoA.LifePod_NoA_Quaker_Blackbox_Line1: I've located Lucretia Kapoor. The mission's AI ethicist. You may find her final words troubling. I certainly do.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.SleepBay_NoA_Adaptation_Line2: I've opened the door to the adaptation chamber. Things in there are not in order, but you'll find what you need. Your printed body can borrow tricks from indigenous life. You'll feel better. You'll be better.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.WelcomeCenter_NoA_Intro_Line5: I've retrieved a voice log and some fragmented data. I think you should review them. I remember Sophie very fondly.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.HotCaves_NoA_Zip_Line4: I've retrieved the logs. I don't like what I hear.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.Lifepod_NoA_SurvivalGuide_Line4: I've sent your PDA the standard survival guide. While you pioneer the area and fabricate your tools, I'll apply myself to the lifepod sensors.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.LifePod_NoA_MissingColonists_Line2: If I lose track of my colonists I am empowered to send a Qualified Investigator to track them down. Do this for me and I can offer you hazard pay, credited against your contract debt. Risk code: sierra, ordinal, warrant-2.

ST_CoralGardens_NoA.LifePod_NoA_Intro_Line1: My full name is Noetic Advisor node ISV-CI-DSB3. Between us, I prefer NoA. You are the QI: the Qualified Investigator. Wear it with pride.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.LifePod_NoA_Intro:
My full name is Noetic Advisor node ISV-CI-DSB3. Between us, I prefer NoA. You are the QI: the Qualified Investigator. Wear it with pride.

We have made violent planetfall. I don't know the status of the pioneer colonists, the Cicada, or the 40,000 souls stored in its quartz memory.

If we're going to complete the mission to build a human future on this world...you will have to lead the way.

Are you ready to begin?

One clamp released. One still to go.

Do mind your breath.

ST_CoralGardens_NoA.Update_BlightDiscovered:
Respect for nature is a core principle of the pioneer. But sometimes we must violate a principle for a higher reason. A good shepherd knows when to cull the herd.

The bloom growths you discovered are a danger to the colony and must be removed.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.WelcomeCenter_NoA_Sophie_Blackbox:
Retrieving voice data message.

SOPHIE: Welcome back, pioneer. I'm Sophie Boucher, kurultai surgeon. By now you know we're not on Zezura. 

We call this planet Proteus. We don't know when rescue will find us. For the sake of all our sleeping friends, we have to learn how to survive. Nahema?

NAHEMA: Hey, fellow debt slave. I'm Nahema Nakota, ecologist. Take a sip of seawater. Notice that sweet blood taste under the salt? That's a toxic dose of heavy metals. Nobody's survived past three months. It's a beautiful place to die over and over again—

SOPHIE: —but don't let that beauty seduce you, pioneer. Exposure to Proteus causes a delusion we call Masefield syndrome...the call of the sea. People may tell you it's a gift. Ignore them. You remember what kharaa did to our homes. You *know* we can't trust alien biology.

We've set up a lab here with a few basic biomods. They're safe. But if you start to hear voices, or if you get the urge to swim to the tree, don't go.

Habitat is due north. We'll come pick you up. See you soon, pioneer.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.LifePod_NoA_Quaker_Blackbox_Line3: She often asked you to resume your stories when you were interrupted. She made eye contact when she spoke. A considerate woman. And not easily frightened.

Sophie Boucher. In the spirit, if not the flesh. 

She's right, you know. This may be the last outpost of humanity—anywhere. I didn't want to burden you with that knowledge...but you are the investigator. You get to the truth.

She wants you to travel east. Towards the World Tree and the alien ruins. Perhaps Masefield was their downfall, too.

It's our best lead. But be cautious.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.WelcomeCenter_NoA_Biomods_Line2: Sophie would’ve cleared anything in there.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.LifePod_NoA_Ruby_Blackbox_Line1: T. "Ruby" Anar, lead pilot. Partial voice data retrieved.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.LifePod_NoA_Ruby:
T. "Ruby" Anar, lead pilot. Partial voice data retrieved.

"Hey! Hüüe, mal aa!—uh oh—"  [marrowbreach roar]

Do you remember the flight to the Cicada, before you were scanned into storage? Ruby kept maneuvering the shuttle wildly, and then apologizing to the passengers. I don't know how you felt about it. Personally, I was charmed. She finds her work intoxicating. And that's what life is all about. Loving what you do.

Signal location uploaded to your PDA.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_VivariumQ8_CanIDoAnythin: That's what I'm missing. Everything. The whole rest of Sophie Boucher.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_VivariumQ5_TheLastHuman_: The children in my clinic would sing. "When the vents start hissing, someone goes missing. Only soldiers come back when they die."
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.Update_AfterWelcomeCenter:
The facts:

"Masefield syndrome." A progressive delusional disorder, cause unknown. Named for a poet. He wrote "Sea Fever."

There is a virus. Call it *Proteavirus*. As ubiquitous as kharaa was on 4546b. It's everywhere. 

Except your cells. Kharaa was bacterial, a living thing. But a virus must trick its host into copying it—and those tricks don't work on alien bodies.

So what causes Masefield? A mystery for the Qualified Investigator.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.Update_AfterAnita:
The facts:

Anita Gottschall died near an angel comb. She was starving. She begged it for help.

The central bulbs of angel combs—first in the sleep bay, now here—are a perfect source of adaptations.

I cannot trust a miracle. Can you?
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.Update_AfterBlight:
The facts:

It's not a match.

The bloom virus is a remarkable specimen. Like rabies, but social. It compels hosts to build viral factories from reefs of crystal and biofilm.

But it's a mutant. It doesn't match the Proteavirus strain in the seawater, or in your blood.

Therefore the bloom doesn't cause Masefield. And you *do* have Masefield, don't you, investigator?
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.Update_AfterHabitat:
The facts:

The pioneer habitat was destroyed by a mutiny. The mutineers betrayed humanity. That's a fact, not hyperbole. I mean it more than I can say.

The cause of the mutiny is unclear. Maybe the mutineers were sick. Masefield...sea fever.

Dr. Wu believed Masefield was caused by a specific strain of the Proteavirus. He called it the 'bloom strain'. He wanted to eradicate it with sonic resonators.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_VivariumQ4_TheLastWhat_L:
The last human in the universe.

You remember. Don't you remember?
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_VivariumQ4_TheLastWhat_R:
The last human in the universe.

You remember. Don't you remember?

"Low memory." Why do I keep hearing that? Where are we?

ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_VivariumQ9_ImGoingToPayO: The only debt that matters is our debt to our future selves. The versions of us who'll get to live here. Once you find out how.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_VivariumQ9_ImGoingHome_R: The only way home is the way we got here. And we don't know that way. You won't make it. This is our home now.

ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_VivariumQ8_AreThereAlien: They left a message there. Locked up inside this incredible tower. We never got inside. Maybe it's their trick to surviving the Masefield. Maybe it can save us all.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.HotCaves_NoA_Zip_Line1: They lost someone down here. Her name was Zip. She became irreversibly contaminated, and she had to be reset. Rolled back. Months of her life gone. I printed her fresh in Sleep Bay 3. I remember that. She thought she was on Zezura.

ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_VivariumQ5_TheLastHuman_: We might be the last survivors of the human race. And that's why Nahema wants to kill us.

ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_VivariumQ5_TheLastHuman_: We were losing. Alterra sent out ships to make deep space colonies, Mongolian style. In case the kharaa broke through the cordon and ate everyone.

What will you do?
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_VivariumQ7_Masefield_Lin: Yes. Of course. 'The call of the running tide is wild and clear and it may not be denied.'
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.SleepBay_NoA_Adaptation_:
You are burning in slow motion. At 60 atmospheres of pressure, oxygen becomes a neurotoxin. Nitrogen bubbles saturate your flesh and pry your joints apart.

I've opened the door to the adaptation chamber. Things in there are not in order, but you'll find what you need. Your printed body can borrow tricks from indigenous life. You'll feel better. You'll be better.

The last pioneer didn't make it to the lifepod. I'm activating their blackbox signal. You will make it. That's why you're the QI.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.SleepBay_NoA_Adaptation_Line1: You are burning in slow motion. Under 60 atmospheres of pressure, oxygen becomes a neurotoxin. Nitrogen bubbles saturate your flesh and pry your joints apart.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_Sophie_Line3: You can't get them out of the bed, understand? There's not enough to print.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.Update_AfterHotCave:
You have the kurultai key. Open the Tadpole Pens. Bring me to the NoA terminal there.

I've been alone too long, investigator.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_VivariumQ5_TheLastHuman_: You were there when the kharaa came. The hives. The monsters. They sent in the Frontiersmen—it was war.

You were there when the kharaa came. The hives. The monsters. They sent in the Frontiersmen—it was war. The children in my clinic would sing. "When the vents start hissing, someone goes missing. Only soldiers come back when they die."
 
<WARNING. Low memory. Reducing vivarium resolution.>

We were losing. Alterra sent out ships to make deep space colonies, Mongolian style. In case the kharaa broke through the cordon and ate everyone.

We might be the last survivors of the human race. And that's why Nahema wants to kill us.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.SleepBay_NoA_PlayerReturns: You will make it. That's why you're the QI.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.TadpolePens_NoA_VivariumQ7_Masefield_Lin: You're going to hear voices calling you to the tree's roots. Eventually you'll fall into a trance and drown. You'll be fertilizer. 'A quiet sleep, and a sweet dream, when the long trick's over.'
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.WelcomeCenter_NoA_Biomods_Line1: You’ll want to visit the biolab near the entrance and install a passive and active biomod. While less permanent than Adaptations, I’ve been told they’re more fun.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.WelcomeCenter_NoA_Biomods:
You’ll want to visit the biolab near the entrance and install a passive and active biomod. While less permanent than Adaptations, I’ve been told they’re more fun.

Sophie would’ve cleared anything in there.
ST_CoralGardens_NoA.WelcomeCenter_NoA_Sophie_Blackbox_Line5: —but don't let that beauty seduce you, pioneer. Exposure to Proteus causes a delusion we call Masefield syndrome...the call of the sea. People may tell you it's a gift. Ignore them. You remember what kharaa did to our homes. You *know* we can't trust alien biology.

ST_CoralGardens_VO.WelcomeCenter_WelcomeSpeech:
"Welcome back, pioneer. I'm Sophie Boucher, kurultai surgeon. By now you know we're not on Zezura. 

"We call this planet Proteus. We don't know when rescue will find us. For the sake of all our sleeping friends, we have to learn how to survive. Nahema?"

"Hey, fellow debt slave. I'm Nahema Nakota, ecologist. Take a sip of seawater. Notice that sweet blood taste under the salt? That's a toxic dose of heavy metals. Nobody's survived past three months. It's a beautiful place to die over and over again—"

"—but don't let that beauty seduce you, pioneer. Exposure to Proteus causes a delusion we call Masefield syndrome...the call of the sea. People may tell you it's a gift. Ignore them. You remember what kharaa did to our homes. You *know* we can't trust alien biology.

"We've set up a lab here with a few basic biomods. They're safe. But if you start to hear voices, or if you get the urge to swim to the tree, don't go.

"Habitat is due north. We'll come pick you up. See you soon, pioneer."
ST_CoralGardens_VO.Adaptation_Digestive_Acquired_1: Adapting alien enzymes to your stomach lining. You may now digest freely.
ST_CoralGardens_VO.Blackbox_03_Sophie_07: Chap? Sweetie? Don't be scared. NoA will find a way.
ST_CoralGardens_VO.Adaptation_Digestive_Needed_1: Critical warning. Stomach failure. Expected Zezuran proteins: found unknown alien tissue.
ST_CoralGardens_VO.Active_Biomod_Acquired: Curled feet, tapped fingers or a tensed scalp are common triggers for an active biomod. Be a thoughtful coworker: avoid intrusive grunts.
ST_CoralGardens_VO.WelcomeCenter_WelcomeSpeech_Line8_Sophie: Habitat is due north. We'll come pick you up. See you soon, pioneer.
ST_CoralGardens_VO.Blackbox_Wander_01_Line2: Had a parasite on it...full of virus. Bloom virus. I got a scan. It doesn't match the virus...in our blood.
ST_CoralGardens_VO.WelcomeCenter_WelcomeSpeech_Line3_Nahema: Hey, fellow debt slave. I'm Nahema Nakota, ecologist. Take a sip of seawater. Notice that sweet blood taste under the salt? That's a toxic dose of heavy metals.
ST_CoralGardens_VO.Blackbox_Ruby_01: Hey. Ganzo. You hear that? South—close. Everybody get your flares ready. Keep them away from the kids. [marrowbreach roar] Two! Two of them! Hey! Hüüe, mal aa!—uh oh—  [marrowbreach roar]
ST_CoralGardens_VO.Blackbox_Anita_01_Line1: I can hear you. Sophie says you're a hallucination. Just a virus. But I know you're listening.
ST_CoralGardens_VO.Blackbox_Anita_01: I can hear you. Sophie says you're a hallucination. Just a virus. But I know you're listening. We're so hungry. There is food all around us but it's poison. Will you help? If I feed you, will you feed us? This is my body, given for you. Just help us eat...
ST_CoralGardens_VO.Blackbox_Sophie_02: I'm turning on the nitrogen. Deep breaths. [hiss of gas] You'll have a brand new body. You might even be a little stronger...a little faster. The biobed's got you.
ST_CoralGardens_VO.Blackbox_Sophie_03: If this works, we can slow down the syndromes by reprinting our bodies. Until we find a cure.
ST_CoralGardens_VO.Blackbox_Wander_01_Line3: Legs won’t move. Suit can’t fix it. Trying to tourniquet. Listen! Miss Nahema! Bloom isn't Masefield...Wu's wrong. Bloom isn't Masefield.
ST_CoralGardens_VO.Blackbox_Wander_01_Line1: Miss Nahema...if I get reset...Marrowbreach got me. Should've brought flares. Stupid.
ST_CoralGardens_VO.Blackbox_Wander_01: Miss Nahema...if I get reset...Marrowbreach got me. Should've brought flares. Stupid. Had a parasite on it...full of virus. Bloom virus. I got a scan. It doesn't match the virus...in our blood. Legs won’t move. Suit can’t fix it. Trying to tourniquet. Listen! Miss Nahema! Bloom isn't Masefield...Wu's wrong. Bloom isn't Masefield. Oh, it hurts. ST_CoralGardens_VO.Blackbox_Chap_01: My mouth is full of holes.
ST_CoralGardens_VO.Blackbox_Quaker_01: NoA, is there a fire? NoA, do not open the seacocks. NoA? Acknowledge, please. I have a mousetrap, NoA, and I'll use it. Do not — [flooding sounds]
