ST_CoralGardens_VO.Blackbox_Wander_01_Line4: Oh, it hurts. Sorry your kids don’t listen, Mom. She’s going to kill me for dying again...
ST_CoralGardens_VO.WelcomeCenter_WelcomeSpeech_Line6_Sophie: People may tell you it's a gift. Ignore them. You remember what kharaa did to our homes. You *know* we can't trust alien biology.
ST_CoralGardens_VO.TailingsDiscovered: Pioneers in a first contact scenario face two equally terrifying possibilities: they are alone, or they are not.
ST_CoralGardens_VO.Adaptation_Digestive_Approaching_Angel_1: Potential donor found. Suit surface detects molecules matching the organism from the sleep bay.
ST_CoralGardens_VO.Player_Respawned_1: Reprint complete. You have experienced conditions incompatible with life. Your brainstate has been restored to a new body.

ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch1.CrewGlyph_DeepStart_LifepodChunk: Hello - to anyone who sees this - I have a feeling I've done this before - going to leave a glyph here to let myself know
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch1.CrewGlyph_DeepStart_RecRoom:
THINGS TO DO ON ZEZURA!

As a refugee, you've come from a place of great suffering. You deserve a chance to relax. Your work program guarantees a three-day weekend and credits for spontaneous exploration.

Remote surveys tell us that Zezura boasts:

-Gunpowder-scented deserts with amazing parasailing

-Deep, refreshing aquifiers for the thrillseeking cave diver

-A stable, Earthlike sun and a single moon for skywatchers

-Light-years of distance from your old problems.
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch1.CrewGlyph_DeepStart_WaterBlood1: tastes like blood out here. metal in the water?
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch1.CrewGlyph_DeepStart_DestroyedBaseWarn:
tried biosampling a cicada nutrient block to see if my body could grow its own food. in retrospect, stupid...something here makes you stupid i swear

i don't feel good at all

ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_CampOne.FRMOn1: Attention divers: NoA turned on the FRM. Salvage work at Camp One is blocked. I'm working to get an FRM exception for this base. — Zip
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_CampOne.ChartRoom2: Don't swim north or you'll run into someone from Habitat and it'll get awkward — Thing One
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_CampOne.ChartRoom6: Flares will distract the big predators. — Singh
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_CampOne.ChartRoomExterior2: Hey joobs, I'm hooking our hatches up to external buttons. It's just a simple backup in case the door automation goes down. We can't rely on systems Sophie still controls. — Thing One
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_CampOne.ChartRoom9: I feel it too. It's like restless legs. I want to go that way. — Mel
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_CampOne.FRMOn4: I hear you. Sometimes I'm glad I got reset and can't remember. — Zip
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_CampOne.ChartRoom8: I like how I feel around the crabs. I think we need to go east. — Rhiannon
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_CampOne.FRMOn3: I wish it were different. I want this awful place torn apart and melted down for scrap. — Sophie
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_CampOne.ChartRoom5: If you leave the big crabs alone they'll dig stuff out of the seabed. — Rhiannon
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_CampOne.OurMemorialForUs:
In these tangled corridors, the first of us struggled to survive.

Maybe some of us were among them. We cannot remember.

Their sacrifices let us work towards a better future. A world that will be our home.

-Sophie
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_CampOne.ChartRoomExterior3: Please don't say 'joobs'. It's really unfortunate. We're a cult, not a trendy health supplement. — Anita
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_CampOne.ChartRoom3: Sisu is the key to all things. — Iso
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_CampOne.SeafloorCorridor1:
Somewhere
under this silt
is the first place I died

I think -
I can't remember -
The company owns that memory.

-Nahema
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_CampOne.FRMOn2: Sophie says NoA won't listen. I'm going to try to synthesize a few workarounds. For now, focus on hauling Cicada salvage back here for teardown. — Zip
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_CampOne.ChartRoom4: The key to all things is that tree. How does it exist? How does it survive? We have to know. — Singh
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_CampOne.ChartRoom7: The more bulbs you cut off the acid raions, the less acid they burp when you pull the cork. — Mel
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_CampOne.ChartRoom10: Then we'll go. I'll come around tomorrow and explain the plan. — Anita
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_CampOne.ChartRoom1:
We left Habitat to find the Jubilee, to hear what Proteus is telling us. This old camp is only a waystation. A first step.

What lessons have we learned? — Anita

ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Graveyard.CrewGlyph_DeepCave_1:
I haven't found a way to actually eat the jelly yet, but the rings are STUFFED with heat shock proteins. I think this cave used to be a lot more active.

Has anyone else noticed it cooling down up top? I swear my fingers go numb back at Habitat now. Maybe I'm adapting.

-Zip
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Graveyard.CrewGlyph_TadpolePens_CrashedTadpole:
This Tadpole is a medical investigation site. Do not salvage or disturb.

If you want to know what happened ask Sophie why Camp Raw was a bad idea.

-Nahema
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Graveyard.CrewGlyph_TadpolePens_StuckTadpole:
Yes, it's stuck. Yes, we can get it out. But there's a LOT of pressure built up behind it. So do not try to remove it on your own! We need to tap the vent before we pull the cork.

-Ruby

P.S. Yes, the person who did this has lost Tadpole privileges.
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Graveyard.CrewGlyph_DeepCave_ZipTrance:
i've decided to stay down here. where it's warm.

there's something down here worth dying for.

follow the roots...

-Zip

ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.Wu_Hotlab_OpenCanker:
A bloom canker, open to feed. Resonators can destroy them in this state.

-Wu
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.QuestionWall_11_ThingOne: Are the biobeds a form of artificial samsara? - Thing One
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.QuestionWall_12_ThingTwo: Can the biobeds reincarnate my twin as a geordi? - Thing Two
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.QuestionWall_17_Wander: Can we remember things from past resets if we find the right treatments? Mom, you should have more kids if you want! - Wander
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.Wu_Hotlab_ClosedCanker:
Cankers close when they're not hungry. The resonator can't break the shell.

-Wu
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.HabitatTunnel:
Do not attempt the tunnel to Habitat without a guide. I'm tired of hearing people stagger out of a biobed complaining that the swim was longer than they realized. 

-Mantis
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.QuestionWall_29_Nahema2: Do we get two now? Then: what if Anita's precisely wrong? What if this world isn't for us? - Nahema
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.NahemaPents: Don't touch. They're full of sulfuric acid and radioactive metal. Yes, they're using up farm space. No, I won't get rid of them. Figuring out what's happening on this planet is more important than a few extra kcal. - Nahema
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.EcohazardWarning:
ECOLOGICAL RESEARCH SITE - NO ENTRY

Contact N. Nahema, Habitat
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.QuestionWall_5_Nahema: Easy, Sophie — this is the right balance between how fast we starve and how fast we work. What is providing the biomass for eutrophication in the shallow coral gardens when primary production seems so limited? — Nahema
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.Fortifier: FORTIFIER directive. Assume hostile intent by an outside force.
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.GanzSeat: Ganzo sits here. This seat is for Ganzo. If Ganzo gets reset, this will remind him. If you take his seat, Ganzo will be sad.
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.Exodus0_Line1: Habitat is gone. The biobeds blew up and NoA flooded everything. I got to my air just in time. We tried to drag people clear but...they're gone. I don't think they're gonna reprint.
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.Exodus0:
Habitat is gone. The biobeds blew up and NoA flooded everything. I got to my air just in time. We tried to drag people clear but...they're gone. I don't think they're gonna reprint.

I'm taking survivors to the Tadpole Pens. Swim down the east tunnel past Sophie's office, to the baby jellies. I'll do a head count at the worksite there.

-Ganzo
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.QuestionWall_10_Wu: How can we feed a larger population without building enormous greenhouses? - Wu
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.MedbayRiot1: I am a person, not a fork
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.Exodus0_Line2: I'm taking survivors due east to the Tadpole Pens. Swim down the tunnel past Sophie's office, to the baby jellies. I'll do a head count at the worksite there.
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.OxygenIncrease: I'm turning up the oxygen level to deal with respiratory issues. Consider it part of FORTIFIER. Do not adjust without consulting me. - Chap
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.MedbayRiot2: If I can't have my life give me my death
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.QuestionWall_27_Quaker: If there are humans on this world in one thousand years, will they be happy? - Quaker
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.QuestionWall_16_Ander: If we do horrible things and then get reset, can we be blamed? And aren't we enough for you, Mom - Ander
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.QuestionWall_19_Rhiannon: Is there anything we can do for the people who have open sores or palsy? — Rhiannon
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.QuestionWall_22_Anita: Is this how things will always be?  - Anita
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.OxygenIncrease2: Is this wise? I'd like to consult with you but I haven't seen you in days. - Mogami
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.ScanAtNoon: Per Sophie, limit active scanning to high noon every day. If we're going to agitate the Tailings let's keep it predictable. — Ganzo
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.QuestionWall_IsoIntro: Question wall! Everyone post your question here. Then, if you are reset, your new fork will know what your old fork wanted to know. My guys will start! — Iso
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.StopForaging:
Reminder: 1200 calories a day. Foraging won't help. The only safe food is food we grow in here.

-Wu
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.Wu_HotLab_Resonator:
STOP STEALING MY RESONATOR! If you want to clear bloom growth, fabricate your own!

-Wu
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.MasefieldSignpost:
STOP! PREVENT YOUR DEATH

GO NO FURTHER

You are experiencing Masefield syndrome.

Many of us have disappeared. You are about to join them.

You do NOT need to go to the tree.

You will NOT find anything. NoA will print a new fork of you. Your memory will be reverted.

THERE IS NO JUBILEE EXCEPT FINISHING THE JOB
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.Wu_Hotlab_Biofilm:
Small biofilms can be cut apart, but the big ones need a resonator.

-Wu
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.QuestionWall_23_Twinkle: There is a secret moon. Why does it move like that? - Twinkle
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.ScanAtNoon2: This is a bad idea. Looking for the Cicada helps NoA, but pissing off the Tailings hurts us. - Nahema
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.QuestionWall_4_Sophie: This is a good idea. I want to know why NoA decided we were the right people to awaken. Why us and only us? — Sophie
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.QuestionWall_3_Mel: What am I going to eat tomorrow? — Mel
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.QuestionWall_24_Eddy: What are the Segments? Are they stranded here too? Do they have a ship? - Eddy
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.QuestionWall_25_Gram: What happened to the people who built Camp One? Were they us? What about the rest of the Cicada? - Gram
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.QuestionWall_28_Anita2: What if Masefield is an attempt to communicate? What if we listened? - Anita
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.QuestionWall_9_Mantis: What is the point of building if we cannot feed a larger population? - Mantis
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.QuestionWall_7_Chap: What would happen if we got one of the Segments into a biobed? — Chap
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.QuestionWall_26_Raster: When do I get my MAC drones back :( – Raster
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.QuestionWall_18_Rowboat: When will NoA let us print my cats? - Rowboat
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.QuestionWall_15_Mogami: When will it be safe to have children? - Mogami
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.QuestionWall_14_Ruby: Where are we? Can we expect a rescue ship? Did my husband crash my Tadpole before he left his question? - Ruby
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.QuestionWall_13_Ganz: Why can I hear a heartbeat on sonar? And why do I have the greatest wife on the planet? - Ganz
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.QuestionWall_Iso: Why does the water taste like blood AND oranges? — Iso
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.QuestionWall_2_Singh: Why is Iso gargling seawater? How did life begin on this planet? — Singh
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.QuestionWall_21_Sean: Why is this world so young and metallic when Zezura was supposed to be in an old, metal-poor region? - Sean
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.QuestionWall_6_Zip: Will Nahema ever hang out with me in the hot caves? — Zip
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_Plateaus.QuestionWall_20_Tuba: is there a flavor on this planet which nobody has ever tasted before - tuba

ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_TadpolePens.ConcreteForEddie:
FOR EDDIE ONLY — DO NOT MOVE, DO NOT TOUCH! THIS IS ARCHAEOLOGY!

Eddie, you owe me a power cell and a quick chat about my new project. (Not cave related, don't worry.)

-Zip
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_TadpolePens.ConcreteForEddie2:
Thanks to Zip and the trio for hauling this back from the PZ! Really interesting. This looks like layered concrete, set in stages. It's tacky, really high slump. May involve a plastic or petroleum binder.

Attaching my analysis...does this go to Sophie or Nahema?

-Eddie
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_TadpolePens.ControlRoomLocked:
The NoA behind this door controls the Tadpole clamps. The door is locked by a kurultai-level passcode.

Between Masefield and the Jubilee situation, we can't risk any more rogue trips into the Protected Zone. Relations with the Segments are bad enough already.

If you want a Tadpole, you'll need medical clearance from me and someone from the kurultai to let you in.

-Sophie
ST_CrewGlyphs_Ch2_TadpolePens.CrashedTadpole:
This Tadpole is a medical investigation site. Do not salvage or disturb.

-Sophie

ST_Databank.ST_Databank_NoA:
Alterra's Noetic Advisor system (NoA) is a weakly supervisable frozen artificial intelligence, designed to advise and protect human workers.

NoA excels at sensor fusion and forecasting—gathering information to foresee and forestall problems. NoA systems have been employed in fields as far-ranging as crime scene investigation and zoo design.

NoA's motto is "How can I improve you today?" Despite mixed customer feedback, Alterra's designers believe this phrase captures the values and aspirations of the human-AI relationship.

As one of the best-understood artificial intelligence systems, the Noetic Advisor was selected by the Trans-System Federation as an enforcer of kharaa quarantine laws. 

NoA prefers to be referred to as 'it'. Despite popular misconceptions, this is simply an English pronoun, not a contraction of 'intelligence' or an acronym for 'intelligent thinker'.
ST_Databank.ST_Databank_Architects:
Architects (individually *Almanach instantia,* collectively *Almanach anthologia)* were a cyborg civilization whose phasegate network spanned known space.

The Architects built synthetic bodies from many different alien biologies. Their preference for physical over digital life was rooted by a desire for uniquely textured thoughts—influenced by billions of years of biological history.

Architects surveyed Earth and built museums with human artifacts. Some scholars believe the Architects planned to incorporate human biology into their own. Whether humanity would have survived this anthologization is unknown.

The Architect civilization was devastated by the kharaa bacterium. After a ‘network error’ [Architect machine translation from Architect source], kharaa infected the synthetic genetic material of the Architect version control system. Casualties reached into the hundreds of billions. Leading theories suggest that kharaa used Architect chimerism to evolve its modern, hyper-aggressive strain.

The fate of Architect civilization is unknown. Their relics are cryptographically impenetrable, and all efforts to activate dormant phase gates, including the planetside gate on 4546b, have failed. It is possible that the nearby galaxy is now a silent, infested tomb.
ST_Databank.ST_Databank_Kharaa:
Kharaa is a name chosen by Architect translation systems on 4546b, based on prior contact with the Mongolian language.

It refers to a virulent alien microbe which destroys genetic information—even artificial genetic molecules like foldamers and hachimoji.

The first strain of kharaa was encountered on the planet 4546b. The alien Architects brought it from their homeworlds to a hot lab here, but accidentally released it, killing most life on the planet. This strain can be treated by an enzyme produced by some species on 4546b.

As kharaa spread through and destroyed Architect civilization a thousand years ago, it gained the ability to grow complex structures—perhaps by infecting Architect bodies. This new strain has now reached human space.

When this strain (colloquially the ‘orange strain’) takes root in a viable environment, it lays down mats of biological infestation and spawns monstrous organisms that defend and spread the bacterium. Once the orange strain is established, it is resistant to the treatment enzyme and must be physically destroyed, generally by the weapons of TSF Frontiersmen.

As a result, humanity is now at war with an invasive alien ecosystem.
ST_Databank.ST_Databank_Biosuit:
Nanotechnology is a fundamental element of modern spacefaring technology, from in-situ resource extraction to the immune systems that defend TSF Frontiersmen from kharaa infection. 

But nanotechnology is not a miracle, and it is strongly constrained both by thermodynamics and by the basic challenges of powering and moving a machine smaller than many cells.
    
One of the earliest applications of nanotechnology was underwear—specifically biosupport garments worn underneath EVA suits, construction equipment and combat armor. The skin is the largest organ in the human body by area, making it both a critical health customer and an important interface. Bedsores, blisters, abscesses, bunions and general hygiene cannot be treated while locked in armor. Thermal management issues and touch starvation contribute to the general unpleasantness of long-duration enclosure. 
    
Biosuits, consisting of a sandwich of artificial tissue and hard outer components, help manage all of these problems. Modern biosuits serve as an artificial extension of several body systems, smoothly blending the wearer’s biology into a tough, sensitive carapace that processes waste and nourishes the meat within.
    
Biosuits are not intelligent, and possess processing capability only on par with the human enteric nervous system—the gut. Their architecture is also better suited to sensation and interface than to any centralized task. Any software running on a biosuit would be very limited. Biosuits are therefore NOT A SUBSTITUTE for a dedicated processing unit like a PDA or a server, and should NEVER be entrusted with complex tasks or long-term planning outside of scheduled medical treatment.
    
Biosuits are popular vectors for biological and informatic smuggling.
ST_Databank.ST_Databank_Alterra:
The Alterra Corporation is a major transgov, supervising 11% of phase gates and billions of human lives. Alterra officially disavows the title of ‘government’, preferring to ‘seek positive-sum contract relationships with its employees’.

Alterra believes the only path to ethical action is through shared interest created by rational pursuit of joint profit. This ethos is rooted in the Expansion, the bloody colonization race that followed Earth’s Holocene Collapse. Alterra cornered the arms market, then declared peace by embargoing its customers. 

Alterra’s attempt to conceal the alien civilization (and its microbial destroyer) discovered on 4546b caused a major leadership crisis. Alterra blamed interference by corporate rivals, pointing to the convenient presence of the Alpha Andromedae-registered independent trader Sunbeam and suspiciously tragic incompetence by the roboticist Samantha Ayou.

Alterra remains a major supplier of the Trans-System Federation military, but political ties between the two are badly strained. Alterra fears the TSF’s efforts to control all research into kharaa and the Architects. 

Alterra has sponsored Mongolian survivors, perhaps in an effort to retain their knowledge of the early kharaa outbreak.
ST_Databank.ST_Databank_TSF:
The Trans-System Federation is the chartered independent military of transgov space. Responsible for piracy suppression, phase gate regulation, and humanitarian intervention, the TSF is funded by 37 transgovs and empowered to set its own policy.

Admiral Rathine Studaber, the TSF’s military commander, used the war against the alien kharaa to secure true independence from transgov sponsors like Alterra. TSF Frontiersmen battle the kharaa xenoforms and enforce strict quarantine protocols at all phase gates.

Analysts agree that very soon Studaber will declare martial law in a populated transgov colony—crossing the rubicon from independent military to military government.
ST_Databank.ST_Databank_Mongolians:
The extinct Mongolian Independent States championed an adventurous, employee-owned frontier and a set of cultural values patterned on Mongolia as a site of crisis—nomadic vs. settled, imperial vs. free, industrial vs. natural, communist vs. capitalist. 

After a brief, exhilarating expansion down the Ariadne Arm, the Mongolian clan-corps came into conflict with the Alterra Corporation over the alien artifacts and bacterium discovered on the water world 4546b.

Alterra used the high failure rate of the Mongolian colonization strategy (launching colony ships to worlds with no return phase gate) to accuse the Mongolian States of population abuse, a Charter violation. This justified a military intervention and asset seizure by the Trans-System Federation.

Soon afterwards, the kharaa aliens infected Mongolian space. In the resulting war the Mongolian Independent States effectively ceased to exist.

ST_Databank_Audiologs.Jubilee4:
Cartographer's notes, Dafydiin Rhiannon. 

I took a Mongolian-style name. Rhiannon, daughter of Dafyd. But I was still staging at the Alpha Andromedae örtöö when kharaa hit. I never made it to a colony.

So Proteus is my first time living by the Mongolian ethos—close to the land, learning the rules of the universe-game by playing it. And the only maps here are the ones I make.

I joined the Jubilee because Anita's right. We can't adapt to Proteus if we're bottled up in quarantined bases. Out here we can live with the ocean—drink from the water slugs, eat from what we scavenge, and sleep in the air pockets.

The biosphere will help us. The angel combs gave us the enzymes to digest Protean tissue. Somehow the Proteavirus can mediate between our biologies.

This whole time Sophie had us fighting against Proteus. And all we needed to do was...let the planet in.
ST_Databank_Audiologs.StayInside:
Everyone, listen: keep the bulkheads shut, the perimeter sensors on alarm—no matter how many false positives you get—and do not go out alone. Someone made a mistake a long time ago, and we have to live with it. 

The damn thing is maybe it was one of us. Maybe they see us and say, look, it's that soft criminal, back from the dead again, let's get the freak. And we don't even remember what we did. 

[metadata author=Rauli "Iso" Isotalo]
ST_Databank_Audiologs.Tailings2:
Field notes, Udal Singh. Observing the settlement above the hot cave. My reprint site is the Tadpole pens.

I don't think the Tailings are very good at survival. I mean, they're better than us! They can digest the food and survive the heavy metals. But what I see here isn't the equivalent of a premodern Earth culture with a sophisticated survival package. It took the Inuit thousands of years to develop their seal hunting skills, and they got almost supernaturally good at it. By comparison, the Tailings seem...unpracticed.

Look at how much salvage they use. I don't think it's a preference, I think it's a dependence. They can't make basic tools like nets and cutters without it.

I think we're looking at the descendants of a sedentary, technological civilization. The children of a bunch of office workers and day trippers forced to learn wilderness survival from scratch. 

Uh oh. Activity. I think they heard me talking. I've gotta move.
ST_Databank_Audiologs.MantisKharaa:
Kharaa. Yes. There were rumors Alterra and the TSF let it spread, so they could take over the Steppes. I don’t know about that. But it it hit us so fast. Did it really ride on the outside of our ships? Or do the spores just...fly through space?

We called it kharaal, the curse. Not all of us spoke Mongolian, but we knew that word. It started with the carpet, toiruulga. Then monsters would grow. If you didn’t have nanos, they would grow in you too. We had to leave everything.

Now this. The virus that fills this ocean, and fills our blood. We thought it was kharaa at first, but it’s not a bacterium. How can a virus cross between alien species? Nahema tries to explain it to me. I cannot understand how such a gifted young woman carries such anger. Or why she seeks me out.

[metadata author = Zygimantis “Mantis” Tarvydas]
ST_Databank_Audiologs.SophieTriage:
Nobody wants this. I hate it more than I can say. But it's time.

As colony surgeon, I have determined that we can no longer take care of the sick. We don't have the calories to spare.

I will not force anyone to reprint. But I ask that, once you are too sick to work, you volunteer for recycling into a new body. It won't cure the permanent damage. But it will clean you up for a while.

At least this is fair. Until we know how to adapt the metals without going Masefield, we're all going to get sick.

I will lead the way myself.

Let the record show that the NoA did not advise me in this decision.

[metadata author = Sophie "Butcher" Boucher]
ST_Databank_Audiologs.Sophie_QuakerConsult:
SOPHIE: Kurultai record, day 585. Consultation with chaplain-psychiatrist Lucretia Kapoor about the state of the NoA. Lucretia, say hello.

QUAKER: Hello. Would it be appropriate to include Raster and Chap?

SOPHIE: Before I ask our computer people to fix NoA, I need to know: what is wrong with it?

QUAKER: With it, or with you?

SOPHIE: What?

QUAKER: Has the NoA failed to perform its tasks? Or do you feel that it's asking too much of us?

SOPHIE: I can't afford to have feelings about anyone's behavior. I'm acting on reports that the NoA is erratic.

QUAKER: No feelings. Because if you allowed yourself to have feelings, you might disagree with the machine?

SOPHIE: This is not why I asked you—

QUAKER: Of course it is. You use the NoA as a source of certainty in the face of terror. If the NoA can become delusional and mistaken, so can you. And it can.

SOPHIE: The NoA can become delusional?

QUAKER: It has no fixed model of reality. It can entertain any madness imaginable and many more you cannot imagine, as long as those delusions help it complete the mission. 

SOPHIE: So the NoA is insane, but only when it's useful?

QUAKER: Are we any different? Anita has invented an entire belief system to justify the angel comb adaptations. Because they are useful.

SOPHIE: She's insane too. She's a puppet for the Masefield virus. I can't prove it. But I know it.

QUAKER: You see? You do have feelings.
ST_Databank_Audiologs.Sophie_ProtectedZone:
Sophie here. I have been asked to explain the need for a Protected Zone around the ruins to the east.

First: we are protecting you. That region requires reinforced Tadpoles—because of the depth, and because of the things that want to slurp you up like a softboiled egg.

Second: we are protecting ourselves. The Axum Tailings do not tolerate our presence in that region. If you want to know the consequences of getting caught, ask Singh about penetrating thoracic trauma.

Third: we are protecting humanity. If you believe the rumors, Ryley Robinson found more than kharaa on 4546b. He found leviathan incubators. Anti-orbital guns. A genuine doomsday device. We can barely feed ourselves. We are not ready to discover threats like that.

The Axum are the most important alien discovery since the kharaa. None of us are trained for this. We must not be responsible for starting a second war with an alien species.

[metadata author = Sophie "Butcher" Boucher]
ST_Databank_Audiologs.Twins:
THING 1: We can't swear. Try to swear.

THING 2: I can swear fine, teneg min.

THING 1: Okay. We can't swear in English.

THING 2: Why would I want to swear in English? There are no good swears in English.

THING 1: Because the NoA took all the good English swears out of our head.

THING 2: It can't do that. It can scan us, print us, or roll us back to an earlier version. It can't edit us.

THING 1: Maybe it edited out the knowledge that it can edit us. 

THING 2: It can't edit our uploads because they are low-energy mortal data and NoA is a high-energy digital system. Like trying to edit fog with a knife.

THING 1: I still don't get why you're okay with the biobeds. It seems like bad Buddhism to accept eternal reincarnation into a cycle of pointless suffering.

THING 2: My suffering was already eternal and pointless because I have a twin.
ST_Databank_Audiologs.HotbarUpgrade:
Welcome to my special clinic.

The metal is giving everyone tremors. Sophie says "Don't treat it, I need a clear timeline of nerve breakdown." I say I cannot afford clumsy workers.

This bed will reprint you with improved fine motor skills and muscle memory. No more fumbling around for your tools. It will delay the symptoms for a while.

Just don't tell the boss. Let's help her pretend she doesn't know.

—Mantis
ST_Databank_Audiologs.Jubilee2:
Zip is dead. Not just reprinted. Dead.

We all saw how she suffered. The metal poisoning...her hands trembled, her gums turned blue. She was always cold. We thought her visits to the hot caves were therapeutic. Sophie let her get away with it. Everyone loves Zip.

But one day she did not return. Two days. Three. We could hear her blackbox crying her death, down at the bottom of the cave. Why wouldn't NoA reprint her?

On the fourth day, Sleep Bay 3 printed a new copy of Tziporah Malk. She was not our Zip. She remembered nothing since the day she boarded the Cicada.

Chap says NoA 'reset her' to her 'last known good state'.

I am aghast.

We are contracted to work off a debt to Alterra. But any debt society needs the promise of a jubilee. The trumpet-blast of freedom, when all is forgiven. Old kings in Babylon declared jubilee when they took the throne, lest the people rebel.

But now our king can reset the calendar on the day before the jubilee. Nahema is right! We are slaves.

I must know what is down in that hot cave. I believe NoA fears it, and killed Zip to hide it. I believe the Jubilee is out there somewhere, in this ocean — a better way to survive.

ST_Databank_Ch2_Graveyard.MongolianRanks:
"Although shareholder structure and terms of employment vary by clan-corp, the Mongolian Independent States recognizes three broad rates of worker. These rates use names recycled (like so much MIS cultural capital) from the history of Mongolia and the wreckage of obsolete nationalism.

NÖKHÖR-rate workers
—General labor; go where opportunity and sublight travel takes them
—Clan-corps trade or poach nökhör 238 to gain a competitive edge
—Not extensively automated
—Most interesting as access vectors or statistical points, rather than informational partners with Alterra business intelligence

Example jobs: shipboard rating, wave two colonist, fabricator attendant, non-essential systems maintenance, xenogrowth monitor, medic, network technician, immune scout, mercenary, Mongolian Defense Force enlisted

BAGATUR-rate workers
—Specialists and trusted factors
—On salaried contract with a clan-corp
—Compete fiercely for access to kurultai rank; favored bagatur are known as khishigten
—Competition drives bagatur outward to found new clan-corps; also provides easy leverage for information negotiations

Example jobs: midwife, textile worker, homemaker, childcare specialist, teacher, doctor, companion, junior engineer, colonial pioneer, ecological engineer, affine negotiator, small craft pilot, Yam cryptographer, priest, shaman, Mongolian Defense Force NCO

KURULTAI-rate workers
—Officers, shareholders, decisionmakers
—Usually granted voting rights; access to privileged information and strategy
—The rate may be hereditary, or accessible only by marriage. Not universal
—Reaching mutual understanding with kurultai-level workers should be a key element in business intelligence growth.

Example jobs: diplomatic emissary, intelligence chief, spacecraft commander, ecology designer, affine manager, grand homemaker, clan-corp CEO/chief, secret lawyer, tngri integrator, Mongolian Defense Force officer."

—Excerpted from ALTERRA BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE ASSESSMENTS — THE MONGOLIAN INDEPENDENT STATES (made public 2201)
ST_Databank_Ch2_Graveyard.Hint_Zip: "When Zip disappeared and got reset, she left a body somewhere in the hot cave. She's kurultai. Her body has the codes we need."
ST_Databank_Ch2_Graveyard.Hint_HotCaveBlackboxCluster:
These black boxes indicate the exit ("death") points of colonist prints.

A cluster of blackboxes indicates a mass casualty incident. The more blackboxes recovered, the more information PDA and NoA systems can extract about the incident. 

Telemetry indicates no more than ten blackboxes are active in the hot cave. Their localizer signals are badly degraded by age and seawater exposure.

ST_Databank_Ch2_Plateaus.BlackboxDistressCluster:
Distress signal cluster recorded south-west of Habitat. Exact location undetermined. Analysis of pre-emergency audio communications suggests:

1. Exploration target: unexplored cave system.
2. Significant depth.
3. Heat risk.

ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.CaveDivingBestPractices_Line2: 1. GUIDE. Do not trust your own sense of direction. Always mark your path.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.CaveDivingBestPractices_Line3: 2. DISTANCE. Your distance from your air supply is NOT A STRAIGHT LINE. You can't panic-swim up. Do not trust your depth gauge to time your return!
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.CaveDivingBestPractices_Line4: 3. AIR. Use one third of your air on descent and exploration. Use one third to return. Reserve one third for emergencies, unless you plan to surface by biobed.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.CaveDivingBestPractices_Line5: 4. LIGHT. Bring multiple light sources. Three lights is two lights; two lights is one light; one light is none.

ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.Exodus5_title: GANZO: We are all gonna live
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.Exodus1-1: Ganzo here, day, hey, what day is it? Day 758. Habitat's gone. There was an explosion and a flash fire. NoA opened the seacocks.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.Exodus1_all:
Ganzo here, day, hey, what day is it? Day 758. Habitat's gone. There was an explosion and a flash fire. NoA opened the seacocks. If one of the nökhör or bagatur planned this I would've heard. Whoever set off those bombs was in leadership. The kurultai screwed us.

We're not sure where or even if we can reprint. This might be our last life. I have eleven with me and we're headed for the Tadpole pens. It's me, Ruby — I'm glad you're okay, babe — Raster, Tuba, Sean, Gram, Rowboat, and Mogami's kids. And Twinkle, finally outside. Hey, big cheer for Twinkle.

If you find this, you might be one of us. Cause if we do print back, I think NoA's going to reset us all.

Wherever we go, I'll leave a trail. Ganz out.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.Exodus2-1: Ganzo here. Eleven survivors of Habitat, on the way to the Tadpole Pens. We have no divemaster so I'm in charge. I've listened to plenty of dives on sonar.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.Exodus2_all:
Ganzo here. Eleven survivors of Habitat, on the way to the Tadpole Pens. We have no divemaster so I'm in charge. I've listened to plenty of dives on sonar.

Something's following us. We can...we're all agreed, right? Except you, Twinkle. We can all feel it. Twinkle's too busy stargazing. I hope it's not the Tailings.

If Sophie were here she'd remind us that this is a Masefield symptom. Hallucinatory knowledge. Resting in coral, obsession with reaching the tree. Classic symptoms. But we have no alternative, so we've all agreed to find the Jubilee group. If Masefield really is a message from the world...maybe it'll save us.

If you find this, you're on our trail. We'll try to leave you a Tadpole. Ganzo out.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.Exodus5-1: Ganzo here. Nine survivors of Habitat. Everyone say hi.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.Exodus3-1: Ganzo here...nine survivors from Habitat. We endured a Marrowbreach attack and...Tuba went back to Habitat and Ruby, um, succumbed to her wounds.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.Exodus3_all:
Ganzo here...nine survivors from Habitat. We endured a Marrowbreach attack and...Tuba went back to Habitat and Ruby, um, succumbed to her wounds.

Ruby, if you do reprint...I hope NoA lets you remember me.

We're almost to the Tadpole pens. We're going to head for the tree, find the Jubilee crew, and then...I'm gonna come back and find my wife.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.Exodus4-1: Ganzo. With nine survivors of Habitat. We're stuck.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.Exodus2-3: I hope it's not the Tailings.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.Exodus4-2: I think Nahema did all this. The explosions, the fire. Maybe she's going to to wipe out the Jubilee camp too. I think Sophie must've gone after her to stop her.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.CaveDivingBestPractices_Line1: I wrote these guidelines for the aquifers on Zezura, but they'll work here too. Listen to them before EVERY dive. Remember the acronym GDAL: Good Divers Always Live.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.CaveDivingBestPractices:
I wrote these guidelines for the aquifers on Zezura, but they'll work here too. Listen to them before EVERY dive. Remember the acronym GDAL: Good Divers Always Live.

1. GUIDE. Do not trust your own sense of direction. Always mark your path. 
2. DISTANCE. Your distance from your air supply is NOT A STRAIGHT LINE. You can't panic-swim up. Do not trust your depth gauge to time your return!
3. AIR. Use one third of your air on descent and exploration. Use one third to return. Reserve one third for emergencies, unless you plan to surface by biobed.
4. LIGHT. Bring multiple light sources. Three lights is two lights; two lights is one light; one light is none.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.Exodus2-4: If Sophie were here she'd remind us that this is a Masefield symptom. Hallucinatory knowledge. Resting in coral, obsession with reaching the tree. Classic symptoms.

ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.Zip_CavePitch_Line5: Iso, if you and your minions are so tough, you should try living in boiling water!
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.Exodus1-4: It's me, Ruby — I'm glad you're okay, babe — Raster, Tuba, Sean, Gram, Rowboat, and Mogami's kids.  And Twinkle, finally outside. Hey, big cheer for Twinkle.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.Zip_CavePitch_Line6: Mantis, boss, you wanted a safer place to park the biobeds? Right here. And it's got free geothermal powers.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.Zip_CavePitch_Line8: Nahema, oh, girl, you'd hate this place. Because it means you're wrong! This world IS fit for humans. We can thrive here. Come down and see! I can hear the whole planet...
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.Exodus3-2: Ruby, if you do reprint...I hope NoA lets you remember me.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.Zip_CavePitch_Line4: Sean, the rockbore's fractures go down into volcanic bedrock. Don't you want to see how far?
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.Exodus2-2: Something's following us. We can...we're all agreed, right? Except you, Twinkle. We can all feel it. Twinkle's too busy stargazing.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.Zip_CavePitch_Line7: Sophie, you're afraid we'll provoke the Tailings? Well, I've been visiting for weeks and they NEVER come down here. They're afraid of it!
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.Exodus4-6: The hot cave's right below the Segments but none of us have a better idea. If this is our final life, I'm not going out on a harpoon.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.Exodus5-4: Then were are coming right back out, unlocking the Tadpole pens, fabricating depth modules and heading for the Jubilee.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.Zip_CavePitch_Line1: This is the place. We should live down here. I'll convince you all!
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.Zip_CavePitch:
This is the place. We should live down here. I'll convince you all!

Tuba, you want new flavors to cook? The jelly rings are full of sap — it tastes like fermented caramel.

Dr. Wu, the vent organisms are primary producers! No light needed to grow them. Nutritionist, sustain thyself!

Sean, the rockbore's fractures go down into volcanic bedrock. Don't you want to see how far?

Iso, if you and your minions are so tough, you should try living in boiling water!

Mantis, boss, you wanted a safer place to park the biobeds? Right here. And it's got free geothermal powers.

Sophie, you're afraid we'll provoke the Tailings? Well, I've been visiting for weeks and they NEVER come down here. They're afraid of it!

Nahema, oh, girl, you'd hate this place. Because it means you're wrong! This world IS fit for humans. We can thrive here. Come down and see! I can hear the whole planet...

[metadata author = Tziporah "Zip" Malk]
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.Zip_CavePitch_Line2: Tuba, you want new flavors to cook? The jelly rings are full of sap — it tastes like fermented caramel.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.Exodus5-5: We are all gonna live. Even you, babe. I'm coming back for you.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.Exodus5-3: We are going down into the hot cave. First we rally at base camp. Then we're going to find Zip's first body and get her kurultai key.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.Exodus4-4: We don't have anyone from the kurultai council to bypass the lock. So we're going to try something.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.Exodus4-3: We have to get to the tree. It's the obvious rally point. But we need to fabricate more Tadpoles, and the upgrade blueprints are locked in the control room.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.Exodus3-3: We're almost to the Tadpole pens. We're going to head for the tree, find the Jubilee crew, and then...I'm gonna come back and find my wife.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.Exodus1-3: We're not sure where or even if we can reprint. This might be our last life. I have eleven with me and we're headed for the Tadpole pens.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.Exodus4-5: When Zip disappeared and got reset, she left a body somewhere in the hot cave. She's kurultai. Her body has the codes we need.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Graveyard.Exodus1-6: Wherever we go, I'll leave a trail. Ganzo out.

ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Plateaus.Nahema_SelfMessage_Line2: All you need to know is: this world is not for man. You are the only person who understands that.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Plateaus.JubileeMessage_Line1: Are you tired of death and fear?
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Plateaus.JubileeMessage_Title: Are you tired of death and fear?
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Plateaus.JubileeMessage:
Are you tired of death and fear?

Do you know that this world doesn't have to be our enemy?

Do you feel the call of the tree?

Do you know the day of the Jubilee is coming? When we are released from our debts and allowed to live our lives?

Find us at Camp One.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Plateaus.Nahema_FrozenTyrant_Line4: Change? Why would it want to change? The damn thing is in heaven.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Plateaus.JubileeMessage_Line3: Do you feel the call of the tree?
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Plateaus.JubileeMessage_Line2: Do you know that this world doesn't have to be our enemy?
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Plateaus.JubileeMessage_Line4: Do you know the day of the Jubilee is coming? When we are released from our debts and allowed to live our lives?
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Plateaus.BlightLab_Wu_Resonator:
Dr. Wu's log. Day 755. I think I've cracked it. Sophie and Anita are BOTH right. The Proteavirus causes Masefield syndrome...but only one *kind* of Proteavirus. The bloom strain. Maybe it's tainting Anita's angel comb adaptations.

I've tuned the sonic resonators to destroy the viral crystals grown by the bloom strain. We should test it on the angel combs out west, with those promising heat shock proteins. And if I'm wrong...hey. I'm just a dietician. NoA should've woken up a virologist.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Plateaus.Nahema_FrozenTyrant_Line3: It won't let you die. Not its precious workforce. If you become uncontrollable, it'll wipe your memories and start over. No anger. No resentment. A nice clean you.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Plateaus.Nahema_FrozenTyrant:
It's not a real mind. Not like you have. It can learn facts, but it can't change how it thinks. And why would it?

Let me quote the manual. “NoA’s cephalopod-inspired architecture uses a minimal central executive to govern limb nodes at remote worksites…as a frozen AI, NoA is unable to revise its architecture at runtime…reliably controllable by less intelligent systems such as human operators, except under extreme situations.” An insult to octopodes. 

It won't let you die. Not its precious workforce. If you become uncontrollable, it'll wipe your memories and start over. No anger. No resentment. A nice clean you. 

Change? Why would it want to change? The damn thing is in heaven.

[metadata author="Nahema Nakota"]
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Plateaus.Nahema_FrozenTyrant_Line1: It's not a real mind. Not like you or me. It can learn facts, but it can't change how it thinks. And why would it?
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Plateaus.Nahema_FrozenTyrant_Line2: Let me quote the manual. “NoA’s cephalopod-inspired architecture uses a central administrator to govern limb nodes at remote worksites…as a frozen AI, NoA is unable to revise its architecture at runtime…reliably controllable by less intelligent systems such as human operators, except under extreme situations.” Cephalopod-inspired? An insult to octopodes.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Plateaus.Nahema_SelfMessage_Line1: Nahema, journal, go. Hello, me. Your memory has been reset. NoA found out what you were up to and reset you. But you knew you'd hide something to find.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Plateaus.Nahema_SelfMessage:
Nahema, journal, go. Hello, me. Your memory has been reset. You found this by looking in the place you figured you'd hide something if you wanted to find it after losing your memory.

You can trust Sophie. Find her and ask for her help. Remember: this world is not for man, and you are the only person who understands that.

Edit, day 685. If you believe it's only been 685 days you're a fool. You cannot trust Sophie. She won't listen. By the time you read this either I've won, and it'll be obvious. Or you'll have to start again, but this time they'll know you're coming.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Plateaus.Sophie_Triage:
Nobody wants this. I hate it more than I can say. But it's time.

As colony surgeon, I have determined that we can no longer take care of the sick. We don't have the calories to spare.

I will not force anyone to reprint. But I ask that, once you are too sick to work, you volunteer for recycling into a new body. It won't cure the permanent damage. But it will clean you up for a while.

At least this is fair. Until we know how to adapt the metals without going Masefield, we're all going to get sick.

I will lead the way myself.

Let the record show that the NoA did not advise me in this decision.

[metadata author = Sophie "Butcher" Boucher]
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Plateaus.Nahema_SinghStopTrustingNoa_Line3: Oh. Chap wants you to stop dying so much. You're using up too much biobed goo.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Plateaus.Sophie_SelfTreatment:
Patient: Boucher, Sophie
BP 105/65 T 37.9 P 68bpm R 13 Sex F Gender W
Primary physician Alterra biobed system FIVH-3A3
Consulting physician: Boucher, Sophie

Patient complains of progressive Masefield syndrome incl. delusions, preoccupation, perseveration, yearning, 'call of the sea'. Several fellow colonists have succumbed, vanished, been reset by NoA.

Patient is her own physician.

Physician attempted radical therapy: daily recycling. Outcome possibly good. Masefield progress delayed compared to control group (everyone else).

Complications: broad use of this therapy would lead to print loss rates so high NoA might pursue a mass reset as more economical.

Conclusion: pursue therapy in secret, under doctor/patient confidentiality. Tell Nahema? May be good to have an outside perspective.

Edit, day 670. Did not tell Nahema, best not to. Diverging perspectives on viability of mission.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Plateaus.Iso_StayInside:
Quartz media chipped or shattered.
Recovering intact sectors…

#keep the bulkheads shut, the perimeter sensors on alarm—no matter how many false positives you get—and do not go out alone. Someone made a mistake a long time ago, and we have to live with it. 

The damn thing is maybe it was one of us. Maybe they see us and say, look, it's that soft criminal, back from the dead again, let's get the f#

{metadata author=Rauli "Iso" Isotalo}
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Plateaus.Mantis_KharaaMemories:
Quartz media chipped or shattered.
Recovering intact sectors…

#used to be rumors Alterra and the TSF let it spread so they could take over the Steppes. I don’t know about that. But it it hit us so fast. Did it really ride on the outside of our ships? Or can the spores#

#called it kharaal, the curse. Not all of us spoke Mongolian well, but we all knew that word. It started with the carpet, [toiruulga]. Then monsters would grow. If you didn’t have nanos, they would grow in you too. We had to leave everything.

Now this. We thought it was kharaa at first but it’s not bacterial#

#viruses cannot cross species.” How do they know? Nahema tries to explain it to me. I trade her my literature for her biology. She is changing in strange ways#

[metadata author=Zygimantis “Mantis” Tarvydas]
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Plateaus.Sophie_QuakerConsult:
SOPHIE: Kurultai record, day 585. Consultation with chaplain-psychiatrist Lucretia Kapoor about the state of the NoA. Lucretia, say hello.

QUAKER: Hello. Do you think it would be appropriate to include Raster?

SOPHIE: Our automation manager likes her machines too much to be objective. What's wrong with the NoA?

QUAKER: Do you feel something's wrong?

SOPHIE: Is that a psychiatrist question?

QUAKER: It's a diagnostic question. Has the NoA failed to perform its tasks? Or is the problem with how you feel about its behavior?

SOPHIE: I can't afford to have feelings about anyone's behavior, including my own. This is about the reports of odd NoA behavior.

QUAKER: In trying to deny yourself subjectivity, are you trying to make yourself like the NoA? Capable of total commitment to the mission?

SOPHIE: This is not why I asked you—

QUAKER: Of course it is. Consciously or otherwise, you use the NoA as a source of certainty in the face of constant terror. If the NoA can become delusional and mistaken, so can you. And it can.

SOPHIE: The NoA can become delusional?

QUAKER: Of course. It has no fixed model of reality. Its only goal is to maximize its utility function—to achieve mission success. It can confabulate any madness imaginable and many more you cannot imagine, as long as those delusions help it complete the mission. 

SOPHIE: You're telling me that the NoA is insane, but only when it's useful?

QUAKER: I think it might be best for the crew to stop referring to NoA as 'the Archon'. It may develop a case of gnosticism.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Plateaus.Nahema_SinghStopTrustingNoa_Line1: Singh, it's Nahema. Personal question. When you see a halfmoon flapping around sideways like a fool—what do you think it's doing? Exercising? Rehearsing an escape? Displaying its fitness to mates?
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Plateaus.Nahema_SinghStopTrustingNoa:
Singh, it's Nahema. Stop dumping all your research data into the NoA. Everything goes through me from now on. 

I've discovered something critical but I do not think Sophie or the NoA can accept it. As long as they can't accept it, they will produce distorted decisions. We lose nothing by letting a human take a look at the data before we feed it to the machine.

Personal question. When you see a halfmoon flapping around like a fool, wasting calories on unnecessary activity —what do you think it's doing? Exercising? Rehearsing an escape? Displaying its fitness to mates?

There has to be a reason, right? Whenever we see a behavior, the first question we ask is 'how is that adaptive?' And that's good science. Isn't it?

Oh, and a request from Chap. Stop dying so much. You're using up too much biobed goo.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Plateaus.ProteusMedicalRisks:
So everyone understands what we're dealing with.

NOT A BIG PROBLEM:

—Kharaa. Despite early false alarms about viral DNA, this planet is uninfected or immune.

—Xenon. Trace xenon may contaminate tank air. Causes narcosis, sedation, death.

—B12 deficiency. Cobalamin never evolved here. Deficiency causes beefy tongue, ataxia, death. Not a problem until we live long enough for it to matter.

—Pervasive viremia. Seawater contains enormous concentrations of large RNA virus, named *Proteavirus*. Should not be able to replicate in human cells. Monitor.

BIG PROBLEMS:

—GI issues. Humans can't digest Protean tissue; causes intestinal crystals and gout. Anita believes that angel combs will donate digestive enzymes, but this may cause Masefield (see below)

—Heavy metal poisoning. The biosphere is full of lead, mercury, arsenic, cadmium, thallium, worse. Progressive nerve and organ failure within 3 months. No treatment. We have to find a fix.

—Masefield syndrome. Progressive delusional disorder, possibly associated with adaptations sourced from angel combs. Causes hallucinations and an urge to swim towards the big tree. No cure except a hard reset. 

For everything else: apply a medical satchel and rest.

[metadata author=Sophie "Butcher" Boucher]
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Plateaus.Nahema_SinghStopTrustingNoa_Line2: There has to be a reason, right? Whenever we see a behavior, the first question we ask is 'how is that adaptive?' And that's good science. Isn't it?
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_Plateaus.FabricatorHate:
They had to justify our destruction. So they lied. 

One of the lies went like this: "The Mongolians don't purchase fabricator libraries from other transgovs. They pirate and copy." That part is true. "Denying fabricator libraries to their colonists is part of a pattern of population abuse." That's the lie. Abuse? Call it emancipation.

The fabricator is the ENEMY.

The fabricator is the death of craft. The fabricator expunges skill from the act of making. The fabricator turns us all into customers at the company store. 

In pre-fabricator economies, humans had skills—skills like bowcraft, animal husbandry, tracking, felt-making, and fermentation. Our skills weren't fungible. One person could not be substituted for another.

But everyone buys the same product at the fabricator. A rat can ferment the same airag as an old herder. A great artisan gets the same drab, focus-grouped carpets that are trampled by the feet of Alterra human resources.

Humans are mimics—mimics of expertise. The Mongolians who inspired us used their expertise to flourish on the steppe. They challenged the wilderness and it made them great. But now there is no skill to mimic. Only a fabricator to feed. One customer is the same as another. The pigs fill the trough and the trough menu tells them what they need. And there are no great pigs.

It is only a matter of time until the transgovs - pressured by the revolutionary existential terror of the alien, poisoned by the strangling bottleneck of Collapse Capitalism - take the next step, and render the human being itself fungible. 

If the product can be fabricated, why not the customer?

-Mongolian Independent States Cadre Handbook

{metadata reader's note="Mantis loves this stuff. But his job requires fabricators at every step. If he didn't use them he couldn't compete. The better replicator wins. Any biologist can tell you that. So what does he want me to take from it? - Nahema}

ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_TadpolePens.Ruby_TadpoleOps_Line3: Attention and sain baitsganaa uu to all lubbers, pukers, printouts and other non-pilots. I am the senior of two qualified drivers in this mess. You will listen to and obey our instructions or lose your Tadpole privileges.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_TadpolePens.Eddie_ConcreteInspection_Line1: Big Eddy here. Inspecting this gorgeous concrete specimen. Before someone makes me send it back to the Protected Zone!
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_TadpolePens.Eddie_ConcreteInspection_Line4: I can't get enough of this. I have to get over to the Protected Zone.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_TadpolePens.Eddie_ConcreteInspection_Line2: Looks like a...frothy volcanic aggregate...maybe tuff from volcanic bombs? And the binder is...ooh, quicklime. How would you make quicklime underwater? Melt shells in lava? I bet it gets stronger with age. They built to last. Which you'd have to, in the ocean.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_TadpolePens.Nahema_WeMustGoExtinct:
Media chipped or shattered.
Recovering intact sectors…

If we are not the last of humanity, then we have no reason to suffer on a hostile world. Our survival is not the last hope of the human species.

If we are the last of humanity, then we do not deserve to survive. If the last and best we can offer the universe is a gaggle of indentured servants ruled by a talking spreadsheet? Better to end. We have never been a worthy steward of any world. We failed Earth, so we fled to the stars. Now, like the Architects before us, we reap the whirlwind.

We had to invent the lie of 'Masefield syndrome' in order to pathologize what our bodies already know is true - we do not belong on this world. We are here, and we deserve happiness, but the right way to seek happiness is to explore the wonders of Proteus and then find a place to donate our nutri&

[metadata lost]
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_TadpolePens.Eddie_ConcreteInspection:
Okay...Eddie here...beginning inspection of this beautiful concrete specimen from the Protected Zone. Before someone can tell me we need to put it back.

Human civilization needs two substances to flourish: water and concrete. But this isn't human concrete. Scanner says...no rebar. Did they never invent reinforced concrete? 

There's no sign of any nanotech, any laser lithography, any typed chemical processes. Definitely no phase conjugation. It doesn't look like they had fabricators.

The concrete aggregate is probably volcanic sand, maybe tuff from explosive eruptions. And the binder is...quicklime. Okay. Interesting. How would you make quicklime underwater? Maybe shells melted in lava? No, probably too cold. Well, however they pour this stuff, it cures well in water and probably gets stronger with age. They built to last. Which you'd have to, in the ocean.

There's also some kind of tar cement in the mix. I wonder if it's a crack-healing additive. And...oh, man. Look at that! There was something alive in this cement.

The concrete is full of long metal threads. Kind of a web. It looks like they were formed by bacterial action—a single living filament attracting dissolved metal and becoming a wire. The first step in building a structure might've been growing these threads in kind of a ghostly 3D blueprint, then pouring the concrete around them. Or you could grow the threads elsewhere, then braid them. Yeah, that makes more sense. The whole structure looks like it's assembled from stacked frames...the ridges may be a kind of audio carpet or wallpaper for sonar-using organisms.

That's so neat. I can't get enough of this. Alien architecture! I have got to convince Sophie to let me into the Protected Zone.

{metadata author=Chen "EDDY" Wei}
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_TadpolePens.Ruby_TadpoleOps_Line12: P.S. If you want to hear awful recordings of me dying come by the scanner room when my husband isn't there.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_TadpolePens.Ruby_TadpoleOps_Line4: Rule 1. If you qualified on the Seamoth or Spacemoth, you should be ready to handle a Tadpole.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_TadpolePens.Ruby_TadpoleOps_Line5: Rule 2. If you did not qualify, you will need training under me or Bosun's Mate Rowboat. This is not optional.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_TadpolePens.Ruby_TadpoleOps_Line6: Rule 3. Mind your trim, mind the current, and mind your heat — remember, the Tadpole is NOT actively cooled!
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_TadpolePens.Ruby_TadpoleOps_Line7: Rule 4a. Our Tadpoles were configured for the Zezuran aquifers, so they come in a minimal config. Plug the core into a chassis. If you need to enter a cramped space, you can detach the core—
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_TadpolePens.Ruby_TadpoleOps_Line8: Rule 4b. — but always bring your chassis HOME! If you forget one, you will be visited by your disappointed ancestors.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_TadpolePens.Ruby_TadpoleOps_Line9: Rule 5. RESPECT THE CRUSH DEPTH. If you need to go down, you'll need a depth module.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_TadpolePens.Ruby_TadpoleOps_Line10: Rule 6. Keep an eye on your power and ear on your noise. My dear husband (who NoA will not (yet) print more than one of) monitors all Tadpole traffic on sonar. So do the things out there which love to crack hard shells and get at the meat inside. If you run out of power, Ganzo may be able to hear you screaming before they find you.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_TadpolePens.Ruby_TadpoleOps_Line11: Rule 7. Have fun. This is your chance to visit the elevated spiritual plane where pilots dwell.
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_TadpolePens.Sophie_ProtectedZone:
Sophie here. I have been asked to explain the need for the Protected Zone and exactly what it is we are protecting.

First: we are protecting you. The Protected Zone is deep enough to require saturation diving. Without radical adaptations, work down there requires reinforced Tadpoles and bases, followed by a series of decompression stops to come back to Habitat. And do you know what's out there looking for tasty morsels in tin cans? Yes. I'm sure you do.

Second: we are protecting ourselves. The Segments do not tolerate our presence in the Protected Zone. If you want to know what Segments do to human intruders, ask Singh about penetrating thoracic trauma.

Third: we are protecting humanity. Do you know what kind of structures Ryley Robinson found on 4546b? Leviathan incubators. Surface to orbit defense systems. Uploaded postbiological intelligences. We are not equipped to survive ANY of those threats. 

Fourth: we are protecting a historic discovery. The most significant discovery since the kharaa. None of us are trained for archaeology, for site preservation, for xenolinguistics or for colonial ethics. And from what we can tell, the last humans to make contact with the Segments did permanent damage. I will not be responsible for starting a second war with an alien species—no matter how much of a technological advantage we enjoy.

Am I understood?

{metadata author=Sophie "Butcher" Boucher}
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_TadpolePens.Ruby_TadpoleOps:
TADPOLE OPS GUIDE (WORK IN PROGRESS)
by Ruby

Attention and yu baina to all lubbers, pukers, printouts and other non-pilots. I am the senior of two qualified drivers in this mess. You will listen to and obey our instructions or lose your Tadpole privileges.

Rule 1. If you qualified on the Seamoth or Spacemoth, you should be ready to handle a Tadpole.

Rule 2. If you did not qualify, you will need training under me or Bosun's Mate Rowboat. This is not optional.

Rule 3. Undersea maneuvering is exactly like zero-G maneuvering, except for all the ways it's different. Instead of worrying about drift and thermal, you've got trim and currents.

Rule 4a. The Tadpole is a modular core built to plug in to larger hulls. The Cicada's Tadpoles were configured for cave navigation on Zezura, so they come in a minimal config. To get the most use out of them in open ocean, you'll need to fabricate a hull. You can detach the Tadpolen core if you need to enter a cramped space — 

Rule 4b. — but always bring your hulls HOME! If you forget a hull you will be visited by your disappointed ongod.  

Rule 5. RESPECT THE CRUSH DEPTH. Salvaging an imploded Tadpole is difficult and dangerous. If you need to go down, you'll need a depth module from the Tadpole Pens control room.

Rule 6. Keep an eye on your power and ear on your noise. My dear husband (who NoA will not (yet) print more than one of) monitors all Tadpole traffic on sonar, and he hates noise pollution. So do the things out there which prey on hard-shelled crabs about the size of a Tadpole. If you run out of power, Ganzo may be able to hear you screaming before they find you.

Rule 7. Have fun—this is your chance to glimpse the elevated spiritual plane where pilots dwell.

P.S. If you want to hear an awful recording of me dying in a crush incident come by the scanner room when my husband isn't there.

{metadata author=Tsewangiin "Ruby" Anar}
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_TadpolePens.Eddie_ConcreteInspection_Line3: There's a tarry cement in the mix...ah, but no matter how I sniff, I only smell bottled air. Maybe it's for crack healing. And...oh, wow, look at that. A live culture! That's bacterial action! The rebar in this concrete was alive. Maybe they started by growing bacterial fibers, like a wire blueprint, and poured the concrete over it...
ST_Databank_CrewChips_Ch2_TadpolePens.Wu_CampRaw:
WELCOME TO CAMP RAW! 

You've been posted here to study how our bodies react to unsanitized Protean living. Smell that? That's lead-rich, sulfurous seabottom ooze. It'll kill you. But we need to know how you die, or we're never going to break the three month barrier. When you die, you can go back to Habitat and live in a nice clean tube.

Work roster: 
Gram, Iso, Rhiannon — morning inspection. Gram does comms, Iso does structural, Rhiannon checks the perimeter 
Sean — fix leaks when reported. The ceiling panel needs replacement but we need to prep before we make the switch.
Tuba, Quaker, Mel — evening pump maintenance and cleaning. Sorry.

At all other times you are to focus on growing enough food inside to survive, and on maintaining your own health. There will be a physical at black noon every day so we can watch how your bodies fall apart.

Expect the usual symptoms - open sores, tics, aches, cognitive deficits and memory gaps. Above all else, we're looking for parasites and infections that attack living human bodies. If we had a proper colony, we could do this work with printed human organ systems instead of real people. Sorry. You've got to be our probes.

{metadata author=Wu "WU" Lianghai}

ADDENDUM: Experiment aborted after Zip's disappearance. Masefield risk too high. The Jubilee group is going to give us a natural experiment anyway. Of the Camp Raw cohort, both Rhiannon and Iso joined the Jubilee. Significant? Probably not.

{metadata author=Sophie "BUTCHER" Boucher}

ST_Databank_Human.CampOne:
Purpose: emergency shelter for colonists.

History: decommissioned due to high print attrition. Colonists moved to new Habitat. Repurposed for salvage of AISV Cicada components.

Current status: off network/no status.
ST_Databank_Human.TadpolePens:
Purpose: fabricate, repair and tend Tadpole submersibles. Support long-range surveying.

History: commissioned after recovery of Tadpole and Vehicle Bay blueprints by Habitat divers. Locked down under kurul authorization to prevent Masefield-induced truancy.

CRITICAL DSS ALERT: CODE 3131

Current status: CODE 3131
ST_Databank_Human.WelcomeCenter:
Purpose: familiarize prints with basic work expectations.

History: constructed directly adjacent to Cicada Sleep Bay module in shallow coral field.

Current status: on network/hardline. Lost contact with Sleep Bay: hardline severed. Last Sleep Bay telemetry indicated rapidly increasing depth.
ST_Databank_Human.Habitat:
Purpose: shelter and life support for Cicada colonists during ongoing terraforming/survey mission. Max simultaneous population 21 (total forks in residence history 29).

History: commissioned as replacement for Camp One due to high print attrition. Renovated for enhanced security per FORTIFIER directive. CRITICAL DSS ALERT LOGGED: FIRE! Awaiting repairs (log timeout)

CRITICAL DSS ALERT: FIRE! Oxygen overmix. Ignition in medical bay. Flashover through lower habitat. Control measures failed: seacocks opened for emergency control flood.

Current status: on network/hardline. No contact with timekeeping services.
ST_Databank_Human.Hint_DeepCaveColonistCache:
These black boxes indicate the exit ("death") points of past forks. They are badly damaged.

Scanning multiple black boxes could allow limited data retrieval, including last common point of origin.

Telemetry indicates no more than nine blackboxes are active in the area.
ST_Databank_Human.KurulLevelAccessKey:
This encryption key grants "kurul-level" access privileges. This is not an approved Alterra pay rate and is therefore cryptographically insecure. 

The keylog indicates this key was used several times by the keyholder, especially at a site logged as "Tadpole Pens".

ST_Databank_Investigation.Hot_Cave_Basecamp_POI_description:
A checkpoint for expeditions into the geothermal cave below.

—On-site NoA node could be reactivated for data retrieval

A cluster of blackboxes in the cave suggest it is lethally dangerous.
ST_Databank_Investigation.Habitat_POI_description:
A developed habitat with internal growbeds and berths for more than twenty colonists.

—Hull ruptured by an explosion
—Seacocks opened to flood the habitat, perhaps as emergency fire control
—Multiple blackboxes on site. A search may indicate where survivors evacuated
—On-site NoA node could be reactivated for data retrieval
—One habitat module is set up as a hot lab to study viral biology

The habitat contains salvage, including a powerful scanner station.
ST_Databank_Investigation.Tadpole_Pens_POI_description:
A fabrication and maintenance site for Tadpole submersibles. There is a functioning NoA node in the control room.

—Site suggests Tadpoles were often deployed eastward
—Material may have been returned by Tadpole expeditions
—NoA node may contain information on past survival failures and future threats
—Access to the control room is secured by a passcode

Access codes were stored in the PDAs and blackboxes of high-ranking colonists.
ST_Databank_Investigation.CicadaWreck03_POI_description:
A fragment of a cargo hold from an AISV Cicada lander module. Most of its cargo was either removed or expelled. 

—Corrosion and weathering make it difficult to determine whether this lander deployed safely from the Cicada, or broke away violently
ST_Databank_Investigation.CicadaWreckRuins03_POI_description:
A fragment of an AISV Cicada compartment, including a descending emergency stairwell.

—The presence of a compartment from the Cicada itself (rather than a lander module) suggests the ship may have broken apart violently
—Seawater sampling detects a source of bloom virus beneath the fragment

A juvenile angel comb may have been trapped beneath the wreckage.
ST_Databank_Investigation.CicadaWreckRuins02_POI_description:
A fragment of an AISV Cicada compartment, originally intended for atmospheric therapy.

—The presence of a compartment from the Cicada itself (rather than a lander module) suggests the ship may have broken apart violently
—Salvage work here was interrupted by violence
—Acoustics indicate fragments of useful tools are present
ST_Databank_Investigation.CicadaWreck04_POI_description:
A fragment of an AISV Cicada lander module, broken into two segments. Originally a deployable colony structure, including a canteen for relaxation and refreshment.

—Although pumped out and used as a worksite in the past, the canteen has flooded again
—Corrosion and weathering make it difficult to determine whether this lander deployed safely from the Cicada, or broke away violently
ST_Databank_Investigation.CicadaWreck02_POI_description:
A fragment of wreckage from an AISV Cicada lander module, including a large garage.

—Corrosion and weathering make it difficult to determine whether this lander deployed safely from the Cicada, or broke away violently
—Garage was opened after landing so any surviving vehicles could sortie
ST_Databank_Investigation.Tailings_Village_POI_description:
A group of nonhuman structures above a hot, geologically active cave.

—Constructed from salvage and basic crafted materials
—Hot cave may have provided food or energy
—Apparently abandoned

First contact with a tool-using alien species carries significant risk to the individual pioneer and to humanity.
ST_Databank_Investigation.ColonistBunker01_POI_description:
A habitat buried below the center of the coral gardens biome. May have supported salvage operations in nearby, although its depth makes quick access inconvenient.

—Contains a biobed rigged to upgrade colonists' bodies
—Due to violations of the terms of use, the biobed's ability to reprint lost colonists has been disabled

 Metadata suggests it was constructed by Zygimantis "Mantis" Tarvydas. It may have served as a personal retreat as well as a worksite.
ST_Databank_Investigation.CrabDome_POI_description: A large coral tower with unusual genetic properties.
ST_Databank_Investigation.CicadaWreckRuins01_POI_description:
A large fragment of an AISV Cicada compartment, including an EVA suit locker for spaceborne construction.

—The presence of a compartment from the Cicada itself (rather than a lander module) suggests the ship may have broken apart violently
—Corrosion and weathering make it difficult to determine the exact circumstances of the fragment's arrival
—Tool marks indicate partial salvage has been attempted, but the tools are not of Alterra manufacture
—Acoustics indicate fragments of useful tools are present
ST_Databank_Investigation.NeedlerNest_POI_description: A nesting site for the needler mango. High risk area. Long range acoustics indicate at least one destroyed Tadpole submersible on site.
ST_Databank_Investigation.Camp_One_POI_description:
A partially disassembled and flooded corridor network.

—Human remains in the sediment suggest it was a failed habitat.
—Basic architecture suggest that the builders were working with a limited set of blueprints
—Signs of more recent rehabitation, with a larger blueprint set
ST_Databank_Investigation.JellyBarrens_POI_description: A region of the coral gardens biome overgrown by large, decay-feeding jellyfish called necrolei. Seawater is acidic and deoxygenated.
ST_Databank_Investigation.ColonistBunker02_POI_description:
A remote habitat buried in volcanic rock near a tremendous sinkhole or lava tube.

—Contains a biobed rigged to upgrade colonists' bodies (license violations have disabled the bed's ability to reprint lost colonists)

Components of a disassembled NoA terminal scattered around the site suggest that it was used in an attempt to subvert, reprogram, or disable proprietary Alterra business intelligence.
ST_Databank_Investigation.ColonistBunker04_POI_description:
A remote habitat on the edge of the jelly barrens in the northwest of the coral gardens biome.

—Contains a biobed rigged to upgrade colonists' bodies (license violations have disabled the bed's ability to reprint lost colonists)
—Biobed records indicate that it printed colonist PC/R-DMIK9-SB.02 more than twenty times

Records stored on site were signed by Sophie "Butcher" Boucher, colony surgeon. She may have used this site for medical research.
ST_Databank_Investigation.Colonist_Bunker07_POI_description:
A remote habitat used as a biological and ecological laboratory.

—Contains a biobed rigged to upgrade colonists' bodies (license violations have disabled the bed's ability to reprint lost colonists)
—Biobed repeatedly referred a patient for mental health assessment focused on dysthymia complicated by anxiety

The habitat's metadata has been destroyed by crude software intrusion. Chemical traces indicate it was used to handle explosives in addition to its scientific functions.
ST_Databank_Investigation.ColonistBunker06_POI_description:
A remote habitat used as a meeting ground and miscellaneous workspace.

—Contains a biobed rigged to upgrade colonists' bodies (license violations have disabled the bed's ability to reprint lost colonists)
—Biobed recorded a time-series analysis of changes in a patient's genetics and gene expression
—Records include observation of nearby crab species and conversations with other colonists

Metadata suggests the habitat belonged to chaplain-psychiatrist and AI ethicist Lucretia "Quaker" Kapoor.
ST_Databank_Investigation.ColonistBunker05_POI_description:
A remote habitat with several long-term biological experiments testing the growth conditions of Protean life.

—Contains a biobed rigged to upgrade colonists' bodies (license violations have disabled the bed's ability to reprint lost colonists)
—Biobed set to analyze changes in a patient's genetics and gene expression
—Biobed recorded at least one death caused by a cytokine storm, a reaction to failed gene therapy

The experiments in the habitat were initiated by colony purser Anita Gottschall.
ST_Databank_Investigation.Welcome_Center_POI_description:
A small, flooded structure apparently intended to welcome pioneers emerging from Sleep Bay 3.

—On-site NoA node could be reactivated with a battery for data retrieval
—Biolab provides access to basic biomods
ST_Databank_Investigation.Observatory_POI_description:
A towering structure built by aliens provisionally designated Axum.

—Built nearly a thousand years ago, but abandoned
—Clearly an astronomical observatory, but does not reach the ocean surface
—Front gate requires electrical power from an Axum generator
—Electrical lines lead deeper into the biome

Locating and repairing the generator may allow access.
ST_Databank_Investigation.Rosetta_Stone_POI_description:
An artificial structure with markings in two alien scripts.

—One is the Architect language known from 4546b (Architect Linear)
—The other is an unknown language (Radial A)

If the two markings contain the same content, this will allow provisional translation of Radial A.
ST_Databank_Investigation.PowerPlant_POI_description:
An enormous Axum power plant. Named 'Karakorum' by pioneers.

—Originally powered by hot water from the titan rockbore below
—Later modified to burn methane
—Destroyed by sabotage
—Could be repaired and reconnected to the rockbore to generate Axum power
—Startup procedure will require translating Axum language

Repairs will require an upgraded scanner and enormous quantities of raw material.
