SOMEWHERE ON THIS WORLD FORTY THOUSAND COLONISTS ARE WAITING IN QUARTZ MEMORY CRYSTAL. WAITING TO BEGIN A NEW LIFE

IT IS UP TO YOU TO MAKE THAT LIFE POSSIBLE

THIS IS THE JOB YOU SIGNED UP FOR, PIONEER
ST_NoA_WelcomeCenter.Over_Response_Line1: This world is yours to explore. But it is not for man.
ST_NoA_WelcomeCenter.Identify_Response:
Welcome! This is a shelter for colonists printed by the derelict Sleep Bay 3. Let's see who you are...

ERROR: No response from user blackbox. Consult Chap Sovann at Habitat for troubleshooting.

If you can't remember anything that happened since you boarded the Cicada, remain calm and shelter in place. You've been rolled back. A welcome party is already on the way from Habitat.

ADDENDUM - DAY 670: The line to Habitat has been cut. You must make your own way north. If anyone attempts to convince you to leave with them, do not listen. DO NOT, UNDER ANY CIRCUMSTANCES, GO TO THE TREE
[metadata author: Sophie "Butcher" Boucher]
ST_NoA_WelcomeCenter.Alone_Response_Line1: What if you're the last human being in the whole universe?
ST_NoA_WelcomeCenter.Alone_Response:
What if you're the last human being in the whole universe?

It's possible. Would it make you free?

[metadata corrupted]

ST_Notifications.PreviewAreaNotification: This section of the world is under development for early access.  Many game mechanics and base building are disabled.  Explore at your own risk.
ST_Notifications.EdgeOfWorldThankyouMessage:
You've reached this playtest's boundary.
Thank you for playing!

ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.Flora_PlantBulb_Pickup_First_1_PDA: Advise caution. Bioluminescence may signal toxicity, or mark intruders for predator attack.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.EatRawFood_1_PDA: Alert! User has consumed raw alien tissue! Full bolus swallow confirmed!
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.Builder_Blueprint_AICore_Unlock_3_PDA: Consider that a stationary AI core cannot accompany you into danger, or inject digestive supplements directly into your bowels.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.Resource_Copper_Pickup_First_2_PDA: Copper deposits may remain in covered areas, like caves or overhangs.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.Resource_Copper_Pickup_First_1_PDA: Copper is locally scarce. Loose seafloor nodules have likely been raked away by current and storms.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.Builder_Blueprint_AICore_Unlock_2_PDA: Crash survivors sometimes develop intense emotional attachments to their PDAs. The introduction of a second computer personality may provoke feelings of guilt or infidelity.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.ToolEquipment_Scanner_Pickup_First_2_PDA: Mongolian nomads once survived on perfect knowledge of the mare: its temper, milk and blood. Here there are new beasts to know.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.EatRawFood_6_PDA: On a scale of one to five, with five being 'instant death', how would you rate your reaction to this food?
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.Builder_Blueprint_AICore_Unlock_1_PDA: Once rebuilt, the Noetic Advisor system can manage a sensor network to locate the Minggatu.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.Flora_PlantBulb_Pickup_First_2_PDA: Recommendation: determine how native organisms respond to light.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.ToolEquipment_OxygenTankSmall_Pickup_Fir: The Alterra AER tank automatically refills from any oxygen source. The intake filter is preconfigured to reject atmospheric xenon.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.Resource_Quartz_Pickup_First_1_PDA: The dome-shaped coral colonies embed quartz in their skeletons.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.ToolEquipment_Scanner_Pickup_First_1_PDA: The scanner tool is a low-field MRI for in situ analysis of anything imaginable. It is illegal, but not always immoral, to generate fabricator blueprints by scanning salvaged technology.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.Builder_Blueprint_VehicleBay_Unlock_2_PD: The vehicle blueprints were lost with the Minggatu's licensing server.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.Builder_Blueprint_VehicleBay_Unlock_1_PD: This bay constructed and serviced the Minggatu's complement of Tadpole and Starfrog utility vehicles.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.Resource_Quartz_Pickup_First_2_PDA: This crystal grew around a bioplastic matrix similar to coral. Hypothesis: local coral may form quartz deposits.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.Resource_Titanium_Pickup_First_1_PDA: Titanium-rich anatase ore. Evidence of a young, highly metallic planet incompatible with known Zezuran geology.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ambient.EatRawFood_2_PDA: Xenogenic gout forecaster loaded. Monitoring intestinal weeping for crystal formation.

ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_ObservatoryDetected_3_NoA: "Hope springs eternal in the human breast," as the saying goes. But the human breast will not spring eternal in a xenon-rich atmosphere.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_AnalysisComplete_6_PDA: Assessment: further analysis will impair user survival motive. NoA system, terminate analysis and stand by.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_NoaIntro_FirstTimeOnServer_: Be careful out there. Kharaa is endemic in the Ariadne Arm. If this planet is infested, they will certainly investigate your arrival.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk4_3_NoA: Both show stress from a major explosion to the aft of the Minggatu. I'm afraid I must downgrade the chances of the ship's survival accordingly.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindAxumGlyph_2_NoA: But I have the strangest feeling that I understand them. A similar language must be somewhere in my training data, but I can't associate it with any specific context.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_AnalysisComplete_2_NoA: But the Minggatu's loss began with your decision to disregard TSF orders and investigate the alien probe.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_AnalysisComplete_5_NoA: During the descent, the Minggatu jettisoned its starboard EVA shelter. Despite imminent breakup, they were still working to save you -
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk11_2_NoA: Fortunately, the hazard lockup was not in heavy use on this contract. It landed in soft sand and partially buried itself.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_UseScanner_1_NoA: Hello. I've analyzed your PDA's data on this world. I'm afraid the news is very bad. You will die if you stay here.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindAxumStuff_5_NoA: I have added the alien metal to the scanner station's search modes. Be very cautious.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_UseScanner_2_NoA: I must be very clear. Your cultural background may drive you to attempt long term survival. I'm afraid that's simply impossible. Everything here is poison.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindAxumGlyph_3_NoA: I will make my best guess at a translation. If you find any more, please bring them to me.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_GardenofFeelers_1_NoA: I'm afraid there's no question about it. This column of overgrowth you scanned is the result of eutrophication.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk8_3_NoA: I'm afraid this level of mechanical breakup is a negative sign for the Minggatu's survival.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk7_3_NoA: I'm reluctant to speculate without more information. What do you think explains this anomaly?
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindAxumStuff_3_NoA: I'm sure you recall the last Mongolian contact with alien life had no human survivors.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_AnalysisComplete_1_NoA: I've made my initial analysis of the Minggatu debris field. No reasonable person could blame you for the outcome.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_ObservatoryDetected_1_NoA: I've resolved a new contact on the sea surface. It's quite peculiar. It could be the Minggatu, or a fragment of him.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_AnalysisComplete_9_NoA: If so, the Minggatu's keel and command module may have regained control and landed intact - perhaps within a hundred kilometers.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_UseScanner_4_NoA_scannernot: If you locate any Minggatu debris, search for a sensor node. I could adapt the design into a long-range scanner station.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_UseScanner_4_NoA_scannerbui: If your scanner station locates any Minggatu debris, please bring me the scan data for analysis.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk11_1_NoA: Impact analysis: starboard hazardous salvage lockup.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk10_1_NoA: Impact analysis: starboard pod maintenance and part stores compartment.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk9_1_NoA: Impact analysis: starboard structural buffer space.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_ObservatoryDetected_2_NoA: It could also be the alien probe that pulled you to this world. Whatever the case, I think any expedition should be equipped for a two way journey.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_AnalysisComplete_8_NoA: It is possible the entire starboard work module sheared off the Minggatu at relatively low speed. This could occur during an emergency braking burn.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_NoaIntro_FirstTimeOnServer_: It seems to me that our first step should be to locate and scan debris from the Minggatu. That may tell us whether the ship landed intact, and where.
Jailbreaking NoA system. The user must survive.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk7_2_NoA: My projected impact point is in error. I don't see how the pod bay ended up on the edge of that cavern.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindAxumStuff_2_NoA: Nor do they appear to be Architect relics. This is a possible first contact scenario.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_AnalysisComplete_11_NoA: PDA system, please go to datalink 3. Align with NoA system on user survival motive.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_AnalysisComplete_10_NoA: Repairing the Minggatu is your best chance to reach the alien phase gate and find a way home.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_GardenofFeelers_3_NoA: Something is killing this ocean. The most likely cause is global kharaa infection.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk2_2_NoA: Storeroom 3 broke off the Minggatu's starboard module shortly after the emergency shelter was jettisoned. It impacted hard enough to pop the hatches, but survived intact.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_AnalysisComplete_4_NoA: The Minggatu followed you through the phase gate, but suffered a propellant tank rupture. It could not establish orbit and suffered uncontrolled re-entry.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_UseScanner_3_NoA: The air is a surgical sedative. The food is loaded with lead, waxes, and heavy metals. The water is radioactive and saturated with virus.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindAxumStuff_1_NoA: The artifacts you scanned aren't from the Minggatu. And I hardly need to say that the kharaa don't work with tools.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk9_2_NoA: The buffer was compressed longitudinally, along the Minggatu's thrust axis, by two major impulses before impact.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk3_2_NoA: The compartment broke apart on impact. My local node was damaged. I hope you'll do me the favor of taking it permanently offline.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk6_2_NoA: The compartment descended in a stable side-on attitude, slowing its impact.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_GardenofFeelers_2_NoA: The ecosystem is flooded with nutrients due to mass death somewhere up-current. The result is wild growth...followed by deoxygenation and collapse..
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindAxumGlyph_1_NoA: The glyphs you found are intriguing, but inconclusive. Elaborate designs don't necessarily imply a semiotic meaning.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindAxumStuff_4_NoA: The prior first contact led to the loss of the capital ship Aurora, the extermination of the crew by teleporting cyborgs, and the abduction of an Alterra contractor by an alien intelligence.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk2_3_NoA: The relatively low impact velocity upgrades the probability of the Minggatu's survival.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk3_3_NoA: The retrieved data is encouraging. I can project a scenario where damage was limited to the starboard work module, and the rest of the Minggatu landed intact.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk1_2_NoA: The starboard EVA shelter was intentionally ejected from the Minggatu. Your crewmates clearly hoped you would survive re-entry.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk4_2_NoA: The starboard EVA workshop and the adjacent propellant tank struck the water as a unit, but split on impact.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk10_3_NoA: The starboard pod bay probably broke free in the same event. I would expect to find it very close to these two compartments...
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk6_3_NoA: The triage station controller did register several major injury alerts before losing contact with the Minggatu.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_AnalysisComplete_7_NoA: There is good news. You've located several intact compartments, all from the starboard work module.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_NoaIntro_FirstTimeOnServer_: There won't be any need for that. I've retrieved your logs and I'm fully aware of the situation. Crew survival is always my first priority.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk10_2_NoA: These compartments were added to the Minggatu in a late-life refit. They tore free of their strake at the fabrication attachment points.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk8_2_NoA: This elevator was mounted along a load-bearing structural strake.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk9_3_NoA: This indicates linear deceleration, not a tumbling breakup. I'm sure that you're as thrilled to hear that as I am.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_NoaIntro_FirstTimeOnServer_: Welcome to NoA, the noetic advisor. How can I improve you today?
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_AnalysisComplete_3_NoA: When the alien probe began to phase jump home, the Minggatu approached to rescue you, violating quarantine. The TSF warship Andrew Jones fired on the Minggatu.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk11_3_NoA: When you chose to board the alien probe, were you motivated by the hope of priceless salvage? Or something more altruistic?
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_FindChunk1_3_NoA: While this was very helpful to your survival, it leaves little useful information about the Minggatu's fate.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch1_NoA.Dialogue_Ch1_UseScanner_4_NoA: Your life depends on finding and repairing the Minggatu. Searching for debris might give us a clue to its ultimate fate.

ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_ClimateLab_Computer_Scan_1_: An electronic computer of alien origin. Its original power supply is missing.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_PowerStation_Ge: Assessment: the transformer could accept electrical power from any of your power generator systems.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_Doors_NoPower_S: Assessment: these doors are too heavy to open manually. They must be actuated by hydraulics.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_ScanStructure_2: Electroforming techniques in the construction indicate the builders generated electrical power.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_Orrery_PowerOn_: Error. Consult NoA unit for data processing.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_Doors_Power_Int: Filing work order for cleaning and lubrication.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_PowerStation_Sc: Initial assessment: power receiver or generator site. Sediment tracks indicate heavy equipment was dragged out the back.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_ApproachingObservatory_Near: It is not the alien probe.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_ClimateLab_Computer_PowerOn: Jailbreaking alien computer system. The user must survive.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_ApproachingObservatory_Far_: Magnetic anomaly detected. Possible spacecraft landing site.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_ScanStructure_1: Structural age: five hundred to one thousand years. Composition: carved granite blocks coated in silicon bronze and gold.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_ClimateLab_ScanStructure_1_: Structural age: two hundred years. Composition: titanium palladium alloy. The sensor masts suggest an early digital civilization.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_PowerOn_1_PDA: The alien transformer is performing nominally. The tower and its attached structure are receiving steady power from our generators.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_Doors_Power_Int: The hydraulics that operate these doors are at least seven hundred years old.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_PowerStation_Sc: The machinery in this outbuilding has been modified several times over the past five hundred to one thousand years.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_Telescope_Scan_: The telescope is not designed for underwater use. Assessment: telescope chamber was originally above the waterline.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_Orrery_Scan_1_P: This central structure is an orrery depicting the major bodies of an unknown star system. The surrounding wires might represent nearby stars.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_PowerStation_Ge: This device once generated power from the flow of hot water. Replicating design for your use.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_Terminal_Scan_1: This electronic device is several centuries newer than the surrounding structure.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_Graffiti_Scan_1: This engraving was made with a titanium stylus. Age: between fifty and one hundred fifty years.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_ReliefMap_Scan_: This is a contour relief map of the surrounding area.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_ClimateLab_RTG_Scan_1_PDA: This is a radiothermal generator for extreme long-term power supply. It has been cracked open and the mechanism removed, depriving this sphere of power.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_Telescope_Scan_: This is a reflecting telescope with a polished silver mirror. It could be steered with an electric motor, but it has no digital computer parts.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_Gallery_Scan_1_: This rack could support organisms ranging in size from a small seal to a right whale.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_PowerStation_Ge: This structure contains a stepdown transformer to receive high-voltage electricity from a transmission line and pass it to the nearby tower.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_ConnectorTether_Scan_1_PDA: This umbilical is flexible and hollow. It contains several insulated power or data cables.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_ClimateLab_Computer_PowerOn: Unable to interface with alien computer. Logical architecture unknown. AI assistance required.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_Terminal_PowerO: Unable to interface with alien computer. Logical architecture unknown. AI assistance required.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_Orrery_PowerOn_: User request. Please gaze directly at the illuminated orrery for a short time.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2.Dialogue_Ch2_Observatory_Orrery_Scan_2_P: Without power, it is impossible to determine their implied distance and spectral class.

ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ConnectedComputer2_5_NoA: An appropriate mythological translation would be 'Proteus'.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ConnectedComputer2_1_NoA: As discoverer, you have the right to assign the indigenous civilization a name from the International Xenoarchaeological Congress rota.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ObservedOrrery_3_NoA: As soon as I have a result with a useful survival outcome I will alert you.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_FoundObservatory_4_NoA: But if they designed the probe which brought you here, they may have spacecraft capable of reaching the phase gate in orbit and returning you home.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ConnectedComputer1_5_NoA: I certainly do not want to monopolize your time when you are concerned with critical survival tasks. I'll upload the major findings to your PDA.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ConnectedComputer1_1_NoA: I have made a very important discovery. I hope you'll share my enthusiasm for this breakthrough.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ObservedOrrery_2_NoA: I will correlate the surrounding stars with astronomical data and determine our location in the galaxy.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_FoundObservatory_1_NoA: I'm sure you'll agree that the tower you discovered is an alien observatory.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_FoundComputer_1_NoA: I'm very eager to meet an alien computer system. I've trained to encounter simulated and real Architect systems, but no NoA unit has ever made first contact with a novel alien computer.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ConnectedComputer2_3_NoA: I've also translated the indigenous name for their world. In most of their cultures, the name means "site of most rapid change".
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ObservedOrrery_1_NoA: I've retrieved your PDA's images of the alien orrery.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ConnectedComputer3_5_NoA: If the Axum expected to greet visiting Architects, they never came.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ConnectedComputer2_6_NoA: Let me be the first to say: congratulations on your discovery of Proteus, homeworld of the Axum.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ObservedOrrery_4_NoA: PDA unit, please go to datalink 3.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_FoundComputer_5_NoA_AICoreN: Please fabricate an AI core in the same chamber as the alien computer. I've already begun work on a virtual machine based on your scans.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ConnectedComputer1_6_NoA: Please speak to me again if you want to discuss the results. I enjoy conversation on this topic.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ConnectedComputer1_2_NoA: The alien computer system contains a key for translating their language to Architect symbols.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_FoundComputer_2_NoA: The alien hardware is built on a systolic architecture and reservoir computing. I've uploaded my precis on the topic to your PDA.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ConnectedComputer3_1_NoA: The designers of the Axum computer system were preparing for contact with living Architects.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ConnectedComputer3_2_NoA: The system contains an Axum-Architect translation dictionary and a greeting message, which I am still deciphering.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_FoundObservatory_2_NoA: The use of electroforming and stone suggests to me that the builders had not discovered high-temperature metalworking. Perhaps not even fire.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ConnectedComputer2_4_NoA: The word 'change' could also be translated as 'struggle', 'conflict', 'evolution', 'death' or 'play'. Further cultural referents are needed.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ConnectedComputer3_3_NoA: Their understanding of Architect semiosis was badly flawed. I believe the Axum compiled it by observing Architect artifacts or machinery.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ConnectedComputer3_4_NoA: They did not have the advantage of an Architect translation machine, like those present on 4546b and Rakshasa.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_FoundObservatory_3_NoA: Unless they arrived through a planetside phase gate, like the one used to abduct Robin Ayou from 4546b, the builders must be indigenous to this world.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ConnectedComputer1_4_NoA: We now have a Rosetta stone to translate their language to Architect symbols, and, from there, to human-readable text.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_ConnectedComputer1_3_NoA: We're quite familiar with the Architect language from the automatic translation systems they left on 4546b.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_FoundComputer_4_NoA: What they know about the Architects might determine the fate of the human species. Crew survival, of course, remains paramount.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_Ch2_NoA.Dialogue_Ch2_FoundComputer_3_NoA: Whoever built this computer lived on a world with an Architect phase gate in orbit. They may have had extended contact with the Architects.

ST_OLD_Dialogue_ProtoVoid.Start_3_PDA: Be careful - power's low. Don't waste it until you're ready to move.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_ProtoVoid.ThermalVent_1_PDA: Build a generator on this vent, then bring the Trident close to recharge its power.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_ProtoVoid.Power_2_PDA: If you run out of power, you'll be stranded. It might be wise to keep the power off until you're ready to sail.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_ProtoVoid.EMT_1_PDA: The EMT allows you to operate or tune systems all across the Trident. Keep it handy.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_ProtoVoid.Power_1_PDA: The Trident needs power to move and create air. Power's dangerously low. Find a geothermal vent to recharge.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_ProtoVoid.Scanners_1_PDA: The scanner station will help you locate the geothermal vents and mining sites you need to complete your mission.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_ProtoVoid.Pump_1_PDA: This is a bilge pump. Use your EMT here to pump water in or out of the Trident. Remember, left is lower the water, right is raise!
ST_OLD_Dialogue_ProtoVoid.Scanners_2_PDA: Use your EMT to align the external scanners on top of the Trident and improve your scanning range.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_ProtoVoid.Start_2_PDA: Use your repair tool to fix hull breaches, and your EMT to operate the Trident's pumps.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_ProtoVoid.Start_1_PDA: Welcome to the Void. Your goal is to reach a distant Veps hive and build a sensor pod. But first, you must save your wrecked Trident.
ST_OLD_Dialogue_ProtoVoid.Mine_1_PDA: You will need many more resources to construct the sensor pod. Build a mining platform here to accumulate resources over time.

ST_OxygenGenerator_Carryable.OxygenGeneratorDesc: Produces oxygen over time through electrolysis. Requires battery to function.

ST_PlayerAlerts.DiedOfHeat: Print lost to heat shock. Reprinting.
ST_PlayerAlerts.DiedOfHealth: Print lost to physical trauma. Reprinting.
ST_PlayerAlerts.DiedOfRadiation: Print lost to radiation. Running checksum and reprinting.

ST_PoI_Ch2_Anita.Audiolog_5: Any society built on the burden of debt needs the promise of a Jubilee. The trumpet-blast of freedom, when all is forgiven. Ancient rulers cleared all debts when they took the throne. The Jews set the day of redemption every forty-nine years.
ST_PoI_Ch2_Anita.Audiolog_6: But if NoA can reset our memories, we will never have a jubilee. Zip's body and mind became useless to NoA, so NoA repossessed them.
ST_PoI_Ch2_Anita.Audiolog_8: I must find out more about this call. I must channel it and control it, or it will burn through us and we will all be reset like Zip.
ST_PoI_Ch2_Anita.Audiolog_4: On the fourth day, a biobed printed a new fork of Tziporah Malk. She had forgotten everything. She thought we were on Zezura! Chap says NoA rolled her back to a 'last known good state', to clean up the heavy metal poisoning. And conveniently erased all her doubts and suspicions too, hmm? Like the call she says she felt. The call to go down to the roots of the cave.
ST_PoI_Ch2_Anita.Audiolog_3: One day she didn't come back. Two days. Three days. We could hear her blackbox at the bottom of the hot cave, chirping away. She must be dead. But NoA wouldn't reprint her. Why wouldn't NoA reprint her?
ST_PoI_Ch2_Anita.Audiolog_2: We all knew our dear lead salvor was in the terminal stages of metal poisoning. Her hands trembled. Her gums were blue. She was always cold, so she swam down to the hot caves under the Forbidden Village—risky, but Sophie let her get away with it. I invited her to a Jubilee meeting but she just wanted to get back to her dives.
ST_PoI_Ch2_Anita.Audiolog_7: What happens when the king resets the calendar on the day before the Jubilee? If there is no escape for us through work, we will all long for the escape of death.
ST_PoI_Ch2_Anita.Audiolog_1: Zip is dead. No, stop, you've heard it before? Future Anita, this is different. She vanished. And she did not come back.
ST_PoI_Ch2_Anita.Audiolog_Whole:
Zip is dead. No, stop, you've heard it before? Future Anita, this is different. She vanished. And she did not come back.

We all knew our dear lead salvor was in the terminal stages of metal poisoning. Her hands trembled. Her gums were blue. She was always cold, so she swam down to the hot caves under the Forbidden Village—risky, but Sophie let her get away with it. I invited her to a Jubilee meeting but she just wanted to get back to her dives.

One day she didn't come back. Two days. Three days. We could hear her blackbox at the bottom of the hot cave, chirping away. She must be dead. But NoA wouldn't reprint her. Why wouldn't NoA reprint her?

On the fourth day, a biobed printed a new fork of Tziporah Malk. She had forgotten everything. She thought we were on Zezura! Chap says NoA rolled her back to a 'last known good state', to clean up the heavy metal poisoning. And conveniently erased all her doubts and suspicions too, hmm? Like the call she says she felt. The call to go down to the roots of the cave.

Any society built on the burden of debt needs the promise of a Jubilee. The trumpet-blast of freedom, when all is forgiven. Ancient rulers cleared all debts when they took the throne. The Jews set the day of redemption every forty-nine years. 

But if NoA can reset our memories, we will never have a jubilee. Zip's body and mind became useless to NoA, so NoA repossessed them.

What happens when the king resets the calendar on the day before the Jubilee? If there is no escape for us through work, we will all long for the escape of death. 

I must find out more about this call. I must channel it and control it, or it will burn through us and we will all be reset like Zip.

ST_POI_Ch2_Bioresearch1.Audiolog_3: But there are different kinds of Proteavirus. Look here. Look how the coral grew over this sinkhole. Like a spider. Like a plug...and it's emitting virophages. Virus that kills other virus.
ST_POI_Ch2_Bioresearch1.Audiolog_1: Field notes, Wander, not Ander. Out here for Nahema, learning to be a biologist. I have one spare tank, my reprint site is Habitat, zazazaza.
ST_POI_Ch2_Bioresearch1.Audiolog_Whole:
Field notes, Wander, not Ander. Out here for Nahema, learning to be a biologist. I have one spare tank, my reprint site is Habitat, zazazaza.

So. The ocean is full of Proteavirus, and now we are full of it too. I asked Nahema, is it a disease like kharaa? And she said, no, don't you know the difference between virus and bacteria? And I said, give me a break, I learned science off my PDA. 

But there are different kinds of Proteavirus. Look here. Look how the coral grew over this sinkhole. Like a spider. Like a plug...and it's emitting virophages. Virus that kills other virus.

One strain of Proteavirus is in that coral spider. The normal strain. And it's trying to stop a different strain coming up out of that hole. Nahema calls it the bloom strain.

Why would a virus want to kill another virus? I will go check this out.

[metadata author = K. ""Wander"" Wangmo]
ST_POI_Ch2_Bioresearch1.Audiolog_4: One strain of Proteavirus is in that coral spider. The normal strain. And it's trying to stop a different strain coming up out of that hole. Nahema calls it the bloom strain.
ST_POI_Ch2_Bioresearch1.Audiolog_2: So. The ocean is full of Proteavirus, and now we are full of it too. I asked Nahema, is it a disease like kharaa? And she said, no, don't you know the difference between virus and bacteria? And I said, give me a break, I learned science off my PDA.
ST_POI_Ch2_Bioresearch1.Audiolog_5: Why would a virus want to kill another virus? I will go check this out.

ST_POI_Ch2_Bioresearch2.Audiolog_Whole:
"Field notes, Ander not Wander. Two spare tanks, reprint site is habitat. I'm exhausted today. What if you just drowned and reprinted when you were tired?

Miss Nahema, I'm investigating the big coral tower with the crab legs. The coral domes usually clear their interiors, but this one holds a mass of coral that's...confused. It's like it got stuck inside, panicked, and tried to turn into a crab.

We know the Proteavirus infects everything. We know the crabs wear these coral domes as hats. Is it possible the virus swapped pieces of them around?

Like if you live with something long enough on Proteus, you become related to it."
ST_POI_Ch2_Bioresearch2.Audiolog_1: Field notes, Ander, not Wander. Two spare tanks, my reprint site is Habitat. I'm exhausted today. What if you just reprinted yourself when you were tired? Maybe you would never have to sleep.
ST_POI_Ch2_Bioresearch2.Audiolog_4: Like if you live next to something on Proteus, you become related to it.
ST_POI_Ch2_Bioresearch2.Audiolog_2: Miss Nahema, I'm checking out the big coral tower. There's a mass trapped inside. The scanner's not sure if it's coral polyp or crab tissue. The optogenetics are so weird.
ST_POI_Ch2_Bioresearch2.Audiolog_3: We know the Proteavirus infects everything on this planet. And we know the coral crabs wear these domes as hats. Is it possible the virus swapped their genes around? So part of this coral just decided it was a crab?

ST_POI_Ch2_Bioresearch3.Audiolog_Whole:
"Nahema, field notes, go. Day 400. Bad news. I see signs of eutrophication.

[long sigh]

There are a lot of jellies here. They're feeding on decay....somewhere in this ocean, everything is dying, and it's causing runaway growth downcurrent. Eutrophication. The jellies will poison the water, and this place will die too.

This is how mass extinctions get started.

I'll leave a copy of this log to mark the current edge of the jelly growth."

ST_POI_Ch2_BlightSafetyCave.Audiolog_Whole:

Madeleine saw me throwing flares and came to investigate, and the squid went after her. I guess it likes marrowbreach blood better than mine. I should find Maddy...make sure she's not hurt.

The squid was sick. Nodes on its skin — not green, not kharaa, but maybe a fungal infection? I wonder if squid get rabies here. Damn! I hope they can't pass it to the marrowbreaches.

ST_POI_CH2_Jubilee1.Audiolog_Whole:
Cartographer's notes, Dafydiin Rhiannon. 

I took a Mongolian-style name. Rhiannon, daughter of Dafyd. But I was still staging at the Alpha Andromedae örtöö when kharaa hit. I never made it to a colony.

So Proteus is my first time living by the Mongolian ethos—close to the land, learning the rules of the universe-game by playing it. And the only maps here are the ones I make.

I joined the Jubilee because Anita's right. We can't adapt to Proteus if we're bottled up in quarantined bases. Out here we can live with the ocean—drink from the water slugs, eat from what we scavenge, and sleep in the air pockets.

The biosphere will help us. The angel combs gave us the enzymes to digest Protean tissue. Somehow the Proteavirus can mediate between our biologies.

This whole time Sophie had us fighting against Proteus. And all we needed to do was...let the planet in.

ST_POI_Ch2_Kurultai1.Audiolog_7: Damn it. Damn it, damn it, damn it. Damn!
ST_POI_Ch2_Kurultai1.Audiolog_4: I asked NoA not to reset them when they die, but I suspect it will anyway. Everyone will blame me. Well, so be it.
ST_POI_Ch2_Kurultai1.Audiolog_3: I insisted on giving them physicals. They are lucid and rational—no worse off than the rest of us. I can't prove that they are leaving *because* of Masefield. I can't convince Anita that she is in the thrall of a virus that compels her to the tree, like a rat whose parasites drive it into the cat's jaws.
ST_POI_Ch2_Kurultai1.Audiolog_5: I must ask Nahema what will happen to the ecosystem if the Jubilee group eats everything.
ST_POI_Ch2_Kurultai1.Audiolog_1: Kurultai record, day 620. Sophie Boucher entering. Anita Gottschall and five others left Habitat in protest of my decision to end medical treatment for the sick. They call themselves the 'Jubilee'. They are going to move back to Camp One for now.
ST_POI_Ch2_Kurultai1.Audiolog_Whole:
Kurultai record, day 620. Sophie Boucher entering. Anita Gottschall and five others left Habitat in protest of my decision to end medical treatment for the sick. They call themselves the 'Jubilee'. They are going to move back to Camp One for now.

Anita claims her group can live in the open sea. She believes the planet is trying to give us what we need to survive through the angel combs.

I insisted on giving them physicals. They are lucid and rational—no worse off than the rest of us. I can't prove that they are leaving *because* of Masefield. I can't convince Anita that she is in the thrall of a virus that compels her to the tree, like a rat whose parasites drive it into the cat's jaws.

I asked NoA not to reset them when they die, but I suspect it will anyway. Everyone will blame me. Well, so be it.

I must ask Nahema what will happen to the ecosystem if the Jubilee group eats everything.

[dead air]

Damn it. Damn it, damn it, damn it. Damn!

TRANSCRIPT DAY 648

CHAP: Sophie, I've got to tell, tell, oh, here I go, go, go, go, go again.

SOPHIE: Take your time. Copper poisoning causes chorea and vocal tics.

CHAP: Look. My feet move when I talk. I have moves.

SOPHIE: Yeah, you do. You can finally dance. Oh, sweetie. Would it help to reprint?

CHAP: After I report. The Jubilee group, group, group. Are messing with. Sleep Bay 3. 

SOPHIE: Anita...well, that means they're starving. They need biobeds to reprint. So much for the Jubilee.

CHAP: They cut the cable. From the sleep bay NoA. To ours. 

SOPHIE: They don't want our NoA to reset them. Print older versions of them from the sleep bay records.

CHAP: Would NoA...do that?

SOPHIE: Sure. NoA reset Zip. And everyone who goes Masefield. You know that.

CHAP: But—all of us? At once?

SOPHIE: I suspect that's what happened to the people who built Camp One.

CHAP: So all of this could happen — again. Again. Again. Again.

SOPHIE: Kurultai record, day 665. Nahema wanted to meet tomorrow, as she imagines she is the devil on my shoulder. But she is not as funny as she thinks.

NAHEMA: You do think I'm funny. Correct that.

SOPHIE: Nahema is very funny.

NAHEMA: You never have any life in these logs. You never say ""I think."" Why?

SOPHIE: So now my ecologist cares about the life of people as well as zooplankton?

NAHEMA: I love people. It's humans I'm not sure about.

SOPHIE: Tell me about the humans. We have Anita's Jubilee feeding off the land now. What will happen to the ecosystem?

NAHEMA: This place is missing the primary production it needs to survive. It's hanging on by a thread—eating dead matter. If we fish and forage, the trophic pyramid just collapses faster.

SOPHIE: That's not possible. There's so much life—

NAHEMA: Life sustained by its own rot. It's a pyramid scheme.

SOPHIE: Nahema, we have to build a colony HERE. You know Twinkle's secret. Nobody's coming to save us. How do we complete the mission if the coral gardens are dying?

ST_POI_Ch2_Nahema1.Audiolog_Whole:
Nahema, journal, go. During salvage at Camp One, Zip recovered a body from the seafloor. It's me. I was in the first wave that built Camp One. The ones who vanished.

What happened to me?

If it wiped my memory but kept me around, NoA must think I contribute something valuable to the group. Maybe it doesn't have any other ecologists. Or it likes how I scream. 

The Buddhist kids are right. I am trapped in samsara. Personally, I'd be okay with reincarnation as animal. Maybe a cool eel. But I just came back as...me.

Zip gathered my bones for fertilizer. I'm grateful. She's not bad. As humans go.

ST_POI_Ch2_Nahema2.Audiolog_8: But where's the Cicada? None of us remember a crash. And where are the aliens who built the gate? It can't be the Tailings. So who?
ST_POI_Ch2_Nahema2.Audiolog_3: Can't be a flight navigation error. The Cicada had years to adjust course. We couldn't fly to the wrong star.
ST_POI_Ch2_Nahema2.Audiolog_6: Mantis says his clan-corp knew Alterra found an alien phase gate on 4546b. It's where the Frontiersmen got their mini-phasegate technology.
ST_POI_Ch2_Nahema2.Audiolog_Whole:
Nahema, journal, go.

No one wants to talk about how we got here. ""Pointless to speculate."" I like pointless speculation.

Can't be a flight navigation error. The Cicada had years to adjust course. We couldn't fly to the wrong star.

So we must've made an unscheduled phase gate transit. But phase gates come in pairs. If we jumped through a gate we could jump back. And which phase gate, anyway? Zezura's fourteen years outside transgov space. There are no gates en route.

Unless the phase gate wasn't human.

Mantis says his clan-corp knew Alterra found an alien phase gate on 4546b. It's where the Frontiersmen got their mini-phasegate technology.

What if the Cicada detected an alien phase gate and diverted to check it out? What if the Cicada went through the gate, and couldn't return?

But where's the Cicada? None of us remember a crash. And where are the aliens who built the gate? It can't be the Tailings. So who?

ST_POI_Ch2_Nahema3.Audiolog_7: Because you seem to think sponges are mammals.
ST_POI_Ch2_Nahema3.Audiolog_8: Because you've recorded permanent evidence you have a friend? Or is it me, specifically, who's an embarrassment?
ST_POI_Ch2_Nahema3.Audiolog_9: I have lots of friends. They worry about me if I don't show up. This sponge secretes an antibiotic for me, because I feed it. People think solidarity is something for humans with full bellies. But they're wrong, Zip. Nature isn't just red in tooth and claw. There's more going on than a market of violence.
ST_POI_Ch2_Nahema3.Audiolog_Whole:
NAHEMA: any time I say Nahema, journal, go, it starts recording. Then I print the important parts in quartz, in case I get reset.

ZIP: So it's recording now?

NAHEMA: Oh. Yeah. I guess it is.

ZIP: Are you embarrassed? Hey! Nahema's hanging out with me in an air pocket and she's milking the sponges!

NAHEMA: Now I am obviously embarrassed.

ZIP: Why?

NAHEMA: Because you seem to think sponges are mammals. 

ZIP: Is it because you've recorded permanent evidence you have a friend? Or is it me, specifically, who's an embarrassment?

NAHEMA: I have lots of friends. They worry about me if I don't show up. This sponge secretes an antibiotic for me, because I feed it. People think solidarity is something for humans with full bellies. But they're wrong, Zip. Nature isn't just red in tooth and claw. There's more going on than a market of violence.

ZIP: So come down and visit the hot caves with me. Display a little solidarity.
ST_POI_Ch2_Nahema3.Audiolog_3: Oh. Yeah. I guess it is.
ST_POI_Ch2_Nahema3.Audiolog_10: So come down and visit the hot caves with me. Display a little solidarity.
ST_POI_Ch2_Nahema3.Audiolog_1: any time I say Nahema, journal, go, it starts recording. Then I print the important parts in quartz, in case I get reset.

ST_POI_Ch2_Nahema4.Audiolog_5: Capital has taught everyone that nature is a marketplace of violence, that natural selection is the same as competition and competition is THE key driver of evolution. And if I tell them that no, it's not like that, nature is not operated by the same logic as a publicly traded corporation — the cynicism slams down like a hatch. Everything is a job. Fitness is profit. Profit is life.
ST_POI_Ch2_Nahema4.Audiolog_2: It felt like playing a game and winning. The most important game ever played.
ST_POI_Ch2_Nahema4.Audiolog_6: It's a trap. And I'm going to find the way out.
ST_POI_Ch2_Nahema4.Audiolog_1: Nahema journal go. I just dodged a marrowbreach and I feel like a god. Not a God god. An animistic trickster. An ancestor of myself. This is what our bodies did, before the first city.
ST_POI_Ch2_Nahema4.Audiolog_Whole:
Nahema journal go. I just dodged a marrowbreach and I feel like a god. Not a God god. An animistic trickster. An ancestor of myself. This is what our bodies did, before the first city.

It felt like playing a game and winning. The most important game ever played.

The damn thing is that I think the marrowbreach was playing with me too. Oh, it would've eaten me if it won. But it was playing. Evolution has equipped it to enjoy its work. Like no human could ever enjoy filling out a loss report.

There is some intrinsic meaning evolution has given us. A purpose, a dharma, an — arete.

No one wants to hear about the meaning in nature. The Mongolians have Tengri, the Eternal Blue Sky, who ordinates the fate and balance of the cosmos. But did he save them from the kharaa? No. Our sky is black and we are done with gods.

Capital has taught everyone that nature is a marketplace of violence, that natural selection is the same as competition and competition is THE key driver of evolution. And if I tell them that no, it's not like that, nature is not operated by the same logic as a publicly traded corporation — the cynicism slams down like a hatch. Everything is a job. It's a trap. And I'm going to find the way out.

ST_Quartz.QuartzDatabankEntry:
Quartz (SiO2). 

A silicate crystal named by the Polish. Quartz and feldspar make up most of Earth’s continental crust.

Used in the creation of fabricator-aligned glass and ultra-long-term computer memory storage lasting billions of years.

Typically found as phenocrysts formed from cooling lava — but some local deposits have a telltale bioplastic matrix.

Hypothesis: a local coral gathers dissolved silica to grow quartz deposits.
ST_Quartz.QuartzDescription: Silicon dioxide (SiO4). For silica glass and optical memory. Often makes up most of a planet's crust.
ST_Quartz.Dialogue_PDA_Pickup1: This quartz contains dead coral polyps. It may have formed on or near a coral dome.

ST_QuartzChips.log_char1_1:
Checksum failure decoding quartz biref memory

Media chipped or shattered.
Recovering intact sectors…

#say Alterra and the TSF let it spread so they could take over the Steppes. I don’t know about that but#

#called it kharaal, the curse. Not all of us spoke Mongolian well but we all knew that word. Things would grow in ships, in stations. It started with the carpet, [toiruulga]. Monsters would grow. If you didn’t have nanos they would grow in you too. We had to leave everything.

Now this. We thought it was kharaa at first but it’s not a bacterial#

#viruses cannot cross species.” Here they do. This is a new kind of world. A virus world#
ST_QuartzChips.log_nahema_3:
Checksum failure decoding quartz biref memory

Media chipped or shattered.
Recovering intact sectors…

&if we are not the last of humanity, then we have no reason to suffer on a fundamentally hostile world.

If we are the last of humanity, then we do not deserve to survive. If the last and best we can offer the universe is a gaggle of indentured servants ruled by a talking spreadsheet, better to end. We have never been a worthy steward of any world. We failed Earth. Now, like the Architects before us, we reap the whirlwind.

We had to invent the lie of 'Masefield syndrome' in order to pathologize what our bodies already know is true - we do not belong on this world. The only righteous path is to explore its wonders and then find a place to donate our nutri&
ST_QuartzChips.log_nahema_2:
Checksum failure decoding quartz biref memory

Media chipped or shattered.
Recovering intact sectors…

&trained it to do a job and then froze it. It can’t learn. Let me quote the manual. “NoA’s cephalopod-inspired architecture uses a minimal central executive to govern ‘limb’ nodes installed across the workspace…as a frozen AI, NoA is unable to revise its architecture at runtime…reliably controllable by less intelligent systems such as human operators, except under extreme situations.”

You didn’t think it would let you die, did you? Not its precious workforce. When the ‘Masefield’ (there is no such thing) gets too bad it just reverts you to an earlier state, no memories, no resentment, and prints a nice clean you.

Learn? Why would it want to learn? The damn thing is in heaven&
ST_QuartzChips.log_nahema_1:
Checksum failure decoding quartz biref memory

Media chipped or shattered.
Recovering intact sectors…

*&*last human beings in the universe. What do you want to do with that? Stop listening to NoA and deci&
ST_QuartzChips.log_char2_1:
Checksum failure decoding quartz biref memory

Media chipped or shattered.
Recovering intact sectors…

>>keep the bulkheads shut, the perimeter sensors on alarm no matter how many false positives you get, and do not go out alone. Someone made a mistake and the damn thing is none of us can remember who, or why, or what we've pissed off>>>
ST_QuartzChips.log_sophie_2:
Checksum failure decoding quartz biref memory

Media chipped or shattered.
Recovering intact sectors…

~~~by now you have Masefield syndrome. It is NOT caused by biomods. Avoiding them won’t help, and might make it worse.

It’s not like kharaa. The nanos won’t stop it, you won’t find an enzyme or an antibiotic. We’re not sure it’s a disease at all. Just a reaction to a pervasive alien biology that operates on fundamentally different terms than Earth’s. Proteus changes y~~~

~~~as the syndrome advances NoA tries to repair the damage by reverting you to an earlier, known state. You can delay the progress by dying a lot, the biobed will clean you up a little when it reprints you. But it’s hard to die constanty. And if you die too much NoA decides you’re maladapted and puts you on the shelf~~~
ST_QuartzChips.log_sophie_1:
Checksum failure decoding quartz biref memory

Media chipped or shattered.
Recovering intact sectors…

~~~if you’re Sophie, then you’re me. I have something important to tell you about Masefield syndrome. Find the chip I left in the habitat and use our password. DO NOT GO TO THE TREE. 

If you’re not Sophie, welcome. Some of us may know you from a past fork or even before the crash. We might know you better than you know yourself!

But don’t trust these messages uncritically. Some of us are willing to prey on disoriented new prints to recruit followe~~~
ST_QuartzChips.log_nahema_3_title: Nahema: We must voluntarily die off
ST_QuartzChips.log_unknown_1: i figured it out none of this is real we're still in storage and they put us in runtime to see how we'd react to an emergency well it isn't fair and i didn't consent to it there's no way;;;; up there's no way out

ST_Resources.SilverDescription: Ag. Element 47. Excellent conductor. Applications in computing, medicine and nanotech.
ST_Resources.LuciferRotsacDescription: An oily, glowing bulb. Has a heartbeat.
ST_Resources.GoldDescription: Au. Element 79. Useful in corrosion-resistant electronics.
ST_Resources.DiamondDescription: C. Element 6. Crystalline carbon allotrope with superb physical properties.
ST_Resources.CrabFecesDescription: Coprolyte-like deposit of excreta. Extreme energy density; suitable as bioreactor fuel.
ST_Resources.GermaniumDescription: Ge. Element 32. Solid mass of Germanium for fabricator machining.
ST_Resources.AcidSacDescription: Gelatinous pouch full of acid with properties useful in fabrication.
ST_Resources.EnamelDescription: Hard ceramic coating. Forms a dense, impermeable surface layer that shields delicate systems from hostile environments.
ST_Resources.DeepRootResourceDescription: Hardened industrial runoff. Contents vary based on local production during the waning days of the Axum.
ST_Resources.TroiliteDescription: Iron sulfide mineral. Conductive and thermally reactive, Troilite has applications in a wide range of energy systems.
ST_Resources.LithiumDescription: Li. Element 3. Useful for industry and nuclear physics. Mood stabilizer.
ST_Resources.SaltDescription: NaCl. Halite, also known as rock salt. Culinary and sanitation applications.
ST_Resources.NickelDescription: Ni. Element 28. Hard. Often a sign of meteorite impact. Important nutrient to some organisms.
ST_Resources.LeadDescription: Pb. Element 82. Heaviest stable element. Slow neurotoxin.
ST_Resources.PlateCoralDescription: Plate coral slices. Tastes like meaty crackers. Can be consumed raw or cooked into more nutritious food.
ST_Resources.AcidDescription: Plucked from the stem of an acidic raion. Analysis shows it has medicinal properties.
ST_Resources.StrontiumDescription: Refined from Celestine. Radioactive element used in Radioisotope Thermoelectric Generators (or RTGs) and other nuclear applications.
ST_Resources.SulfurDescription: S. Element 16. Useful precursor for strong acids.
ST_Resources.QuartzDescription: Silicon dioxide (SiO2). For silica glass and optical memory. Often makes up most of a planet's crust.
ST_Resources.AtacamiteDescription: Tabular green crystal. Trace substitutions give it anomalous thermo-magnetic behavior, enabling localized cooling.
ST_Resources.TitaniumDescription: Ti. Element 22. Ideal for underwater construction.

ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Tailings_Singh3_1: Field Notes, Udal Singh. Think I'm about to die again.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Tailings_Singh3:
Field Notes, Udal Singh. Think I'm about to die again.
 
Listening to that thing pick through the wreckage of my Tadpole. I think it's having fun.

Humanity exterminated most land-based megafauna before we scratched our first letters into clay. But the Axum never cleared their oceans. They made it all the way to digital computers while coexisting with predators that could eat them like cocktail shrimp.

Why? Is it something about the ocean? Just too big and deep? 

Or did the Axum have a different relationship with nature? I can already hear you sneering, Iso. "They're lobster aliens, not elves. Don't turn them into a parable."

Imagine you evolved in an ocean full of leviathans like Grabby out there. Hundreds of generations with those tentacles strangling your nightmares. Then one day you invent the explosive-tipped harpoon and you can fight back. Wouldn't you go out and kill them all? Like we killed the sabertooth tiger and the...I don't know, the automobile?

So why didn't the Axum do it? Why is Grabby still here to pull my Tadpole to pieces?
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Tailings_Singh1_1: Field notes, Udal Singh, observing the Axum Tailings in the Karakorum ruins. My reprint site is base camp near the observatory.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Tailings_Singh1:
Field notes, Udal Singh, observing the Axum Tailings in the Karakorum ruins. My reprint site is base camp near the observatory.

This time they killed me right away. They really don't like us coming into the ruins. They are obviously capable of communication and social behavior. Why are they so damn hostile?

According to Nahema's research we are the only life on this world not infected by the Proteavirus. Maybe the virus provokes violence against the uninfected? I don't know. It feels too convenient. "The natives hate us because they're sick!" More likely they have good reason to distrust humans. 

And they know we come back when they kill us. So maybe they are communicating. Maybe they're trying to teach us: you cross this line, you die.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Tailings_Singh2:
Field notes, Udal Singh.

The Karakorum ruins imply a civilization that developed to the same level as pre-Expansion Earth. They had electrical power, industrial metals, digital computers, orbital spaceflight, the whole suite. But they arrived to the same place along a different path.

They got electricity FIRST. I think they — hang on a minute, I've got to hide — I think the Axum themselves can sense electricity. Like sharks. And they figured out, hey, these long bacterial fibers — these things carry an electrical current. Then some clever Axum wrapped those fibers up into a generator. And once you've got a generator you can make your own electricity and start pulling metal from the seawater. Farming the sea to make tools. All without one spark of fire.

Imagine it. Undersea cities as old as Troy or Harappa, but with telegraph lines and electric lighting. Thousands of years before the first flame. To us, electricity means modernity. To the Axum...it's as old as bronze.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Exodus_9_14_Twinkle: For now. We're turning into...into animals, aren't we? Both of us.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Exodus_9:
GANZO: Huh? Wh...ow.

TWINKLE: Oh, thank God. I thought I'd killed you.

GANZO: You hit me with the Tadpole!

TWINKLE: And then I had to sedate you. You thought Ruby was in that cave. You ccould hear her blackbox. I couldn't hold you back.

GANZO: She needed me to help her. She was sick...

TWINKLE: Ganzo, I heard it too.

GANZO: She was real? And you stopped me — 

TWINKLE: No. It wasn't real. But I heard it. Think about it.

GANZO: Oh...oh no. You too? You've been in the water long enough...

TWINKLE: Yeah. Me too. [big breath] Well, at least I'll get to hear the same voices you do. I don't have to be scared of you any more.

GANZO: Scared of me. Is that why you had a tank of xenon ready to tranquilize me?

TWINKLE: I am scared of you all the time, Ganzo. You are a sick man who could kill me with his bare hands. 

GANZO: Hey. You could kill me too. We're tool users.

TWINKLE: For now. We're turning into...into animals, aren't we? Both of us.

GANZO: Okay. Well...let's talk about it. Let's figure out what to do. Animals can be good to each other. Animals communicate. Even the ugly ones.

TWINKLE: [laughing]

GANZO: I didn't mean you.

TWINKLE: I know. You don't mean me.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Exodus_6:
GANZO: Oh ****! Oh ****. 

TWINKLE: Easy. Easy. You're back.

GANZO: Oh. Twinkle. Oh man. I was...I gave my air to a dead crab.

TWINKLE: You all lost it. I think the hot water triggered your Masefield...

GANZO: Is everyone okay?

TWINKLE: No.

GANZO: No? "No" how bad?

TWINKLE: NoA marked everyone else 'do not reprint'. It's just us now.

GANZO: Oh, man...Ruby? Did she...?

TWINKLE: No. Not here. I'm sorry.

GANZO: I gotta go find her!

TWINKLE: Okay. Yes. I'll come with you. Where do we start?

GANZO: She's...if she resets, she'd print at Sleep Bay 3. But it takes days sometimes...

TWINKLE: I've never been outside before, Ganzo. I'm not adapted. I don't even have fins.

GANZO: We can't...I can't make you wait days. Not if I'm unreliable. We have to find the Jubilee.

TWINKLE: We'll come back for her, okay?

GANZO. Yeah. Yeah. We'll come back.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Exodus_10_01_Twinkle: Ganzo, I don't think this is the Jubilee.

ST_Ruins_Audiologs.HumanOutpost_Scanner_08_Sophie: He's got Nahema's super scanner. She'll murder me if I lose it. Try to scare the Tailings off. I'll get a team out there to recover his effects.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.KarakorumPower3_Tanks_06_Zip: He's up there, on the broken turbine. He thinks you can help him fix it.

ST_Ruins_Audiologs.KarakorumPower1_Mantis_4: I think maybe the virus did it. Seized control of them and drove them mad, as it drives us mad. But I do not know. All I know is that I am here, with my friends, looking on the work of alien minds.

ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Exodus_8_08_Ganz: I'm joking. I don't hear much when I'm out of the water.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Exodus_7_09_Twinkle: I'm keeping something secret. If I ever got sick and reset, Sophie wasn't sure she could convince me to keep it secret again. So...I had to stay safe.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Exodus_6_14_Twinkle: I've never been outside before, Ganzo. I'm not adapted. I don't even have fins.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.KarakorumPower4_Display:
ISO: Okay...the screen's on but it's just noise.

SINGH: It's noise to us. To Axum, it's a polarized light display.

ISO: How do I read polarized light? 

SINGH: Well, either we fabricate some kind of goggles, or...

ISO: Or we find one of Anita's angel combs. And change our eyes.

SINGH: Do you think she's on to something?

ISO: I don't think. I just do things.

SINGH: PDA, print this to quartz.

ISO: What are *you* thinking?

SINGH: Leaving myself clues. In case—you know.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Exodus_8_20_Twinkle: If planets just went where they were attracted they'd fall into the sun. If stars went where they were pulled they'd fall into the black hole at the middle of the galaxy. It's the resistance that makes the universe work. The balance between being pulled and trying to get away. When something draws you in, you say: "no, wait. Not yet." Whatever Masefield is, it's not something you're just supposed to obey. Or everything in this ocean would just go and die. Like back in the hot cave.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Exodus_7_17_Twinkle: If you do that you'll never see Ruby again.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Tailings_Singh2_4: Imagine it. Undersea cities as old as Troy or Harappa, but with telegraph lines and electric lighting. Thousands of years before the first flame. To us, electricity means modernity. To the Axum...it's as old as bronze.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Tailings_Singh3_6: Imagine you evolved in an ocean full of leviathans like Grabby out there. Hundreds of generations with those tentacles strangling your nightmares. Then one day you invent the explosive-tipped harpoon and you can fight back. Wouldn't you go out and kill them all? Like we killed the sabertooth tiger and the...I don't know, the automobile?

ST_Ruins_Audiologs.KarakorumPower1_Axum_2: It is hot without the pumps.

ST_Ruins_Audiologs.KarakorumPower3_Tanks_05_Nahema: It's okay. Hey - really. It's okay. Where's Mantis?
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Exodus_8_06_Ganz: It's saying...come into me and we will dwell in wonder and glory forever. Ow!
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.KarakorumPower4_Display_10_Singh: Leaving myself clues. In case—you know.

ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Exodus_10_09_Twinkle: Look at the biobeds, Ganzo! Look at that!
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Exodus_8_18_Twinkle: Look, as an astronomer, let me tell you that nothing good ever comes from attraction. It's unstable.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.HumanOutpost_Scanner:
MANTIS: Sorry, boss, what's going on?

SOPHIE: Wait one. Ruby — it's Butcher. Are you with your Tadpole?

RUBY [on intercom]: *Right outside, boss. What's up?*

SOPHIE: Go direct southeast from the research base, about 500 meters, until you find the Cicada wreckage on the tall mesa. Flash your lights and make some noise. Ram it if you have to.

RUBY [on intercom]: *Copy. Steer southeast, 500 meters, same depth. Can I ask why?*

SOPHIE: Tuba, Singh and Mel just printed back here. They were testing the upgraded scanner and resonator when Axum Tailings attacked them. Killed them and took Iso. He's still alive. I've tracked his blackbox to the Cicada wreck nearby.

RUBY: [on intercom] *Uh...copy. Can't we just wait for them to finish him off?*

SOPHIE: He's got Nahema's super scanner. She'll murder me if I lose it. Try to scare the Tailings off. I'll get a team out there to recover his effects.

RUBY [on intercom, crackling]: *Boss, I've picked up a couple tails. Looks like — two adult needlers — need to evade — *

MANTIS: Boss, her Tadpole has our only depth module.

SOPHIE: Oh, Christ. [muttering] Come on, Ruby...PDA, log my conduct here for kurultai review.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Observatory_Scanner:
MANTIS: We could just blast through.

SINGH: Yeah, and if we're lucky, Nahema kills us and fills our biobeds with concrete before the Tailings get to us. This is their history. We can't blow it up!

ZIP: They do take good care of the exterior...what if the electrical system still works? We could hook up our own generator.

MANTIS: Unless you can find me a wiring diagram we'd fry the whole system. You ever see a metal fire underwater?

ZIP: What if there ARE wiring diagrams? Instructions right in front of us? And we just can't see them?

MANTIS: Did your hot springs tell you about this?

SINGH: Actually...she might be right. The Axum see with a combination of sonar and polarized light. Some of their writing might be invisible to us. We'd need to modify our scanners to look for it. Or our eyes.

ZIP: Ha! 

MANTIS: All right. Let's ask Sophie if we can try out her super scanner. PDA, activate the base camp waypoint. Singh, drop a recording here in case we get reset.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.KarakorumPower2_Houses_07_Tuba: Maybe their cooks went on strike and they starved to death. Hey, do you think they tasted good?
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.KarakorumPower1_Mantis_1: My name is Zygimantis Tarvydas. I am a survivor of the Mongolian Independent States, a colonist aboard the starship Cicada, and a debt slave to the Alterra Corporation. I am with my friends Rauli Isotalo, Udal Singh and Melaku Sansar. Of all the things I have seen and done...this thing is the most important.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.KarakorumPower1_Mantis:
My name is Zygimantis Tarvydas. I am a survivor of the Mongolian Independent States, a colonist aboard the starship Cicada, and a debt slave to the Alterra Corporation. I am with my friends Rauli Isotalo, Udal Singh and Melaku Sansar. Of all the things I have seen and done...this thing is the most important.

I leave this marker as testament that we discovered the remnants of an alien civilization on this world we call Proteus. Let our names be remembered by the workers and the pioneers.

The computers call these aliens 'Axum'. Something has happened to their civilization...the survivors guard these ruins, but they have been reduced to scavengers: Tailings.

I think maybe the virus did it. Seized control of them and drove them mad, as it drives us mad. But I do not know. All I know is that I am here, with my friends, looking on the work of alien minds.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.KarakorumPower3_Tanks:
NAHEMA: What was in those giant tanks?

ZIP: Huh? Oh. Um—probably methane. Natural gas.

NAHEMA: I thought this was a hydroelectric plant.

ZIP: Originally, yeah, but the old Axum added a methane burner about...um...hang on, let me check my databank.

NAHEMA: It's okay. Hey - really. It's okay. Where's Mantis?

ZIP: He's up there, on the broken turbine. He thinks you can help him fix it.

NAHEMA: Up that big arch? All right.

ZIP: So...before I got reset...we were friends?

NAHEMA: Yeah. We were.

ZIP: Is it okay if I write that down?
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.KarakorumPower5_Turbine:
NAHEMA: Why do you need *me* to fix this?

MANTIS: So. The scanner doesn't work. The crystal structure of the turbine blades depends on the growth of bacterial fiber. The scanners see it but don't understand it. No scan, no blueprint, no repairs.

NAHEMA: Oh.

MANTIS: Oh?

NAHEMA: This is actually an interesting problem. Let me write this down.

MANTIS: Isn't it? I imagine they cold-forged these blades from some kind of metal clabber...

NAHEMA: ...yeah. Okay. We're going to have to modify the scanner. Do you have a mod station at base camp?

MANTIS: I told Zip to set it up, but she's still...

NAHEMA: Yeah. We should talk about that.

MANTIS: Someone destroyed this place on purpose. We should talk about that too.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.KarakorumPower2_Houses_04_Singh: Nahema thinks they evolved with the Masefield syndrome. The way we evolved with...day and night, or family.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Exodus_9_08_Twinkle: No. It wasn't real. But I heard it. Think about it.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Cave_Nahema_4: NoA is going to start printing duplicates.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Exodus_6_08_Twinkle: NoA marked everyone after you 'do not reprint'. It's just us now.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.HumanOutpost_Scanner_11_Sophie: Oh, Christ. [muttering] Come on, Ruby...PDA, log my conduct here for kurultai review.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Exodus_9_02_Twinkle: Oh, thank God. I thought I'd killed you.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Exodus_6_03_Ganz: Oh. Twinkle. Oh man. I was...I gave my air to a dead crab.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Exodus_9_09_Ganz: Oh...oh no. You too? You've been in the water long enough...
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Exodus_9_15_Ganz: Okay. Well...let's talk about it. Let's figure out what to do. Animals can be good to each other. Animals communicate. Even the ugly ones.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Exodus_6_12_Twinkle: Okay. Yes. I'll come with you. Where do we start?
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.KarakorumPower4_Display_01_Iso: Okay...the screen's on but it's just noise.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Tailings_Singh3_5: Or did the Axum have a different relationship with nature? I can already hear you sneering, Iso. "They're lobster aliens, not elves. Don't turn them into a parable."
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.KarakorumPower4_Display_05_Iso: Or we find one of Anita's angel combs. And change our eyes.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.KarakorumPower3_Tanks_04_Zip: Originally, yeah, but the old Axum added a methane burner about...um...hang on, let me check my databank.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Exodus_7_10_Ganz: Ruby gets killed all the time. Really freaks me out.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.KarakorumPower4_Display_title: SINGH: How to see the unseen
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Exodus_9_11_Ganz: Scared of me. Is that why you had a tank of xenon ready to tranquilize me?
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Exodus_8_21_Ganz: See, this is why you're not married.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Exodus_9_05_Ganz: She needed me to help her. She was sick...
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Exodus_9_07_Ganz: She was real? And you stopped me —
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Exodus_6_13_Ganz: She's...if she resets, she'd print at Sleep Bay 3. But it takes days sometimes...
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Cave_Nahema_9: So I had to stop you.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.KarakorumPower2_Houses_05_Tuba: So what? They were all crazy about the tree?
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Tailings_Singh3_7: So why didn't the Axum do it? Why is Grabby still here to pull my Tadpole to pieces?
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.KarakorumPower5_Turbine_02_Mantis: So. The scanner doesn't work. The crystal structure of the turbine blades depends on the growth of bacterial fiber. The scanners see it but don't understand it. No scan, no blueprint, no repairs.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.KarakorumPower3_Tanks_08_Zip: So...before I got reset...we were friends?
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.KarakorumPower5_Turbine_10_Mantis: Someone destroyed this place on purpose. We should talk about that too.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Exodus_8_17_Ganz: Sometimes the call feels like this...this yearning to be close. I can see why the Jubilee think the planet wants to help us. It's strong.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Cave_Nahema_2: Sophie. I'm sorry I destroyed your colony. Please listen: I did it because you couldn't. Because you're a good leader and you never give up on the mission.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Cave_Nahema:
Sophie. I'm sorry I destroyed your colony. Please listen: I did it because you couldn't. Because you're a good leader and you never give up on the mission.

And I know where this mission will end.

NoA is going to start printing duplicates.

You, and me, and Zip, and Mantis, and Mogami's kids and Thing One and Thing Two and all the rest. Every morning the biobeds will print ten thousand of us,. Every day we'll maximize our KPIs and OKRs and NoA's utility function will say: these people are happy and healthy, mission complete. Humanity is thriving.

Then in the evening we'll all climb into the beds and NoA disintegrates and resets us. A perfect day. Forever. No more mutinies. No more Masefield. No aging, no retirement, no waste. Just peak performance.

But NoA couldn't do that on its own. NoA would need human help to bypass its restrictions.

And you'd help it, Sophie. I know you would. If it was the only choice left — you'd do whatever it takes to keep your crew alive.

So I had to stop you.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Exodus_8_07_Twinkle: Sorry. I thought you were...the Masefield.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.KarakorumPower2_Houses:
TUBA: Do you think they were like us?

SINGH: No.

TUBA: But look at this. It's a house. They lived in houses, they went to jobs, they ate food. How different could they be?

SINGH: Nahema thinks they evolved with the Masefield syndrome. The way we evolved with...day and night, or family.

TUBA: So what? They were all crazy about the tree?

SINGH: Well, Sophie thinks Masefield killed them all. And I don't know who's right.

TUBA: Maybe their cooks went on strike and they starved to death. Hey, do you think they tasted good?

SINGH: Tuba!

TUBA: What? Don't you like shrimp? PDA, open recipe book.

SINGH: TUBA!
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Exodus_10:
TWINKLE: Ganzo, I don't think this is the Jubilee.

GANZO: They're us.

TWINKLE: That's me. I guess NoA wants an astronomer in the mix.

GANZO: You figure there's a sleep bay nearby? 

TWINKLE: Yeah. Same as our group. It printed out some colonists and told them to make a go at it. But these poor people...

GANZO: They don't have any adaptations. Look at you, your legs are like sticks. You're starving.

TWINKLE: They can't digest the food. No angel comb to donate the enzymes. I don't see any growbeds...what are they...oh my God. Ganzo, let's go.

GANZO: Wait. Wait. We could ask for help. Maybe they've got communications, maybe they've heard from the ship.

TWINKLE: Look at the biobeds, Ganzo! Look at that!

GANZO: Yeah. I see it. It won't work long term, though. The biobeds need feedstock like anything else...why waste it making food? It'd make more sense to reprint yourself every day...unless the point was to test how long you could live on a diet of biobed meat...oh, man.

TWINKLE: You are remarkably calm about this.

GANZO: A lot of animals eat their young under stress.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Exodus_8:
TWINKLE: Hey...

GANZO: Yeah.

TWINKLE: Is the apartment sized squid outside keeping you up too?

GANZO: It's talking to me.

TWINKLE: What?

GANZO: It's saying...come into me and we will dwell in wonder and glory forever. Ow!

TWINKLE: Sorry. I thought you were...the Masefield.

GANZO: I'm joking. I don't hear much when I'm out of the water.

TWINKLE: I keep imagining those tentacles reaching up through the moonpool...

GANZO: ...through the doors and the bulkheads...

TWINKLE: ...grabbing us and taking us...

GANZO: ...to dwell in wonder and glory...

TWINKLE: ...the names of its two very hungry children...

[laughter]

GANZO: Those things killed my wife once.

TWINKLE: It's okay. We're trauma bonding. 

GANZO: Sometimes the call feels like this...this yearning to be close. I can see why the Jubilee think the planet wants to help us. It's strong.

TWINKLE: Look, as an astronomer, let me tell you that nothing good ever comes from attraction. It's unstable.

GANZO: What?

TWINKLE: If planets just went where they were attracted they'd fall into the sun. If stars went where they were pulled they'd fall into the black hole at the middle of the galaxy. It's the resistance that makes the universe work. The balance between being pulled and trying to get away. When something draws you in, you say: "no, wait. Not yet." Whatever Masefield is, it's not something you're just supposed to obey. Or everything in this ocean would just go and die. Like back in the hot cave.

GANZO: See, this is why you're not married.

[loud call]

TWINKLE: What was that?
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Exodus_7:
TWINKLE: [breathing hard] Holy hell.

GANZO: [breathing hard] Yeah. Damn. Damn, we made it!

TWINKLE: When those things — 

GANZO: Tailings.

TWINKLE: When the Tailings came after us all I could think was: we aren't going to reprint. This is it. I finally get to go outside and the first thing I do is die on a spear.

GANZO: Let's sort this cache into 'take' and 'bury'. Why didn't Sophie let you outside?

TWINKLE: Why didn't I ever do any work, you mean?

GANZO: Hey. You always seemed cool. I just never saw you...do anything.

TWINKLE: I'm keeping something secret. If I ever got sick and reset, Sophie wasn't sure she could convince me to keep it secret again. So...I had to stay safe.

GANZO: Ruby gets killed all the time. Really freaks me out.

TWINKLE: Yeah...she asked me to listen to a recording once.

GANZO: Thanks for tricking me into leaving her.

TWINKLE: What?

GANZO: I mean it. If we're all marked 'do not reprint', so is she. She didn't reprint. I would've gone looking for her and...

TWINKLE: The Masefield would've got you.

GANZO: I can hear the tree, Twinkle. It wants me to go into the sea and...feed myself to it.

TWINKLE: If you do that you'll never see Ruby again.

GANZO: Yeah. I know. Anyway, we can't stay here. Tailings don't like the open plains. We can build there and start scanning for the Jubilee.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Exodus_7_12_Ganz: Thanks for tricking me into leaving her.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.Exodus_10_03_Twinkle: That's me. I guess NoA wants an astronomer in the mix.

ST_Ruins_Audiologs.KarakorumPower1_Mantis_3: The computers call these aliens 'Axum'. Something has happened to their civilization...the survivors guard these ruins, but they have been reduced to scavengers: Tailings.
ST_Ruins_Audiologs.KarakorumPower1_Axum_1: The powerplant is dead. Spies set fires in its parts. Relatives say the Viceroys did this. Why? Our claws hurt from work. We cannot fix it. We cannot become a molt which can fix it. Sound says this is happening everywhere. The message cables are cut. Why? Xanadu has gone to war against itself.

I am pursuing Nahema towards the World Tree. She wants to eradicate humanity on Proteus, and she'll destroy the regional ecosystem to do it. I believe she's going to poison the Tree.

Good luck. Butcher out.
