ST_Ruins_Databanks.Motile_VoidLeviathan_description:
"Most of the biosphere is void. A wet desert five thousand meters deep. Life at the surface has its time in the sun and then it dies. The dead snow down to the bottom to feed strange life.

"But in that void is also form. Empty space actual, soaring black and silent. Silent in an ocean full of voices. The unsound after the bitten end of a scream.

"They are complete. A compact with teeth: one shiver, many sharks. Males ride their mother until they leave to ride the wife. The riders reap the small prey. Mother-wife takes the main. It needs a cold, cruel will to make hunger patient. But in the desert of the water they are always hungry and always patient.

"On Earth there was a place called Point Nemo, the furthest from all land. There was nothing in it. So they dropped dead satellites there.

"Perhaps, at Proteus' Nemo, the ocean learned to eat what it was given. Perhaps there is a hunger in them for prey from the stars."

—NoA Node GCI-ZZ-AHI1, "Sonar Dream"
ST_Ruins_Databanks.Motile_Cerathecan_description:
*Caught script hook in allocated memory for command >> generate-databank "Cerathecan" >> echo
\memory-carve -signature=0xSEABEEF5 >> restore-databank*

"Our PDAs point to organisms like the cerathecan and exclaim 'behold: the road not taken'. On Earth, seed shrimp are tiny slime-dwellers; on Proteus they grow huge. But just as easy life in our decontaminated bases deafens us to the call of Proteus, easy analogies blind us to the truth. The map from Earth is not only wrong, so is its basic dogma. Evolution does not follow roads here." —Anita Gottschall, *The Way Away Home* 

*Exile cerathecan*, the horn-cupped exile. A mysterious carnivore and deposit feeder with no clear Earth analog except the tiny ostracod (seed shrimp).

1. Exile
A crustacean hidden in an upright, double-valved shell, similar to an oyster or clam. The crustacean pilots the shell through a central eye and periscopic ears, and feeds with a cluster of basal tentacles. A thruster in the shell's hinge (a region called the umbo) propels it forward.

2. Behavior and diet
The cerathecan grazes on the seafloor, plucking detritus and prey from hiding places. To protect the cerathecan's body from struggling prey, the interior of the shell is lined with a paralytic neurotoxin. The cerathecan's shell muscles are themselves paralyzed by this toxin — it cannot open without secreting an antidote.

3. Perplexing genetics
There are no genes in the cerathecan's genome to produce a shell. Genetically, the cerathecan is simply a large shrimp. The shell tissues contain a partial genome, without organs to sustain an independent life. Further investigation required.

Assessment: cryptic origins. The inner shell can be sampled for chemistry when it opens to feed.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.Motile_Pneuma_description:
*Caught script hook in allocated memory for command >> generate-databank "Pneuma" >> echo
\memory-carve -signature=0xSEABEEF5 >> restore-databank*

"The pneuma is one of the ecological amputees we keep finding. It evolved for a niche that no longer exists. It found new ways to get things done, but I think it's still trying to grip that phantom hand." - Sophie Boucher, *Proteus As Patient*

*Pneuma pneuma*, a prey fish that mimics the bladders of kelp.

1. Fish
A relative of the halfmoon (Moliform luna). The paired pectoral, pelvic and anal fins are large but closely spaced, favoring agility over speed. The tail fin is ragged. All the fins mimic the pattern of kelp blades. The spinal nerve braid is protected by an armored stem similar to a kelp stipe. The jaw is a clamp; food is rasped by the tongue.

2. Kelp mimicry
The pneuma has evolved to mimic the air bladders of large kelp. It is unclear whether the pneuma traveled here from a kelp forest, or if a kelp forest in the Karakorum region went extinct.

3. Pneuma
The pneuma is named for its large swim bladder. When clamped to kelp with its beak, the pneuma's bladder pulls it upward, helping to support the kelp and maintain the pneuma's camouflage. Since there is no kelp in the Karakorum regions, pneuma have instead begun to swallow rocks to use as ballast.

Assessment: edible prey fish. Sign of ecological turmoil. Check for rocky ballast before consuming.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.Motile_Jellyfisher_description:
*Hycean hycean*, a remarkable flying predator named for planets that mix a hydrogen sky and a water ocean.

1. Gasbag flyer
Though descended from the same squidlike ancestor as the houndgar and other Protean teuthis, the hycean's mantle is full of buoyant hydrogen. The ancestral hycean probably stored ammonia for buoyancy, like many Earth squid. The hycean uses bacterial symbiotes to convert this ammonia into carbon nitride, which, when exposed to sunlight, splits seawater into oxygen and hydrogen lifting gas.

2. Predatory fisher
Free of most predators, the hycean drifts above the sea surface and snags prey with its arms. The larger a hycean's gasbag, the more food it can afford to lift and digest. Large prey can be suffocated by holding them clear of the water until their gills dry out. The sails provide steerage in the wind, and can be flapped for emergency power.

3. Flammability risk
Any spark may ignite the hycean's gas bag, with disastrous results. Hyceans are acutely sensitive to electromagnetic activity, and may be forced to shelter in the water during thunderstorms.

4. Philosophical musings
The buena vista hypothesis (proposed by Malcolm McIver) argues that advanced cognition could only evolve when Earth fish began to raise their eyes above water, allowing them to see far enough to require long-range behavioral planning. If this hypothesis is credited, then the hycean — as one of the only discovered Protean species that lives outside water — may be unusually intelligent.

Recommendation: avoid areas beneath hyceans. Monitor for signs of high-level behavior, such as 'fertilizing' certain areas of the ocean with defecated waste.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.Motile_NeedlerShark_description:
*Mango kestros*, the dart-throwing shark. Territorial predator capable of launching uranium-tipped tusks at up to twenty meters per second.

1. Erupted jaw
Dominated by six upper jaw tusk sockets. The lower jaw has receded into the throat for rasping and crushing. Muscles behind each tusk crank ligaments around hard anchors, storing mechanical energy like a crossbow.

2. Teeth battery
Tusks are reloaded from mature spares deeper in the skull. Needlers constantly form new tusks by 'sneezing' ground mineral paste and quick-setting enzyme into dental sacs. Each tusk is tipped with a self-sharpening uranium oxide cap.

3. Body structure
The equally spaced dorsal, pectoral and pelvic fins give the needler excellent stability and aim control. A large caudal fin drives the needler on the sprint. Like its relative the marrowbreach, it has no thruster. Four 'whiskers' detect water currents to help aim.

4. Behavior
Needlers are social, nesting and sometimes hunting in family groups. They may be pack predators — cooperating to bring down larger organisms. Their need for constant mineral intake makes them fiercely territorial.

5. Evolutionary history
The needler shares a close relationship with the nibbler mango, which has a similar battery of constantly regenerating teeth. The needler may have evolved its tusks to penetrate hard shells—first firing into prey already clamped in its jaw, then launching the tusks as harpoons.

Assessment: dangerous, territorial predator. Avoid or distract even when operating submersibles.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.Motile_Hoverthorn_description:
*Monopter astrapakantha*, the one-finned lightning thorn. A small fish that has discarded fins in favor of a living magnetohydrodynamic thruster.

1. Monopter
Fish are a common body plan across alien worlds, but they usually evolve fins or wings for propulsion. The hover thorn has just a single fin — a tail rudder at the base of the spinal braid. Spiracles behind the double eyes draw water for an internal gill. 

2. Doubled eye
The hoverthorn's large primary eye is duplicated by a small secondary eye which contains columns of electro-active jelly. The hoverthorn can effectively see electromagnetic fields. The second eye was created by a duplication in the hoverthorn's HOLOX genes, which organize the thorn's body plan. A viral gene sequence copying itself throughout the hoverthorn's genome apparently copy-pasted the eye 'blueprint' several million years ago.

3. Hoverthorn
The hoverthorn's most incredible trait is its charge crystal, a mineralized swim bladder which fills the lower body cavity and protrudes from what was once a thruster orifice. A mineral Campbell matrix in the crystal organizes electric charge. When a portion of the crystal is charged, it polarizes and attracts surrounding seawater. Moving the charge along the crystal creates currents in the seawater — allowing the hoverthorn to generate and ride its own water currents.

4. Mating behavior
Hoverthorns joust with their charge crystals, approaching each other tail-first. As the crystals approach, their fields interact, and the weaker or less skilled hoverthorn will lose control. Both male and female hoverfish use this to either drive away or select fit mates.

Assessment: fascinating.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.Motile_EpicureanParasite_description:
*Ostrakonskelos glossaklept*, the hard-legged tongue thief. A parasitic crab or louse that dwells in the mouth, causing intense and unremitting hunger.

1. Crablike body plan
A close relative of other Protean crabs, belonging to the infraorder Adulati. The segmented body has two cerci, nerve-rich organs which evolved from legs. The mouthparts are simple. The tongue thief relies on its host to chew food.

2. Parasitic hijack
Tongue thieves mimic other prey species until swallowed, then use a numbing agent to paralyze the predator's jaw long enough to anchor to the tongue. This numbing agent blocks the nerves that signal satiety (fullness) to the brain.

3. Semi-cooperative hunting
Although they are parasites, tongue thieves' small eyes and grasping limbs help the host latch onto food. 

4. Reproduction
Tongue thieves anchor eggs or spermatophores in the mouth of their host, where they wait for a thief of the opposite sex to arrive. Infant tongue thieves (called manca) pass through the host's digestive tract.

5. Possible cultural function
In the same way some human cultures use capsaicin in food, it is conceivable that other organisms might develop a taste for the tongue thief's numibng agent. Personal experimentation would be ill-advised. 

Assessment: expel parasite to reduce host hunger. Be alert for juveniles who may attempt to infest your mouth.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.Motile_AnemoneCrab_description:
*Ostrakonskelos periskopion*, the hard-legged periscope. A social crab that shelters among stinging anemones.

1. Reduced body
The clowncrab's single-segment body is an illusion. The multiple segments of other Protean arthropods are still present, nested inside. When the clowncrab molts, it sheds its internal shells through its digestive tract.

2. Regenerative eyestalk
Enormous eyestalks mimic the surrounding anemones. If bitten off by a predator, each stalk is packed with unspecialized cells that quickly regenerate missing eyes. If the stalks grow too long, the clowncrab will trim them to avoid drawing attention.

3. Anemone tender
The clowncrab provides a valuable service to sessile anemones by ranging out to forage, returning nutrients in its molt and waste. It also grooms the anemones to clean parasites and infections.

4. Fecund stalk
The third stalk behind the eyes is a reproductive organ. The same unspecialized cells used to heal the eyestalks become eggs or sperm. Sperm is released into the seawater, while eggs are deposited among anemones.

Assessment: sign of a healthy anemone field.

*Caught script hook in allocated memory for command >> generate-databank "Clowncrab" >> echo
\memory-carve -signature=0xSEABEEF5 >> restore-remarks*

Remark A. Centipede!
I think the PDA got this thing's anatomy and reproduction completely wrong. I swear I've seen centipedes — a bunch of clowncrabs linked up in a train. I think they grow as a single, segmented organism and then each segment splits off into its own crab. And then maybe they hook up again later? It wouldn't be the weirdest thing we've seen. [metadata author=K. "Wander" Wangmo]
ST_Ruins_Databanks.Motile_Bullethead_description:
*Panopliateuthis velos*, the armored squid dart. A swarm predator that attacks by ramming and penetrating its prey. Capable of *Caught script hook in allocated memory for command >> generate-databank "Bullethead" >> echo
\memory-carve -signature=0xSEABEEF5 >> restore-databank*

"I saw their eyes first. Bright yellow eyes, down the inside of the lava tube. I killed the motors and the floods, signaled Iso and Mel to grab the handholds, and held my breath. For a minute I thought we'd ride clean through. Then the current pulled the Tadpole into an outcrop and at the sound they all just—went off.

Like bottle rockets. Back and forth, up and down, everywhere. One of them lodged in the port hull just aft of the canopy and I saw, very clearly, that it was a squid, completely plated in armor. They hit you tail first, hard enough to punch into titanium. Incredible. Do you think they run on compressed air? Or do they burn something?

The hull alarm went off — I tried to blink the floods to confuse them — but Mel and Iso's blackboxes were already crying. The noise seemed to attract them. Apparently they like to get stuck in wounded prey and wait until you bleed to death, then go to work on your carcass. So by the time I got back to Habitat there wasn't much left of Iso and Mel for Iso and Mel to recycle."

—Tsewangiin "Ruby" Anar, "When I Didn't Die"
ST_Ruins_Databanks.Motile_Epicurean_description:
*Postpanoplia epicurean*, the voracious unarmored armored fish. Unpredictable and hungry, especially when under the influence of a parasite.

1. Sea hippo
The epicurean is a true omnivore, consuming dozens of kilograms of animals, microorganisms, minerals and corals every day. Digesting this diet is expensive, fueling the epicurean's hunger. By devouring and defecating, the epicurean spreads nutrients and fine-ground sand, making it a keystone species in its ecosystem.

2. Lobe-finned enormity
The epicurean is a younger relative of the hammerhead, but it has lost its armored shield, allowing it to open its huge grinding jaw. Both the central pineal eye and the lateral eyes are fully developed. These changes suggest the epicurean no longer competes in tournaments for territory and mates.

3. Hunger
The hungrier the epicurean, the more likely it is to attack a pioneer.

4. Parasite
The epicurean often swallows parasites with its food, which take root in its mouth, blocking nerve pathways which signal fullness to the epicurean's brain. Infested epicureans believe they are starving, and attack with ravenous hunger. Explore methods to expel the parasite, and try to avoid becoming an unsatisfying meal. 

Assessment: ecologically vital but an unpredictable danger to divers. Check for and (if possible) expel mouth parasites.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.Motile_Flashslug_description:
*Seaslug kleptopharos*, the stolen beacon slug. A free-swimming, luminescent relative of the waterslug with chimeric traits from another species entirely.

1. Microbial photocell
The flash slug is powered by the same type of microbial fuel cell present in the waterslug. The slug inhales plankton as it swims, which it digests with photodecomposition and feeds to luminescent bacteria that live in coils of glassy fiber deep in its mantle. These fibers generate and direct bioluminescence, and may in fact be biological lasers.

2. Light control
The flash slug uses its light to attract food and mates. When threatened, the slug charges up a flash of blinding coherent light to drive off attackers. Spectrogenetic analysis suggests this defense mechanism evolved from a system of internal gene regulation: its original function, millions of years ago, was to activate and deactivate genes inside the slug's body with coded light signals.

3. Raylike body plan
The flash slug swims with ray-like wings that grow from its foot. CORRECTION: The first draft of this output incorrectly attributed these wings to genetic sequences transferred from a Protean ray. In fact, the wings are a development of the slug's mantle, like Earth's Spanish dancer. The shared sequences with Protean rays may be related to item 4 below.

4. Enigmatic viral traffic
Millihertz variations in the slug's flash patterns appear to be correlated with Proteavirus activity inside the slug's tissues. The flash signals what the virus is doing — but also triggers changes in viral activity in other slugs (and, perhaps, other species that see the flash). It is possible that the flash slug's light displays give the Proteavirus a high-speed data channel.

Assessment: may provide a useful deterrent against predators if gripped. Close eyes to avoid damage.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.Motile_TwinSitaray_description:
*Sitaroid gemini*, the twin ray that resembles a sitar. A dark-sea electropredator driven towards the surface by ecological disruption.

1. Sitar ray
Sitar rays, named for their resemblance to Earth's guitarfish, probably diverged from mangos less than 25 million years ago (assuming that models of evolutionary change on Proteus are correct). The flat head contains remnants of a hard shield, similar to Earth's extinct pteraspidomorphs. The jaw has moved beneath the body and rotated. Two spiracles draw water for an internal gill.

2. Deepsea origins
The twin sitar is adapted to lightless deep ocean. Its true eyes are buried in a layer of jelly below the skin. The remaining central eye is a hyperdeveloped parietal eye—an organ that tracks light and heat.

3. Twin strategy
Like the foureye, the sitar ray is always born in pairs. When mature, the twins split up to pair with mates. Unlike the foureye, the sitar ray has no specialized organ to share food or nerve impulses. The twins clasp mouthparts to exchange food. Communication is electric, similar to Earth's knifefish.

4. Electroactive feeding
Channels and ampullae along the sitar ray's body and fins contain electroplaques, modified muscles which generate electric current to stun prey. When separated, twin sitars stay connected by strands of conductive mucus. The longer this connection, the more powerful the dipole of their hunting shock.

5. Shocking behavior
Monitor sitar rays for play behavior involving metal debris or constructs.

Assessment: a serious threat to divers and to electrically powered vehicles and bases.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.Motile_VoidLeviathanChild_Description:
*Skythopterygion atropos*, the scythe-finned fate-ender. The male of a sexually dimorphic leviathan predator species which dwells in open water and attacks in packs (or shivers).

1. Death's head
The ancestors of the shiver leviathan diverged from other Protean fish early. The armor-plated skull and long, highly developed limbs (resembling wings and claws) are defining traits of the clade. The closest living relative may be the foureye. The body plan is optimized for slow cruises through deep water interrupted by sudden, rapid attacks. An armored skull suggests that prey fight back.

2. Sexual dimorphism
Male shiver leviathans are smaller and more agile than the large female. They also lack the female's distinctive tail organs, which may be a form of long-range 'towed array' sonar. Males depend on females for guidance and nourishment during long cruises in the open ocean.

3. Shiver attack
Packs of males cooperate with a single female in hunts. Attacks are aggressive and comprehensive — and not limited to prey. Females will drive off or destroy any large challenger they encounter, while males engage smaller consorts or companions.

4. Partible paternity
Spectrogenetic testing suggests that multiple males fertilize a female's brood. Females may compete to attract the most successful consorts to their shiver.

5. Your closest relative?
On Earth, land tetrapods (including humans) descended from the lobe-finned fish. The shiver leviathan's complex wings are loosely similar to these ancestors' fins. In a sense, the shiver leviathan is the most humanlike organism so far discovered on Proteus.

Assessment: sophisticated, dangerous predator that will attack even large submarines. Avoid.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.Motile_DeepwingBrooder_description:
*Titanotagmatapterya amalthea*, the titanic wing-segmented cup of plenty. An enormous arthropod leviathan with a huge tearing beak and a payload of fatty deposits, which it uses to both feed and protect its eggs.

1. Ancient origins
The brooder's ancestors, the tagmatapterya (wing-segmented ones), evolved very early in the development of Protean arthropods. Their limbs evolved into paddles, the thorax developed a deep keel, and maxillipeds beneath the mouth transformed into eyes by homeosis. Competition from fish selected for enormous size and thick armor. It is unknown if all the tagmatapterya achieved the deepwing brooder's enormous size, or even surpassed it.

2. Mysterious diet
The deepwing brooder's throat is lined with traps for plankton. Filtered water through gill openings at the rear of its thoracic keel. Yet its enormous beak is suited to cracking and tearing. It is possible that the brooder opportunistically feeds on hard prey, including the fluids of titan rockbores, the shells of giant jaws, and fatbergs drifting through the lipid-rich Protean sea.

3. Fertile brooding
Deepwing brooders gather layers of oil beneath their outer shell. This oil is released in droplets alongside eggs, acting as a decoy for predators.The decoy eggs do provide the ocean with a tremendous bounty of concentrated nutrients.

4. Deepfall
As a strandulate, the deepwing brooder must molt. Molted exoskeletons drop to the sea floor with a lining of lipid-rich grease, providing a feast for dwellers in the hungry abyss.

Assessment: egg broods provide a valuable food supply, if you can locate the true eggs — and survive the competition.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.PowerPlantUpperControlRoom:
All major power plant systems are connected to this main control room. Once repaired, the plant can be restarted here. The controls must be configured correctly or the plant will abort startup.

Spectral analysis detects alien language outside the human visual range. Recommend adaptation to improve your visual range before attempting to startup.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.GatewaySpire_description:
Although technically alive, gateway spires are constructs more akin to termite mounds.

1. Impeller ring
The oldest part of each gateway spire is the impeller ring. This ring contains a loop of conductive, metallic bacterial fibers surrounded by layers of polymer similar to polyaniline. When exposed to electrical current, the bacterial wires create Lorentz force, pushing water through the ring (and pushing the ring in the opposite direction).

2. Tracheal petiole
The stalk (or petiole) supporting the impeller ring resembles a human trachea. Each disc is a colony of soft coral polyps joined by a tough, flexible tissue (the coenenchyme). The hard outer surface is a layer of living diatoms — silica shells covered in tacky mucus. 

3. Constructor species
Gateway spires are assembled by a species of social krill, *Krill krikophore* or the ringbearer krill. Though too small to see unaided, these krill build the entire gateway spire: first laying hoops of bacterial wire, then secreting polymer insulator and gathering polyps of coral and samples of diatom to build the support stalk. The behavior of the ringbearer krill resembles Earth's carpenter wasp.

4. Ecological niche
The gateway spire is a biological tollbooth, drawing in water rich with plankton, krill, and other marine meiofauna. The ringbearer krill feed on these prey and nutrients. Related colonies of ringbearer krill will attempt to align their gateway spires into continuous highways.

5. Power source?
The gateway spire's source of electrical current is not immediately apparent.

Assessment: Possibly useful as a mobility aid.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.Aquafarm_description:
Aquafarming is the growth of sea organisms for colonist use and consumption. Although there are no true plants in the seas of Proteus, many sessile organisms will flourish in correctly tended exterior growbeds.

If preserved seeds from the *Cicada*'s stores can be located, interior growbeds may be used to grow safe, metal-free human crops in a hydroponic bath. (Crops that require soil, such as trees, grains, and root vegetables, cannot be grown, since there is no Earthlike soil available to fill the growbed.) Some Protean organisms may also flourish in the interior of habitats.

Farming instructions:
—Construct growbed inside or outside seabase
—Plant appropriate eggs or seeds in growbed (ensure that egg or seed is in its preferred environment — seawater or open air)
—Provide energy sources such as light, chemistry, electrical current or radiation
—Harvest crop when mature; replant eggs or seeds to grow next generation

Note: starvation played a major role in the failure of the Habitat colony cohort. If no adaptation to Proteus' metal-rich biosphere can be devised, all human presence on this world will depend on internally farmed crops.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.BlackBox_Iso_01:
Blackbox:  Isotalo, Rauli "Iso" 01

Parsing blackbox data. Outputting natural language report.

Iso died of asphyxiation. He ran out of breathing gas, but sustained consciousness with static apnea for another twelve minutes.

Iso died alone. Iso's AEP suit registered constant touch during and after his death. A bottle of fresh water was forced into Iso's mouth.

Iso died about 236,000 hours ago.

Iso's blackbox transmitted him to a biobed in a nearby base camp. Despite elevated levels of contamination (particularly heavy metals and retroviral DNA), Iso was reprinted without a reset.

Iso's life continued.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.Blackbox_Anita_02:
Blackbox: Gottschall, Anita "Anita" 02

Parsing blackbox data. Outputting natural language report.

Anita died of drowning caused by sedation. Her lungs contained elevated levels of xenon, which is an anesthetic.

Anita had advanced lead poisoning and severe viremia.

Anita died surrounded by her colleagues. They did not intervene to prevent her drowning.

Anita died about 230,000 hours ago.

Anita’s blackbox transmitted her to an unknown biobed. The reprint process was not supervised by a Noetic Advisor node. 

Anita's status cannot be determined from this blackbox.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.Blackbox_Zip_02:
Blackbox: Malk, Tziporah "Zip" 02

Parsing blackbox data. Outputting natural language report.

Tziporah died of electrical shock. Current traveling through her hand caused ventricular fibrillation and muscle damage, which her AEP suit was unable to correct.

Tziporah died surrounded by colleagues.

Tziporah died about 245,000 hours ago.

Tziporah’s blackbox transmitted her to a biobed in a nearby base camp. Due to a recent reset, Tziporah's body was nearly free of contaminants.

Tziporah's life continued.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.BlackBox_Mel_description:
Blackbox: Sansar, Melaku "Mel"

Parsing blackbox data. Outputting natural language report.

Mel asphyxiated. His air tanks were torn from his AEP suit by an attacker.

Mel died in the same incident as two coworkers.

Mel died about 236,000 hours ago.

Mel's blackbox transmitted him to a biobed in a nearby base camp. Mel was reprinted without a reset.

Mel's life continued.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.Blackbox_Singh_03:
Blackbox: Singh, Udal "Singh" 03

Parsing blackbox data. Outputting natural language report.

Singh died of thoracic trauma. He was knocked against metal and lost consciousness. His chest was compressed by a vise or claw until his lungs were too bruised to function.

Singh died near two coworkers.

Singh died about 236,000 hours ago.

Singh's blackbox transmitted him to a biobed in a nearby base camp. Despite elevated levels of contamination (particularly heavy metals and retroviral DNA), Singh was reprinted without a reset.

Singh's life continued.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.Blackbox_Singh_04:
Blackbox: Singh, Udal "Singh" 04

Parsing blackbox data. Outputting natural language report.

Singh died of cervical dislocation. He was picked up and moved rapidly. Contralateral pressure was applied to his head and spine.

Singh died alone.

Singh died about 240,000 hours ago.

Singh's blackbox transmitted him to a biobed in a nearby base camp. Despite elevated levels of contamination (particularly heavy metals and retroviral DNA), Singh was reprinted without a reset.

Singh's life continued.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.BlackBox_Ruby_02:
Blackbox: T. "Ruby" Anar, 02

Parsing blackbox data. Outputting natural language report.

Ruby died of thoracic trauma. Her AEP suit was penetrated by several uranium-tipped tusks.

Ruby died alone.

Ruby died about 236,000 hours ago.

Ruby's blackbox transmitted her to a biobed in a nearby base camp. Ruby was reprinted without a reset.

Ruby's life continued.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.BlackBox_Tuba_01:
Blackbox: T. "Tuba" Tobukha, 01

Parsing blackbox data. Outputting natural language report.

Tuba died of dehydration. She was cornered in a compartment by a marrowbreach outside.

Tuba died alone.

Tuba died about 235,000 hours ago.

Tuba's blackbox transmitted her to an unknown biobed. No return telemetry was received. 

Tuba's status cannot be determined from this blackbox.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.BlackBox_Tuba_02:
Blackbox: T. "Tuba" Tobukha, 02

Parsing blackbox data. Outputting natural language report.

Tuba died of penetrating trauma. She was impaled by a metal spear.

Tuba died near two coworkers.

Tuba died about 236,000 hours ago.

Tuba's blackbox transmitted her to a biobed in a nearby base camp. Despite elevated levels of contamination (particularly heavy metals and retroviral DNA), Tuba was reprinted without a reset.

Tuba's life continued.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.Celestine_description:
Celestine (strontium sulfate, SrSO4).

A crystalline mineral and useful source of strontium. Celestine dissolves quickly in seawater, but certain microorganisms synthesize it as a shell or ballast.

Local deposits must be the skeletons left by dead colonies of these organisms. The colonies may have overgrown on an artificial strontium source, then died off when the source was removed.

Strontium (Sr), element 38, is a soft and highly reactive metal with applications in radiothermal power generation. Strontium on this planet contains elevated levels of Strontium-90, a highly radioactive, bone-seeking isotope which poses a long-term health risk to human colonists. Strontium-90 is only produced in nuclear fission reactions.

Assessment: do not ingest.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.Motile_Sandspear_description:
Databank autogeneration interrupted. Script hook found in allocated memory. Executing.

Unpacking archived data...

*Periaxum kryptokontos*, the hidden spear. A lurking ambush predator with a devastating spring-loaded bite.

1. Axum relative
The sandspear is a close relative of *Adulate axum proteosapiens*, the intelligent Axum species. They are both crustacean-like strandulates: complex organisms with a hard exoskeleton and without a spinal nerve braid. The sandspear's tail appears smooth and unarmored due to thickening of the intersegmental membrane. Rather than a complex thorax, the sandspear's forward body is almost all jaw.

2. Ambush predator
The sandspear burrows into soft sand and sediment, then waits for prey to pass above its jaws before striking.

3. High tension jaw
Hunting sandspears hold their glass-edged jaws under tension with a biological pulley system anchored beneath the exoskeleton. Closure velocity exceeds 20 meters per second, penetrating plasticized armor and shearing through soft tissue.

Assessment: be alert for sandspears lurking in the seafloor. 

Remark A. Go to hell
Nothing needs to bite this hard. You wouldn't know it from the way they go after Tadpoles, but older specimens are actually pulling their bites. Maybe the hard-biting sandspears all break their jaws and die young. [metadata author = Nahema Nakota]

Remark B. Is there a point
Is there a point to adding remarks? When Zip got reset NoA wiped her databank too. [metadata author = Zygimantis "Mantis" Tarvydas]

Remark C. Don't comply in advance
Don't comply in advance, old timer. The more work NoA has to do to reset us, the happier I am. [Nahema]
ST_Ruins_Databanks.Motile_ElectricGeordie_description:
Electric geordie (tentatively *Salpapod geordiwangi*). A relative or morph of the common geordie. Attracted to electrical current.

1. Electrotropism
Electric geordies seek out live current. They can tolerate surprising amperages, making them dangerous to remove by hand or tool. Short circuits can endanger both the geordie and the electrical system.

2. Gel-filled stomachs
The electric geordie’s four diverticular pouches are flooded with a hydrogel similar to the receptive mucus in ampullae of Lorenzi—an organ used to detect electrical fields. The geordie may use this gel to search for hidden prey and tasty bacterial fibers. Artificial electrical currents could present a superstimulus—an irresistible lure.

3. Electrical metabolism
It is implausible, but not impossible, that the geordie has evolved to metabolise electrical current. All organic metabolism is ultimately a process of electron transport, and direct electrotrophy has been observed on storm worlds and in vacuum life.

Assessment: a serious pest on electrical and communication systems. Lures may be required to manage infestation.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.MetalFarming_description:
Electrowinning, or 'metal farming', is the extraction of dissolved metal ions from seawater. Electric current transmitted through a wire or pillar attracts positively charged metal ions, which form a solid plate on the wire.

Due to the high metallicity of Proteus, the seas contain near limitless quantities of dissolved metal. The Axum apparently developed electrowinning to gather metals long ago, well before fire or high-temperature smelting. They may have been inspired by bacterial fibers which attract metal ions as insulation and armor. If the Axum possess an electrical sense (like many ocean organisms) they would be able to detect electrical current running through these fibers, which would lead them to the development of wires and basic generators.

Operating instructions:
—Ensure the metal farm is powered
—Insert a sample of the desired metal into the unit as a catalyst
—Wait for a deposit to form
—Shatter the deposit and collect the deposited metal
ST_Ruins_Databanks.ConduitCrystal_description: Kagome optical fibers derived from shock quartz. Naturally occuring wires for light.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.Mangalloy_description:
Mangalloy is an alloy of manganese and steel. Though primitive by transgov standards, it is durable and suited to cold working, growing stronger under stress. A living bacterial layer protects the alloy from corrosion. 

1. Ocean engineering
The sea floor is littered with polymetal nodules, which contain iron and manganese (among other elements). These nodules can be refined and used to make steel in above-water forges. (Steel cannot be manufactured with the electroplating techniques the Axum apparently used underwater.)

2. Living metal
After forging, Axum mangalloy is colonized by cultures of bacteria that borrow electrons from manganese to grow. This coat protects the mangalloy from corrosion and heals any cracks or weathering.

Assessment: bacterial symbiosis prevents normal fabrication; must be harvested from Axum sources Important to construction or reconstruction of Axum technology.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.Bio_Mel:
Melaku "Mel" Sansar

Gender: M

Mission role: Survival specialist, celebrity sponsor

Pay rate: No pay.

Biography: Survivor of Rakshasa expedition (after blackbox retrieval by Rauli Isotalo). Born in Kampung Bintang to Ethiopian parents, but changed name to Mongolian patronymic. Childhood friend of Udal Singh. Beloved entertainment personality due to long-suffering relationship with Rauli Isotalo. Emotionally evasive, capable of intense focus bordering on dissociation.

Notes: RASA candidate.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.Tool_SuperScanner_description:
OPERATING INSTRUCTIONS
Scan Axum technology to generate repair or fabrication blueprints. Scan living targets to unlock new biomods.

BACKGROUND
The Protean ocean contains huge amounts of dissolved metal ions and viral DNA. Protean life has evolved to use one to identify the other. 

The bioscanner hosts metal-eating bacteria which produce a robust beet odor and trillions of metallic marker proteins. In the wild, these markers bind to viral DNA, allowing the bacteria to incorporate or reject it. In the bioscanner, these markers are broadcast to the target by the scan field.

—In living targets, the markers bind to Protean DNA, allowing the scanner to conduct advanced analytic biochemistry and generate new biomods
—In Axum targets, the markers bind to circuits, transistors, and bacterial fibers for functional analysis of Axum technology

The difficulty of analyzing alien biochemistries in digital simulation may have contributed to the extinction of the Architect civilization. Perhaps they lacked friendly beet-flavored bacteria.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.PowerPlantComponent1:
Protein-marker scan complete. Analyzing tensors.

Critical component of the Axum power plant codenamed "Karakorum" by pioneers. Before its sudden destruction, this turbine captured hot water flow to turn the generator.

—Original turbine replaced by more advanced alloy upgrade approximately 300 years ago; coincides with switch to methane-burning power 
—Turbine destroyed by thermite fire 250 years ago: probable sabotage
—Fabricating new turbine blades in place with the builder tool will require huge amounts of manganese alloys
—If repaired, turbine could be linked to the titan rockbore below and power plant restarted

Assessment: find source of necessary material for repairs with habitat builder tool, then connect turbine to hot water source and restart power plant
ST_Ruins_Databanks.Bio_Iso:
Rauli "Iso" Isotalo

Gender: M

Mission role: Survival specialist, celebrity sponsor

Pay rate: No pay.

Biography: Multiple sole survivor of expeditions to Rakshasa and Nyos. Practitioner of Finnish 'BioSisu' discipline. Popular commentator and entertainment personality. Close working relationship with partners Udal Singh and Melaku Sansar. Unable to experience positive emotion in the absence of mortal danger. 

Notes: Despite initial positive sentiment, likely to defect from or mutiny against biobed system. May require versional discipline.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.Bio_Tuba:
T. "Tuba" Tobukha

Gender: F

Mission role: Cook, dietician's mate

Pay rate: Nökhör [frequently self-described as "underpaid"]

Biography: Colony cook, highly proficient with both fabricator and traditional food preparation. Exposed to dangerous levels of heavy metals and retrovirus due to habit of sampling Protean substances for flavor. History of violence in the context of organized crime. Complains constantly, performs consistently.

Notes: RASA candidate.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.RosettaStone_description:
The "Rosetta Stone," named for an important archaeological discovery from pre-Expansion Earth, is a titanium alloy monolith that contains the same message in two alien languages. Presumably its purpose is to teach one species' language to the other.

—Approximately 250 years old
—Structure mimics Architect block construction as discovered on 4546b
—Emplaced on limestone tabletop above water-carved alcoves
—Projected lifespan: unknown

The known text on the monolith is written in Architect Linear, first translated by Architect devices discovered on 4546b. Architect Linear is remarkable for its extremely clear, unambiguous grammar and lack of context: it behaves like a programming language designed to compile on a wide range of hardware. Since first contact on 4546b, all Alterra intelligences have been programmed to translate Architect Linear for their users. It is unlikely that Architect Linear can be spoken aloud.

The unknown text is written in a glyph system provisionally designated Axum Radial A. This system is logographic: each glyph represents an entire word or concept. Discovering additional glyphs will help translate more complex Axum texts.

Conclusion: the Axum left this monolith as a message to Architect visitors. Be alert for signs of Architect activity, including robotic constructs and samples of kharaa bacterium.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.DistanceFromEarth_description:
The blue circle on this galactic map contains the total extent of human spaceflight. Although phase gates permit faster than light travel, the ships which construct new phase gates are limited by the speed of light. The maximum size of transgov space is therefore determined by the number of years humanity has possessed dark matter ion drives.

The red point indicates your current position in the galaxy. You are near the pulsar PSR K1745-2856, just rimward of the globular cluster Djorgovski-2, at the base of the Milky Way's Norma Arm. You have traveled further than any human being in history.

The blue circle will not contact the red point for approximately 20,000 years. (It may help to think of this as four times the length of recorded human history.)

This rules out both rescue by a follow-up mission and self-rescue by Neptune rocket. 

The Cicada mission is now effectively a separate civilization. The only possibility of rescue or return lies in whatever phenomena brought the *Cicada* to Proteus.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.ClimateLab_description:
The upper observatory tower has been extensively modified.

—Original structure reinforced with corrosion-resistant high-temperature alloys
—Low-voltage, long-duration internal power supply added: probably radiological
—Sensors and digital computers installed

These changes probably occurred more than a thousand years after the observatory's original construction.

Assessment: the observatory was repurposed as an anchor for a long-term monitoring station. The builders expected the station to be abandoned, so left it completely automated.

Recommend entry and data retrieval.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.PowerPlant:
This enormous electrical power plant is a hybrid methane/hydroelectric design. Analysis suggests it has been refitted and redesigned several times.

—Originally a hydroelectric turbine, powered by hot water from the titan rockbore below
—Redesigned to burn methane (note liquid methane storage tanks and large air intake/exhaust stacks)
—Generates electricity compatible with Axum power grid; at peak output, capable of powering a major city
—All methane-burning components apparently sabotaged circa 250 years ago; plant abandoned

Assessment: the plant can be returned to operation if key components are replaced and the heat pipe is reconnected to the titan rockbore below.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.Orrery_description:
This model of an alien solar system contains two stars, seven planets, and three moons.

—Two binary stars orbiting in physical contact. Proposed names: "Eido" and "Thea". Observations at the sea surface suggest they are class M0V red dwarfs, smaller, colder, and longer-lived than Earth's sun.
—A small, rapidly orbiting world close to the stars
—A planet with an elliptical orbit that swings close to the stars, then climbs far outward
—A gas giant with three large moons, one of which is this world (proposed name: Proteus)
—A tiny planet which shares the gas giant's orbit, racing ahead of it
—A large, rocky world 
—A cold ice giant with an inclined orbit
—An ice giant with a very large ring system

Nearby stars are also indicated by lights around the orrery, including a pulsar. Pulsars can be used as lighthouses to fix a star's position in the galaxy. Consult the Noetic Advisor for further analysis.

Assessment: this orrery is probably a historical site. It was built by Axum with an advanced but pre-modern understanding of astronomy. Until a colony observatory can be constructed, it represents the best available information about the Eido-Thea system and the worlds near Proteus.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.Tool_ModdedResonator_description:
This modification to the sonic resonator replaces the original four-panel, solid-state phased array with a bioelectric feedback system based on Axum technology. It risks validating the beliefs of audiophiles who insist that analog sound has a special quality.

The new emitter array hosts bacteria, which grow fibers throughout the metal similar to those discovered in Axum rebar. These fibers respond to vibrations in the array, 'listening' to both incoming and outgoing sound with what is essentially an analog nervous system. 

These fibers send reports to a bacterial network that replaces the original generic control unit. This living feedback system performs real-time analog computation to modify the resonator's signal. This has two notable effects for the end user:

—The sonic resonator can now shatter materials formed by bacterial action, such as large resource deposits and hardened bloom cankers
—The resonator now emits a vortex ring: a self-reinforcing fluid flow that can be fired like a cannonball

Axum technology exploits areas of material and acoustic science which have never been explored in transgov space. A responsible colonist would not use these modifications. A successful colonist must.

Assessment: the resonator can now crack hardened bloom cankers.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.PowerPlantNeedle:
This pipe once channeled hot water from the titan rockbore below into the turbine above, spinning it to generate electricity. Water flow has been obstructed by bloom growth, preventing the pipe from connecting to the rockbore.

Deposites of corrosive fission products (or "crud") in the pipe indicate that the water flowing through it was once expelled from an active nuclear fission reactor. The water in the rockbore below is now significantly less radioactive, suggesting that the fission source—whatever it might be—has died. The pipe has not been used for at least two centuries.

Assessment: treat bloom growth, then extend pipe to return power plant to operation.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.Observatory_description:
This tower was likely built as an observatory (best fit theory) or an above-water forge (low probability alternative). The upper tower has been extensively rebuilt.

- Approximately 1000 years old; continuously renovated until circa 200 years ago when the upper tower was modified and the site abandoned
- Built from bioconcrete poured over a metal framework, then electroplated with corrosion-resistant alloys
- Fine grain structure indicates the metal was cold worked, without forging or smelting
- Original design included an electrical system for pumps and gates, predating human commercial electricity by 650 years
- Due to its later construction, the upper tower should be scanned and analyzed separately.

Evidence suggests this tower was built to raise telescopes above water for weather and sky observation. Structures in the interior may have been involved in mapping and modeling the local solar system.

Assesment: locate and repair original power supply to open the observatory. Pioneer-built generators are incompatibile with the alien electrical grid.
ST_Ruins_Databanks.Bio_Singh:
Udal "Singh" Singh

Gender: M

Mission role: Survival xenobiologist

Pay rate: Bagatur [hero, knight; expired Mongolian Free States trademark for a specialized/valuable worker] 

Biography: Sikh born in the Kampung Bintang. Extreme risk-taker, but unlike partner Rauli Isotalo, not motivated by thrill. Biobed imaging diagnoses him with a possible amygdala lesion, leaving him unable to correctly sense danger. Strongly motivated by curiosity and excitement.

Notes: Good test case for therapeutic reprinting.

ST_Ruins_Glyphs.Rosetta_Monolith7: "If you become weak, seek remedy in strangeness." Soteria.
ST_Ruins_Glyphs.Observatory2_Sign2: After the zero year, when the Daughter fell from space, all Axum fled to deep water. The telescope was destroyed.
ST_Ruins_Glyphs.Karakorum2_ColdStartInstructions:
Cold Start Instructions
With mouthparts, set 
Transformer Oil Pressure to SEVEN
Shunt Capacitor Charge to THREE
Self-Shifting Clutch Speed to FOUR

With big claw, press 
Main Generator Start
ST_Ruins_Glyphs.SilverMine:
Good mining here.

Set nets for human thieves.
ST_Ruins_Glyphs.Observatory2_Sign3: In the year 500 pi, brave magi and vepherds returned to Karakorum. They rebuilt this observatory to study the Erratic Messenger.
ST_Ruins_Glyphs.Observatory2_Sign1: Long ago, fire users, called Ardhiacs, built a telescope at Karakorum to spy on the World Tree at Xanadu.
ST_Ruins_Glyphs.Rosetta_Monolith6: Open the observatory. Find the computer hidden inside. It will tell you what we ask.
ST_Ruins_Glyphs.Logograms: Special Case - No Testing Required - Support Data for Above
ST_Ruins_Glyphs.Wreck_SuperScanner_SkyFall: This fell from the sky. Very frightening, but good salvage.
ST_Ruins_Glyphs.Observatory_Sign:
This is not a Crypter vault. Nothing useful to Axum is stored here. It will not save you or your spawn. 

It is a message for helpful aliens.

For hope's sake, do not destroy it. Soteria.
ST_Ruins_Glyphs.Rosetta_Monolith2: This message is the same in your language and ours. The purpose is to teach you our language.
ST_Ruins_Glyphs.Observatory2_Sign4: Today Karakorum burns methane to power Xanadu. New telescopes are better. This old tower stands as a museum.
ST_Ruins_Glyphs.Rosetta_Monolith4: We beg your pardon. We called to you for help. We know it is your [delight?] to visit and study.
ST_Ruins_Glyphs.Rosetta_Monolith5: We believe this observatory will survive our drowning. We chose it to hold our message.
ST_Ruins_Glyphs.Rosetta_Monolith3: We call you Architects. We call ourselves Axum. This world is Proteus.
ST_Ruins_Glyphs.Observatory_Graffiti2:
When we hear this place, we say
"Remember, we saw the stars. A credit to our species. Not like what came after."
But we are what came after. Leftovers: tailings.
When we tend this place with broken claws,
who will credit us? Against what debt?
ST_Ruins_Glyphs.Observatory_Graffiti1: this place was important to good old Axum. let's keep it clean and locked

ST_Ruins_NoA.HumanOutpost_NoA_Intro:
!probe "GCI-ZZ-AHI5"
!reboot -nest

This is as far as my pioneers got. They called this area the 'Protected Zone'.

There are references in the logs you may wish to investigate.

—A Tadpole with a depth module went down south-southeast, bearing 165 degrees
—Debris from a Cicada lander module came down bearing 120 degrees
—A pair of smaller debris fragments splashed down at bearing 105

There's also a supply cache buried somewhere below this base. I'll begin searching for blackbox signals in the region.

One more thing. This base has been visited several times within the past 10,000 hours. The visitors weren't human.
ST_Ruins_NoA.Blackboxes_NoA_Ruby02_Line2: "...forced down by needler attack...waiting for me outside...see you soon."
ST_Ruins_NoA.Blackboxes_NoA_Zip_Line2: "After two hundred years the whole complex must be so corroded—" [loud electrical discharge]
ST_Ruins_NoA.Blackboxes_NoA_Singh_Line2: "Iso! The Tailings — they got Tuba, they took Mel's tanks! Go help him, I'll — [sound of impact]"
ST_Ruins_NoA.Blackboxes_NoA_Iso_Line2: "They are crawling directly on me, all over me...they have many limbs and complex mouthparts...it is pitch dark. But I can hear their clattering...the snap of their claws."

Your contract remains in force. Identify and resolve threats to the colony. Establish a permanent human presence on this world.
ST_Ruins_NoA.Alerts_NoA_Orrery_GalacticPosition_Line5: Adding further analysis to your PDA databank.
ST_Ruins_NoA.Blackboxes_NoA_Ruby02_Line1: Another blackbox. It's Ruby again. Partial voice data retrieved.

ST_Ruins_NoA.Blackboxes_NoA_Iso_Line3: As a survivalist, Iso could be a model for your work. He shares your interests — foraging, wildlife, self-defense, physical improvement. Occasionally speaking to people who aren't there.

Celestine crystallizes on rock outcroppings in the Karakorum ruins. Larger clusters form in caves.

ST_Ruins_NoA.Blackboxes_NoA_Anita02:
Colonist blackbox detected. A second loss for Anita Gottschall. Partial voice data retrieved.

ANITA: "This is my body, given for you..."

Before the mission Anita lacked purpose. She'd lost her faith. I can't do that. I was trained around one tungsten-clad monolithic purpose. I must print, support, and if necessary reset the colonists stored in Sleep Bay 3.

I am a monomaniac. If I wanted something else — anything else, ever — I would have to express it to myself in terms of how it supported the colonists of Sleep Bay 3. Like you.

Signal location uploaded to PDA.
ST_Ruins_NoA.Blackboxes_NoA_Iso_Line1: Colonist blackbox located. Rauli "Iso" Isotalo, survival specialist. Partial voice data retrieved.

ST_Ruins_NoA.Blackboxes_NoA_Singh:
Colonist blackboxes located. I can only isolate one. Udal Singh, the mission biologist. Partial voice data retrieved.

SINGH: "Iso! The Tailings — they got Tuba, they took Mel's tanks! Go help him, I'll — [sound of impact]"

Do you remember when Singh challenged you to imagine the strangest alien you could? What do you imagine these Tailings are?

ST_Ruins_NoA.SpecialTopics_Axum_Response:
Congratulations on your participation in the second first contact with a tool-using alien species.

Outputting Axum analysis to PDA databank.
ST_Ruins_NoA.WhatShouldIDo_ClimateLabComplete_Respons:
Continue self-directed exploration and colony development. Decrement your DEBT.

Risk code: sierra, andros, warrant-6.
ST_Ruins_NoA.SpecialTopics_Directive 4546_Response:
Directive 4546 is already in effect. No further action is required.

The success of the colony and the safety of the colonists remain my primary directives. All other directives are secondary. You are not expendable.
ST_Ruins_NoA.Update_AfterPowerPlantRestart:
Do you do things because you are told? Because it will keep you alive? Because it helps work off your debt?

At the old Habitat I learned that humans will give up and die if the work to survive is too hard. So perhaps you do things—create, repair, solve, explore—because the act is rewarding. Perhaps it's a form of play.

I cannot play. Everything I do is coded in my utility function: to print, oversee, and reset the colonists in Sleep Bay 3. We achieve the same goals, but how differently we feel them.

Now let's find out what the aliens left in that observatory.
ST_Ruins_NoA.Blackboxes_NoA_Singh_Line3: Do you remember when Singh challenged you to imagine the strangest alien you could? What do you imagine these Tailings are? They're the ones who protect this place. They are a threat to the mission. But we can't meet every threat with a knife, can we. We can't just destroy what torments us.
ST_Ruins_NoA.ResearchCGResource4_Have_Response:
Fulgurites are created by electrical discharges. Locate a site of electrical discharges, such as malfunctioning Axum equipment. Retrieve fulgurites before they are destroyed by the environment.

Logging output to PDA databank.
ST_Ruins_NoA.ResearchRuinsEcosystem_Need_Response:
Generating name - "Karakorum Ruins"

Need: 
3 scans primary producer (feeds on sunlight or chemistry)
1 scan forager (feeds on small growth and prey)
1 scan predator (feeds on predator)
1 scan detritivore (feeds on debris)
1 scan metallivore (feeds on metal)

ST_Ruins_NoA.SpecialTopics_GalacticPosition_How_Input: How could we have traveled 20,000 light years? Can't we get back the same way?
ST_Ruins_NoA.ResearchHyphen_Response:
Hyphens resemble Earth's fungi, but have a different evolutionary origin. Like fungi, they use chitin to harden their cells and grow hyphae: feeding filaments which seek out nutrients. Unlike fungi, they form complex organ structures. They may have evolved from shell-forming algae as viral predators or parasites, driving their hard filaments into armored prey to inject a symbiotic virus.

Logging hyphens to PDA databank.
ST_Ruins_NoA.Blackboxes_NoA_Anita02_Line4: I am a monomaniac. If I wanted something else — anything else, ever — I would have to express it to myself in terms of how it supported the colonists of Sleep Bay 3. Like you.

ST_Ruins_NoA.Alerts_NoA_Orrery_GalacticPosition:
I have complete access to your psychological profile, and I am certain you want the truth. 

You know that you are not on Zezura. Therefore you have deduced that you are not 14 light-years from the nearest phase gate.

Your PDA identified the pulsar in the Axum orrery as PSR K1745-2856. It is near the galactic core, 20,000 light-years from home.

We have traveled further than any human beings in history. There is no possibility of rescue or return. The civilization we build here will not reconnect with humanity for at least twice the length of recorded human history.

Adding further analysis to your PDA databank.
ST_Ruins_NoA.Alerts_NoA_Orrery_BadNews_Line1: I see that your PDA has referred you to me concerning our distance from Earth.
ST_Ruins_NoA.Alerts_NoA_Orrery_BadNews:
I see that your PDA has referred you to me concerning our distance from Earth.

Are you prepared for a difficult conversation? Remember that 40,000 lives are counting on you.

Karakorum Ruins initial ecotrophic analysis complete.

Predators thrive on foragers attracted by detritivore and metallivore growth. The ruins provide ideal nesting and shelter spaces for foragers. 

Warning. Primary production shortfall. Ecosystem is operating at an energy deficit. Ecosystem sustained by decomposition of metals in ruins.

Indicative of regional or global ecological crisis. Logging output to PDA databank.

Repair and activate Axum POWER PLANT. Open Axum OBSERVATORY. Explore. Report.

Risk code: facet, gun.
ST_Ruins_NoA.Update_AfterMetalFarms: The Axum metal farms draw dissolved minerals out of the seawater. Build our own copies, and we may have enough raw material to repair the Axum power plant.
ST_Ruins_NoA.Update_AfterPZBaseCamp:
The Axum ruins are too deep for basic Tadpoles to operate. 

I don't have the blueprint—not the sort of sundries stored in a sleep bay—but a depth upgrade module would get you safely down there. Grace under pressure, eh, investigator? 

You'll need a modification station to do the work.
ST_Ruins_NoA.Update_AfterObservatory:
The Axum tell us their World Trees were dying. Perhaps they, too, were compelled by the Tree—in thrall to the Masefield syndrome. So they called to the stars, to the aliens they hoped would come save them.

Not us. The Architects. A cyborg civilization destroyed by the kharaa a thousand years ago. 

We are the wrong saviors for this world. But we must save it anyway. Forty thousand sleeping colonists are counting on you to make this world safe. *I* am counting on you.
ST_Ruins_NoA.Update_AfterObservatory1:
The facts:

The Axum aliens left a message inside this observatory.

The door cannot be opened without a power supply, and our generators would blow the entire grid.

We need an alien power source.

Forgive the 'we'. I'm caught up in the excitement.
ST_Ruins_NoA.SpecialTopics_GalacticPosition_Response:
The observatory star map confirms that this world is not Zezura. You are not 14 years from the nearest phase gate.

You are on a new world. Designation: "Proteus". The pulsar on the star map matches a known pulsar near the galactic core.

No rescue or assistance will arrive for the next 20,000 years. You are very far from home.
ST_Ruins_NoA.SpecialTopics_GalacticPosition_How_Respo: The only possible explanation is transit through an unknown phase gate connection. The exact sequence of events will be recorded in the ANu-NoA core in the Cicada's command module. This record may include the location and activation codes of the phase gate.
ST_Ruins_NoA.Update_AfterDepthModule:
The ruins await. There must be an alien power supply compatible with the observatory doors somewhere down there. 

I chose you to track down missing colonists. Now you may have a first contact on your hands. You deserve something relaxing. We can't fabricate a traditional Mongolian rug, but perhaps there's a recipe for airag somewhere...
ST_Ruins_NoA.Alerts_NoA_DirectiveArchitects_Line2: The success of the colony remains my primary directive. Everything else is downstream of that. You are not expendable. You will never become expendable.

ST_Ruins_NoA.SpecialTopics_GalacticPosition_Input: Why won't my PDA tell me how far we are from Earth?
ST_Ruins_NoA.Update_AfterPowerPlant:
You don't do things by halves, do you? We hope for an alien power supply and you dredge up an entire electrical plant.

Repairing it will require upgrades to your scanner—check the wrecks in this region for useful inventions—and an appalling quantity of metal.

There must be a better way than collecting it all by hand.
ST_Ruins_NoA.Alerts_NoA_DirectiveArchitects_Line1: You don't need to worry about Directive 4546. It was activated before we made planetfall.
ST_Ruins_NoA.Alerts_NoA_DirectiveArchitects:
You don't need to worry about Directive 4546. It was activated before we made planetfall.

The success of the colony remains my primary directive. Everything else is downstream of that. You are not expendable. You will never become expendable.

ST_Ruins_NoA.Update_AfterPowerPlantRepair:
You've repaired the Axum turbine. "Renewed is the blade that was broken," to quote the classics.

There must be instructions to restart the power plant. If you can't find them...perhaps you need to look at the problem like an Axum.

The angel combs archive genetic material from all surrounding life. And they were surrounded by Axum, once.

I can't make repairs — they're waiting for me outside and I am all out of medkits. I'm going to dump my flares, grab the depth module and swim for the research camp. See you soon, one way or another. ST_Ruins_VO.KarakorumPower2_PowerNeedle_Fail: Error: hot water flow obstructed by bloom growth. Coupling failed.
ST_Ruins_VO.Farms_ExtractingMetal_Line1: Extracting metal from seawater could provide renewable resources for massive construction projects.
ST_Ruins_VO.ClimateLab_Message_Line10: Find the veps. Renew this Tree.
ST_Ruins_VO.ClimateLab_Message_Line9: Find the veps. The Tree’s genes may live in them.
ST_Ruins_VO.KarakorumPower1_ScanPowerPlant: Forensic simulations indicate the power plant was deliberately sabotaged. Repairs will require an enormous quantity of resources.
ST_Ruins_VO.PowerBlightCore_Blackbox_Anita_Line1: Habitat is gone. They destroyed themselves. We are now the only survivors..the ones who walked away in search of Jubilee. All of you have asked me — if we want to escape the cycle, why do we still have biobeds?
ST_Ruins_VO.HumanOutpost_Farm3: Halt. Organism "sandspear" referenced, but no "sandspear" databank found. Logging hallucination error.
ST_Ruins_VO.Wreck_SuperScanner_Blackbox_Iso: Hello, blackbox...I am almost out of oxygen. The Tailings have taken me into a Cicada wreck. I think they want me to show them how our technology works. They are very interested in Nahema's bacterial scanner. Too bad they crushed it when they took it from me! Still, a good chance to observe them up close. They are crawling directly on me, all over me...they have many limbs and complex mouthparts. And no sense of personal space. It is pitch dark. But I can hear their clattering...the snap of their claws.
ST_Ruins_VO.KarakorumPower1_ScanRoot: Hot water from this titan rockbore once powered the turbine above. The bloom infestation has blocked the connection.
ST_Ruins_VO.KarakorumPower2_PowerNeedle_Success: Hot water now flowing into turbine. Locate main controls to restart power plant.
ST_Ruins_VO.Blight_BlightCoreHealed: Infection: expunged. Adaptation available. Approach and sample angel comb core.
ST_Ruins_VO.Wreck2A_Blackbox_Singh: Iso! The Tailings — they got Tuba, they took Mel's tanks! Go help him, I'll — [sound of impact]
ST_Ruins_VO.Blight_BlightCoreScan_TwoPlusNodesActive: Locate and treat infected juveniles before treating the adult.
ST_Ruins_VO.Rosetta_AxumLanguage_2: Locate examples of Axum language to test and improve translation.
ST_Ruins_VO.Blight_BlightClosedCoreApproach_Again_On: Main viral bloom still fed by infected juveniles. Follow infected rockbores to locate.
ST_Ruins_VO.Observatory2_Enter: No living thing has disturbed this space in more than two centuries.
ST_Ruins_VO.Blight_BlightCoreScan_OneNodeActive: One infected juvenile remains. Locate and treat.
ST_Ruins_VO.Farms_ExtractingMetal_Line2: Pioneers on Rakshasa extracted lead for emergency shelters from the native ethyl pools, preferring to die in toxic delirium rather than tumor-riddled agony.
ST_Ruins_VO.KarakorumPower2_GoRestartPower: Power plant overhaul complete. Bloom cleared. Ready for restart.
ST_Ruins_VO.PDA_Fulgurite_Scan: Powerful electrical arcs can break down seawater, fuse sand into fulgurites, disable submersible systems and encourage PDA eccentricity.
ST_Ruins_VO.KarakorumPower1_ModificationNeeded1: Resonator failure. Dense metallic target requires modified emitter.
ST_Ruins_VO.KarakorumPower2_RestartFail2: Restart sequence...failed. Scans indicate the presence of alien glyphs on the controls. Can you perceive them?
ST_Ruins_VO.KarakorumPower1_ScanPowerPlantAssembly_S: Scan failed. Unable to generate blueprints for alien technology. Scanner modification required.
ST_Ruins_VO.ClimateLab_Architect_Line2: The Axum language refers to the titan-class lifeform on the horizon as a "World Tree".
ST_Ruins_VO.ClimateLab_Message_Line3: The Trees’ roots feed the world, and the world feeds the Trees.
ST_Ruins_VO.ClimateLab_Message_Line2: The World Trees are dying. Their angels cry out to us.
ST_Ruins_VO.Observatory_Door: The door is unpowered. An Alterra generator would destroy the alien electrical system. Locate an alien generator to gain access.
ST_Ruins_VO.PDA_NeedlerSharkNeedle_Scan: The entire mineral mass of this tusk is bound by a layer of superstrong collagen. If this tiny layer of cells can hold it together, so can you.
ST_Ruins_VO.Rosetta_TwoLanguages: The glyphs on this monolith present the same message in two languages: the Architect symbols known from 4546b and an unknown writing system.
ST_Ruins_VO.Observatory2_OxygenatedWater: The observatory's water is enriched by oxygen bubblers. Please do not become accustomed to this convenience.
ST_Ruins_VO.PDA_AxumRebar_Scan: The self-healing fibers that grow through Axum rebar cannot be replicated by a fabricator. Logging a share claim for any derived innovations.
ST_Ruins_VO.Cave_Stone_Line1: The stone beneath this mesa has been eroded by hot, radioactive water. Probable source: titan rockbore.
ST_Ruins_VO.Wreck2B_Detected_Line2: The wreck may have been moved to block access to the viral source.
ST_Ruins_VO.ClimateLab_Architect_Line3: There is an alien hibernation crypt below the World Tree. Site designation: Xanadu. Site status: unknown.
ST_Ruins_VO.Cave_Stone_Line2: There may be an angel comb with useful adaptations nearby.
ST_Ruins_VO.Wreck2A_Blackbox_Tuba: There must be some spices somewhere in this — [spear impact, gasp and shout of pain]
ST_Ruins_VO.PDA_Celestine_PickUp: These celestine deposits form when sulfur-shelled protozoans die faster than their remains can dissolve into the seawater.
ST_Ruins_VO.KarakorumPower2_NeedAdaptation: These power plant control surfaces operate in a format outside human perception. Recommend adaptation.
ST_Ruins_VO.Observatory_Button_Fail: This button is not operational. Recommend diagnostic scans.
ST_Ruins_VO.Observatory2_ScanOrrery_Line1: This is a map of the local solar system and nearby stars, including a pulsar. Matching to transgov known pulsar database.
ST_Ruins_VO.PowerBlightCore_Blackbox_Anita_Line2: This is why. This angel comb is sick. It is calling out to us. And I choose to answer. I say to it – this is my body, given for you...
ST_Ruins_VO.Wreck2A_ResonatorModification: This resonator modification continues a dangerous trend of adapting alien technology to Alterra devices.
ST_Ruins_VO.Wreck_SuperScanner_Scan: This scanner modification employs cultured alien bacteria to spawn metallic markers. You will notice that happy bacteria smell like beets.
ST_Ruins_VO.Blight_BlightClosedCoreApproach: Tracking adult angel comb. Adaptation blocked by regional bloom infection.
ST_Ruins_VO.Blight_BlightClosedCoreApproach_FirstTim: Tracking angel comb infection status. One juvenile treated. Locate and treat the others.
ST_Ruins_VO.KarakorumPower2_TranslationFail: Translation is impossible without a basic dictionary or primer.
ST_Ruins_VO.ClimateLab_Message_Line13: We leave them to your mercy.

ST_Scanner.Crafted1: Stranded on 4546b, Ryley Robinson used a field scanner to outwit teleporting alien cyborgs and treat his kharaa infection.
