ST_SettingsAudio.DialogueVolumeDescription: Adjusts the volume of dialogue for game characters and voice overs.
ST_SettingsAudio.SoundEffectsVolumeDescription: Adjusts the volume of sound effects.
ST_SettingsAudio.AudioOutputDeviceDescription: Changes the audio output device for game audio.
ST_SettingsAudio.HeadphoneModeDescription: Enable binaural audio.  Provides 3D audio spatialization, so you can hear the location of sounds more precisely, including above, below, and behind you. Recommended for use with stereo headphones only.
ST_SettingsAudio.HDRAudioModeDescription: Enable high dynamic range audio. Changes the runtime processing chain to increase the dynamic range of the audio mixdown, appropriate for theater or more cinematic experiences.
ST_SettingsAudio.BackgroundAudioDescription: Turns game audio on/off when the game is in the background. When on, the game audio will continue to play when the game is minimized, or another window is focused.

ST_SettingsController.ControllerLayoutDescription: Change the controller layout between the classic Subnautica experience from the previous games, or try a new (and maybe improved) version!
ST_SettingsController.SwapControllerSticksDescription:
Determine which sticks get used for movement and camera orientation. 

Only affects gameplay controls, not UI navigation.
ST_SettingsController.ControllerOrientationDescription:
How do you hold your controller? 

Left = 90° Counter Clock Wise
Right = 90° Clock Wise
Upside Down = 180°

Only affects gameplay controls, not UI navigation.
ST_SettingsController.InvertVerticalControlsDescriptoin: Reverses the vertical look controls – when enabled, pushing forward looks down, and pulling back looks up
ST_SettingsController.ControllerSensitivityXDescription: Sets the sensitivity of the controller's horizontal (x) axis. With higher settings the camera will move faster when looking left and right with the controller.
ST_SettingsController.ControllerSensitivityYDescription: Sets the sensitivity of the controller's vertical (y) axis. With higher settings the camera will move faster when looking up and down with the controller.
ST_SettingsController.VibrationDescription: Sets the vibration intensity of the controller.
ST_SettingsController.PickupVibrationDescription: Should the controller vibrate when you pick up an item?
ST_SettingsController.SwapControllerSticks: Swap Left Stick and Right Stick

ST_SettingsGraphics.FrameGenerationDLSSModeDescription:
- "On" generates an extra frame in every situation
- "Auto" will only generate extra frames when it's considered beneficia
ST_SettingsGraphics.GraphicsQualityPresetsDescription_Consol:
- "Quality mode" will run the game at 30 FPS, with the best visual quality
- "Performance mode" will run the game at 60 FPS, compromising some visual quality
ST_SettingsGraphics.WindowModeDescription:
- In Windowed mode, you can interact with other windows more easily, and drag the edges of the window to set the size.
- In Windowed Fullscreen mode, you can easily switch between applications.
- In Fullscreen mode you cannot interact with other windows as easily, but the game will run slightly faster.
ST_SettingsGraphics.AutoSetQualityDescription: Automatically configure the graphics quality options based on a benchmark of the hardware.
ST_SettingsGraphics.CameraSwimBobbingDescription: Causes the camera to move up and down while swimming.
ST_SettingsGraphics.DeviceProfileSuffixDescription: Choose between different quality presets to make a trade off between quality and speed.
ST_SettingsGraphics.CloudsQualityDescription: Clouds determines the quality of the clouds in game. Increasing this setting can reduce performance.
ST_SettingsGraphics.ReflectionQualityDescription: Determinds the quality of the reflections in the game. Increasing this setting improves reflections visual fidelity, but can reduce performance.
ST_SettingsGraphics.ShadingQualityDescription: Determinds the quality of the shaders in the game. Increasing this setting improves visual fidelity, but can reduce performance.
ST_SettingsGraphics.FoliageQualityDescription: Determings the quality of the foliage in the game. Increasing this setting improves foliage visual fidelity, but can reduce performance.
ST_SettingsGraphics.ResolutionDescription: Display Resolution determines the size of the window in Windowed mode. In Fullscreen mode, Display Resolution determines the graphics card output resolution, which can result in black bars depending on monitor and graphics card. Display Resolution is inactive in Windowed Fullscreen mode.
ST_SettingsGraphics.DynamicResolutionDescription: Dynamic resolution helps maintaining consistent frame by trading resolution on heavy scenes.
ST_SettingsGraphics.VisualEffectQualityDescription: Effects determines the quality of visual effects in game. Increasing this setting will increase the quality of visual effects, but can reduce performance.
ST_SettingsGraphics.ChromaticAberrationDescription: Enables or disables chromatic aberration. Chromatic aberration simulates how colors get distorted in a physical camera in the real world
ST_SettingsGraphics.LensFlareDescription: Enables or disables lens flare. Enabling Lens Flare simulates an effect present on physical cameras when pointing at a bright light source
ST_SettingsGraphics.VerticalSyncDescription: Enabling Vertical Sync eliminates screen tearing by always rendering and presenting a full frame. Disabling Vertical Sync can give higher frame rate and better input response, but can result in horizontal screen tearing.
ST_SettingsGraphics.FieldOfViewDescription: Field of view adjusts how wide the camera angle in the game. A wider camera angle will introduce more distorsion and very high values might impact peformance
ST_SettingsGraphics.ColorBlindFilterDescription: Filter game color to help you differentiate elements of the game between each other.
ST_SettingsGraphics.FrameGenerationDescription: Frame generation increases the frame rate by predicting and interpolating the missing frames between two real frames.
ST_SettingsGraphics.FrameRateLimit_AlwaysDescription: Frame rate limit sets the highest frame rate that is allowed. Set this lower for a more consistent frame rate or higher for the best fidelity on faster machines. You may need to disable Vsync to reach high frame rates.
ST_SettingsGraphics.FrameRateLimit_WhenBackgroundedDescripti: Frame rate limit when in the background. Set this lower for a more consistent frame rate or higher for the best experience on faster machines. You may need to disable Vsync to reach high frame rates.
ST_SettingsGraphics.FrameRateLimit_InMenuDescription: Frame rate limit when in the menu. Set this lower for a more consistent frame rate or higher for the best fidelity on faster machines. You may need to disable Vsync to reach high frame rates.
ST_SettingsGraphics.FrameRateLimit_OnBatteryDescription: Frame rate limit when running on battery. Set this lower for a more consistent frame rate or higher for the best fidelity on faster machines. You may need to disable Vsync to reach high frame rates.
ST_SettingsGraphics.GlobalIlluminationQualityDescription: Global Illumination controls the quality of dynamically calculated indirect lighting bounces, sky shadowing and Ambient Occlusion. Settings of 'High' and above use more rays to solve lighting, but can reduce performance.
ST_SettingsGraphics.ResolutionScaleDescription: Internal resolution determines the resolution that objects are rendered in game, but does not affect the main menu.  Lower resolutions can significantly increase frame rate.
ST_SettingsGraphics.LandscapeQualityDescription: Landscape determines the quality of the landscape in game. Increasing this setting can reduce performance.
ST_SettingsGraphics.ResolutionScaleMaxDescription: Maximum internal resolution determines the max dynamic resolution that objects are rendered in game, but does not affect the main menu.  Lower resolutions can significantly increase frame rate.
ST_SettingsGraphics.ResolutionScaleMinDescription: Minimum internal resolution determines the min dynamic resolution that objects are rendered in game, but does not affect the main menu.  Lower resolutions can significantly increase frame rate.
ST_SettingsGraphics.MotionBlurDescription: Motion Blur adds a blur along the motion of your camera.
ST_SettingsGraphics.PostProcessingQualityDescription: Post Processing effects include Motion Blur, Depth of Field and Bloom. Increasing this setting improves the quality of post process effects, but can reduce performance.
ST_SettingsGraphics.GraphicsQualityPresetsDescription:
Quality Preset allows you to adjust multiple video options at once. Try a few options to see what fits your preference and device's performance.

Minimum Specs PC:
- Low: 1080p at 30 FPS

Recommended Specs PC:
- Medium: 1440p at 60 FPS
- Medium: 4K at 30 FPS
- High: 1440p at 30 FPS
ST_SettingsGraphics.RunHardwareBenchmarkDescription: Runs a hardware benchmark and sets the graphics options to a preset based on the result.
ST_SettingsGraphics.UpscalingTSRFramerateTargetDescription: Set the framerate you would like to achieve. TSR upscaling will adjust internal resolution between "Minimum Internal Resolution" and "Maximum Internal Resolution" so that the framerate is above this target. Only applies to the GPU framerate.
ST_SettingsGraphics.ShadowsDescription: Shadow quality determines the resolution and view distance of dynamic shadows. Shadows improve visual quality and give better depth perception, but can reduce performance.
ST_SettingsGraphics.TextureQualityDescription: Textures determines the resolution of textures in game. Increasing this setting will make objects more detailed, but can reduce performance.
ST_SettingsGraphics.FullscreenNeededForVSync: This feature only works if 'Window Mode' is set to 'Fullscreen'.
ST_SettingsGraphics.UpscalingMethodDescription:
Upscaling Method helps maintaining consistent framerate by trading resolution on heavy scenes.

- Fixed: Fixed resolution
- TSR: Temporal Super Resolution (Default upscaling method)
- DLSS: NVIDIA DLSS (Deep learning upscaling method)
ST_SettingsGraphics.UpscalingQualityModeDescription:
Upscaling quality determines the trade-off made between performance and quality

- Ultra Performance
- Performance
- Balanced
- Quality
ST_SettingsGraphics.DLSSQualityDescription:
Upscaling quality determines the trade-off made between performance and quality

- Ultra Performance
- Performance
- Balanced
- Quality
- DLAA
ST_SettingsGraphics.TSRQualityDescription:
Upscaling quality determines the trade-off made between performance and quality by setting min and max values for the internal resolution

- Performance: Min 50%, Max 50%
- Balanced: Min 50%, Max 66%
- Quality: Min 50%, Max 75%
- Native: Min 50%, Max 100%
- Custom: Allows specifying min and max values manually
ST_SettingsGraphics.ViewDistanceDescription: View distance determines how far away objects are culled for performance.
ST_SettingsGraphics.ResolutionWindowedFullscreen_Disabled: When the Window Mode is set to <strong>Windowed Fullscreen</>, the resolution must match the native desktop resolution.

ST_StartScreen.Warning2paragraph: A small percentage of people may experience seizures when exposed to certain lights, patterns or images, even those with no history of epilepsy or seizures. Player discretion is advised.
ST_StartScreen.TOSTextStart: By continuing, you agree to the Subnautica 2 Terms of Service:
ST_StartScreen.TOS_5: Full Terms of Service and Privacy Policy are available via Settings / Info.
ST_StartScreen.TOS_2: Game assets and content are protected and may not be modified or redistributed beyond permitted use.
ST_StartScreen.TOS_4: Online interactions must follow respectful conduct.
ST_StartScreen.EATextPCXBOX_2:
Pre-Release Software.
This game is in a pre-release stage of development. This means that some parts of the game, including Xbox Live features (like chat and multiplayer), might not function as expected (or might not function at all). The game might even crash.
For support, please visit https://support.krafton.com
ST_StartScreen.EAText: Subnautica 2 is actively being developed while in Early Access.
ST_StartScreen.EATextPCXBOX: Subnautica 2 is actively being developed while in Game Preview.
ST_StartScreen.TOS_advanced:
Temporary for testing:

Warning about underwater phobia alone:
 
WARNING: This game contains immersive underwater environments that may trigger thalassophobia (a fear of deep water). Player discretion is advised.
 
Combined with photosensitive epilepsy warning:
 
WARNING: This game contains flashing lights and visual effects that may affect a small percentage of individuals with photosensitive epilepsy, even those with no history of epilepsy or seizures. It also features immersive underwater environments that may trigger thalassophobia (fear of deep water). Player discretion is advised.
 


For the seizure warning, for Redacted we recommended the following language:
 
SEIZURE WARNING: Redacted may contain flashing lights and images that may induce epileptic seizures, even for those with no history of seizures or epilepsy. If you or anyone in your household has an epileptic condition, or if you begin to experience symptoms of a seizure (e.g., dizziness, altered vision, eye or muscle twitches, motion sickness, etc.), immediately stop playing and consult a doctor before proceeding. You can reduce the likelihood of a seizure by avoiding large screens, taking breaks, playing in a well-lit room, and not playing when you feel drowsy or fatigued.
 
I recognize that SN previously used a shorter warning. We reviewed that as well, and have previously proposed the following slightly modified short version:
 
SEIZURE WARNING: A small percentage of people may experience seizures when exposed to certain lights, patterns or images, even those with no history of epilepsy or seizures. Player discretion is advised.
 
As noted before, there are no hard rules on where to place this warning. It could be done on the platform in an obvious manner, or on the same splash screen as the underwater warning.  Let us know if you’d like proposed language for the underwater aspect.
ST_StartScreen.Warning1paragraph: This game contains flashing lights and visual effects that may affect a small percentage of individuals with photosensitive epilepsy, even those with no history of epilepsy or seizures. It also features immersive underwater environments that may trigger thalassophobia (fear of deep water). Player discretion is advised.
ST_StartScreen.TOS_1: You are granted a personal, non-commercial license to play.
ST_StartScreen.EAText_2: You may encounter bugs and issues while playing. We look forward to your ideas and feedback as we collaborate with you and our community on all our future updates.
ST_StartScreen.TOS_3: Your gameplay data may be collected to improve the experience.

ST_Titanium.TitaniumDescription: Ti. Element 22. Ideal for underwater construction.
ST_Titanium.TitaniumDatabankEntry:
Titanium (Ti), element 22.

A light, strong, non-conductive, non-reactive metal. Perfect for underwater construction—won’t corrode.

Binds with oxygen to form minerals like rutile and anatase, which are likely embedded in bedrock on the sea floor.

Osseointegral metal—can be fused directly and safely to bone. Organisms on the bombardment world Thokcha and more recently encountered variants of the kharaa "onos" xenorhinoid both incorporate titanium into their biology.
ST_Titanium.Dialogue_PDA_Pickup_2: Your body contains several grams of titanium implants, knit directly to your bone.

ST_WorldTreeRoot.Databank_TitanRockbore:
This enormous colony organism (Lithodont titanicae) resembles a spinal cord protected by a thick crablike shell. It extends beyond scanner range in both directions, growing through fractures in the seabed rock.

1. Hollow interior:
The interior of the bore contains at least two large flooded channels—one strikingly radioactive. The ancestral form may be a pyrosome (a sea squirt).

2. Poor health:
Most of the zooids in the bore are dead, leaving only a mineral skeleton and a hydrocarbon-polymer shell. The interior channels have clogged.

3. Unknown metabolism:
The bore has no apparent food supply. It may have derived chemical energy from minerals, but if so, its bacterial symbiotes have fled. It is starving.

4. Viral activity:
The surviving tissue is infected with large RNA virus similar to Earthly giant sea viruses. The bore's anatomy includes receptor sites that encourage viral infection and reproduction. Further analysis required.

Assessment: Entirely unknown organism - investigate further, determine origin and ecological niche.
